name: 1517 Fund
award: Flux Capacitor program: $50K to $1M for moonshot scientists and dropouts
notes: 1517 Fund is a San Francisco firm founded in 2015 by Thiel Fellowship cofounders. The fund backs college dropouts and renegade scientists at the earliest stages, writing first checks from $50K to $1M.
thesis: Prioritizes uncredentialed founders and frontier scientists across software, hardware, and deep tech, betting on people over polished decks.

notes: 2150 is a European urban-tech venture firm with offices in London, Copenhagen, and Berlin, part of the Urban Partners platform. It backs companies reshaping how cities are designed, built, and powered.
thesis: Urban Stack thesis: invests in building, mobility, and materials technology that compounds both financial returns and SDG outcomes. Searches for 'Gigacorns' that benefit billions while cutting emissions.
recent_activity: Continues to deploy across the urban technology stack from three European offices.

name: 3M Ventures
notes: 3M Ventures is the corporate venture capital arm of 3M, founded in 2008 and based in St. Paul, MN. It backs early and growth-stage companies with strong IP and traction across automotive electrification, climate tech, semiconductors, sustainable packaging, and digital health.
thesis: Minority growth financing for companies with strong IP positions, demonstrated commercial traction, and strategic alignment to 3M business units.
recent_activity: Has made 72 investments to date. Recently led rounds in Swiss climate-tech Voltiris and Dutch advanced manufacturing firm Morphotonics.

name: 5AM Ventures
notes: 5AM Ventures is a Menlo Park life sciences firm established in 2002. Currently managing $450M from Fund VII and $300M from Opportunities II across private therapeutics and public/late-stage biotech.
thesis: Early-stage therapeutics with a focus on Series A. The 4:59 Initiative incubates breakthrough science with academics ahead of formal Series A financing.
recent_activity: Arvinas secured FDA approval for VEPPANU (vepdegestrant). Artiva priced a $300M underwritten offering and reported positive AlloNK clinical data. Entrada posted positive Phase 1/2 results for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy.

name: 8090 Industries
award: Series A and growth checks from a $100M+ fund
notes: 8090 Industries is a New York-based VC founded in 2022 by Kerem Ozmen and Rayyan Islam. The firm has partnered with 20-plus global industrial conglomerates and raised $100M for its growth-focused industrial fund in March 2026.
thesis: Backs breakthrough industrial technology in decarbonization, aerospace and defense, synthetic biology, and energy with Series A and growth checks.
recent_activity: Closed $100M in additional capital in March 2026 and participated in Fleetzero's Series A for marine electrification.

name: 8VC
notes: 8VC is an Austin-based investment firm founded by Joe Lonsdale, the Palantir co-founder. The firm backs platforms across life sciences, healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, enterprise, and defense.
thesis: Partners with founders building durable technology platforms with both economic and societal value. Industrial, defense, and healthcare are core verticals.
recent_activity: Announced Kos.ai (AI accountant) in April 2026 and introduced PointOne for legal AI in March 2026.

name: A*STAR Singapore
notes: Singapore's national research agency. Operates two research councils (Biomedical Research; Science and Engineering) covering medtech, genomics, infectious disease, cancer diagnostics, semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, AI, materials, and sustainability. Translates research through Tech for Enterprise and ETPL commercialization programs.
thesis: Multiple non-dilutive grant schemes for Singapore-anchored R&D in biomedical, deep tech, and advanced manufacturing.
recent_activity: Lucence, DxD Hub, and NCCS launched a S$6M cancer diagnostics collaboration. New open-source robotics initiatives launched. Singapore 100 Women in Tech 2025 announced.

name: ABB Ventures
notes: Now operating as ABB Ventures. Founded in 2009 and headquartered in Zurich with offices in Silicon Valley and Washington DC. Roughly $400M AUM with check sizes from $500K to $10M. Backs robotics, industrial IoT, AI, energy transition, cyber, electric mobility, smart buildings, and distributed energy.
thesis: Backs companies aligned with ABB's robotics, electrification, and automation portfolio.
recent_activity: Most recent investment in Enline (business productivity software), March 19, 2026.

name: AE Industrial Partners
award: Growth equity and PE for aerospace, defense, and industrial
notes: AE Industrial Partners is a Boca Raton private investment firm focused on aerospace, defense, and national security. The firm manages $9.2B across 155+ closed investments with 110 professionals in Boca Raton and Washington DC.
thesis: Invests in technologies and services considered critical to national and economic security across aerospace, defense, industrial services, and related venture-stage companies.
recent_activity: Acquiring controlling interest in L3Harris Space Propulsion (Rocketdyne) at $845M enterprise value in 2026.

name: AEI HorizonX (formerly Boeing HorizonX)
notes: Boeing's former HorizonX corporate venture unit was spun into a joint platform with AE Industrial Partners and now operates as AE Ventures (AEI HorizonX). Backs aerospace, defense, national security, and industrial companies. Fund II targeted $250M with $50M anchored by Boeing.
thesis: Backs early-stage companies addressing aerospace, national security, and industrial market problems with government and OEM customer access built in.

team_sample.name: Brian Schettler
team_sample.title: Head of Ventures, formerly Founder of Boeing HorizonX

recent_activity: Recent deals include Last Energy (modular nuclear, Nov 2025), Code Metal (Oct 2025), and Blank Slate Technologies (Dec 2025).
correction_note: Boeing's HorizonX program was acquired and now operates as AEI HorizonX under AE Industrial Partners.

name: AENU
notes: AENU is a Berlin-based climate-only impact fund and certified B Corp with an evergreen structure. It invests across Europe with seed tickets of EUR 1M-5M and follow-on up to EUR 10M.
thesis: Backs disruptive hardware, software, and AI-native solutions across energy, industrial decarbonization, and systemic resilience. Prefers lead or co-lead with board seat.
recent_activity: Active across DACH, France, UK, and Nordics. Maintains a proprietary Systemic Impact Framework for portfolio operations.

name: AFWERX
award: Phase I $75K-$1.7M; Phase II up to $1.9M; STRATFI up to $15M govt + $30M private = $60M total; TACFI up to $3.75M govt match
deadline: 26.BZ & 26.TZ Specific Topic Release 1 open now; Release 2 pre-release. PY26.2 TACFI coming soon.
notes: The innovation arm of the Department of the Air Force. Runs the SBIR/STTR Open Topic and STRATFI/TACFI tracks designed to fund dual-use companies on commercial timelines. Spark and SpaceWERX subprograms handle base-level adoption and space-specific bets.
thesis: Non-dilutive Phase I, II, and III SBIR contracts (from $75K up to $50M+) for commercial dual-use technology relevant to Air Force or Space Force missions.
recent_activity: PY26.2 TACFI solicitation is upcoming. Release 2 of the 26.BZ and 26.TZ Specific Topic competitions are in pre-release.

past_awardees.name: Castelion
past_awardees.description: Builds low-cost long-range strike missiles for the US military.

past_awardees.name: Evolution Space
past_awardees.description: Manufactures solid rocket motors used as hypersonic test targets and boosters.

past_awardees.name: Rise8
past_awardees.description: Builds software tools that help the US Air Force ship secure cloud applications faster.

past_awardees.name: Highlight Technologies
past_awardees.description: Develops secure mobile access software for handling controlled government data in the field.

past_awardees.name: Hermeus
past_awardees.description: Develops hypersonic aircraft for defense reconnaissance and commercial passenger travel.

past_awardees.name: Firestorm Labs
past_awardees.description: Builds 3D-printed expendable drones that can be manufactured close to where they are used.

name: AME Cloud Ventures
notes: AME Cloud Ventures is Jerry Yang's Palo Alto firm, funded by his personal capital rather than external LPs. The fund has backed roughly 252 companies including 17 unicorns and concentrates on data infrastructure, deep tech, and life sciences.
thesis: Backs seed and early-stage companies with deep science or technology defensibility, focused on data-driven businesses while avoiding consumer internet.
recent_activity: Latest disclosed investment was Cartography Biosciences Series B in October 2025.

name: ARCH Venture Partners
notes: A Chicago and Seattle venture firm specializing in deep-science biotech and healthcare. Co-founded by Bob Nelsen, one of the original biotech venture investors. Builds companies from groundbreaking academic research across therapeutics, diagnostics, and platform biology.
thesis: Seed through Series B+ ($5M-$50M+) commitments to company creation in life sciences and disease prevention, detection, and cure.
recent_activity: Continues to anchor flagship biotech rounds from offices in Chicago and Seattle.

name: ARENA (Australian Renewable Energy Agency)
notes: Australian government agency funding pre-commercial renewable energy innovation. Covers solar, wind, energy storage, hydrogen, EVs, geothermal, ocean energy, bioenergy, industrial decarbonization, and grid stability. Supports research through field demonstration.
thesis: Funds projects accelerating Australia's transition to renewable energy and net-zero emissions.
recent_activity: Recent grants include AUD 25.3M to SunDrive for copper solar cells (November 2025), AUD 21M for EV transition (December 2025), AUD 13M for industrial decarbonization (March 2026), and AUD 5M for critical mineral processing (February 2026).

name: ARIA (UK Advanced Research and Invention Agency)
award: Opportunity Seeds up to £500K; programme awards £100K-£5M+
deadline: 3 open: Enduring Atmospheric Platforms (May 22, 2026); Safeguarded AI Cybersecurity (July 1, 2026); Opportunity Seeds (rolling)
notes: The UK's ARPA-style research agency, funding high-risk scientific and technological breakthroughs. Programme Directors are given autonomy and term-limited authority to run multi-million-pound R&D programmes. Active programmes include Safeguarded AI, Enduring Atmospheric Platforms, Rolling Opportunity Seeds, and Hypersensory Intelligence.
thesis: £1M-£50M+ non-dilutive funding for high-risk, high-reward research aligned to programme theses set by Programme Directors.
recent_activity: Safeguarded AI Cybersecurity proposals due July 1, 2026. Enduring Atmospheric Platforms proposals due May 22, 2026. Third Programme Director cohort applications open August 2026.

past_awardees.name: Topos Institute (UK)
past_awardees.description: Research institute applying category theory and applied mathematics to AI safety and complex systems.

past_awardees.name: GLAIVE Research Institute
past_awardees.description: Glasgow nonprofit lab building formally verifiable software techniques to make AI systems safer.

past_awardees.name: Lumai
past_awardees.description: Oxford spinout building optical computing hardware that runs AI inference at much lower energy than chips.

past_awardees.name: Salience Labs
past_awardees.description: Builds photonic AI accelerators that use light instead of electricity to run neural networks.

past_awardees.name: Phlux Technology
past_awardees.description: Builds infrared photodetectors used in lidar, communications, and chemical sensing.

past_awardees.name: Faculty AI (Safeguarded AI)
past_awardees.description: Builds applied AI products for UK government and enterprise, including the Safeguarded AI workflow under ARIA.

past_awardees.name: COMSATS University Pakistan (climate cooling research)
past_awardees.description: University team running evidence-based assessment of marine cloud brightening in South Asia under ARIA's climate cooling programme.

name: ARPA-E SCALEUP
award: Up to $20M+ (SCALEUP cohort); SCALEUP Ready bridges to scale
deadline: SCALEUP Ready (DE-FOA-0003467) rolling; flagship cohort solicitation TBD
notes: DOE's Advanced Research Projects Agency for Energy runs SCALEUP for prior ARPA-E awardees ready to move from prototype to commercial scale. Awards up to $20M over three years with cost-share required from the recipient. Designed to bridge the valley between proven R&D and first-of-a-kind deployment.
thesis: Non-dilutive scale-up grants for ARPA-E alumni with technology ready for first commercial deployment.
recent_activity: Continues to run SCALEUP solicitations on a rolling basis aligned with ARPA-E program priorities.

past_awardees.name: Antora Energy
past_awardees.description: Stores renewable energy as heat in carbon blocks and delivers it as electricity and process heat to industrial customers.

past_awardees.name: AeroShield Materials
past_awardees.description: Manufactures transparent aerogel inserts that make windows roughly twice as insulating as standard double-pane glass.

past_awardees.name: Ion Storage Systems
past_awardees.description: Builds solid-state lithium-metal batteries using a ceramic separator for higher energy density and safer operation.

past_awardees.name: Queens Carbon
past_awardees.description: Operates a low-temperature pilot plant that produces low-carbon cement using standard raw materials.

past_awardees.name: Sila Nanotechnologies
past_awardees.description: Manufactures silicon-based anode materials that boost the energy density of lithium-ion batteries.

past_awardees.name: Form Energy
past_awardees.description: Builds 100-hour iron-air batteries for multi-day grid storage.

name: ARPA-H
notes: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health, established in 2022 inside HHS and modeled on DARPA. Funds high-risk biomedical and health programs across Health Science Futures, Scalable Solutions, Proactive Health, and Resilient Systems.
thesis: Funds high-risk, high-reward biomedical and health research that traditional NIH and industry funders will not back.
recent_activity: Announced the HEARING brain-driven hearing restoration program and the $139M EVIDENT behavioral health initiative in April-May 2026. THEA and IGoR programs launched in May 2026.

past_awardees.name: Tessera Therapeutics
past_awardees.description: Develops gene-writing therapies that engineer immune cells inside the patient to treat autoimmune disease and cancer.

past_awardees.name: Cytiva (Global Life Science Solutions)
past_awardees.description: Develops a lipid nanoparticle platform for editing genes inside the body to treat gastrointestinal cancers.

past_awardees.name: Kernal Biologics
past_awardees.description: Builds an mRNA delivery platform that targets immune cells to treat multiple sclerosis and blood cancers.

past_awardees.name: STRM.BIO
past_awardees.description: Develops a delivery platform that carries therapeutic cargo to immune cells for autoimmune disease.

past_awardees.name: Peptilogics
past_awardees.description: Uses computational design to develop new antibiotic and therapeutic peptides.

past_awardees.name: Inductive Bio
past_awardees.description: Builds AI tools that predict drug safety problems before clinical trials.

past_awardees.name: Homeward Health
past_awardees.description: Delivers in-home medical care to rural patients using a mobile, procedure-driven model.

past_awardees.name: 10XBeta
past_awardees.description: Designs modular medical hardware platforms for interchangeable clinical use cases.

name: Acre Venture Partners
notes: Food and agriculture-focused venture firm based in Santa Monica with anchor capital from Campbell Soup. Backs companies improving food systems, sustainability, and agricultural supply chains from seed through Series B.
thesis: Backs companies modernizing food, agriculture, and supply chains across health, sustainability, and technology.
recent_activity: Continues to invest from Acre Fund III across protein, agtech, and supply-chain transparency companies through 2025-2026.

name: Activate
award: Two-year fellowship with stipend, R&D funding, and embedded national lab access
notes: Hard-tech fellowship for science entrepreneurs, originally Cyclotron Road at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab. Active cohorts in Berkeley, Boston, New York, Houston, and a national virtual program plus a Louisiana track. Public Apply Now intake during open cycles.

name: Activate Fellowship
hq: Berkeley, CA / Boston, MA / NYC / Houston
notes: Two-year fellowship for entrepreneurial scientists turning hard tech research into companies. Originated as Cyclotron Road at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab and now runs across multiple US sites plus Singapore. Fellows receive a stipend, lab access, and a senior mentor network.
thesis: Backs science-founded hardtech founders building physical-world products with deep IP.
recent_activity: Applications for the 2026 cohort opened with target growth toward 100 fellows annually across eight US locations. The 2025 impact report cites 294 fellows and $5.5B in follow-on funding.

name: Aerospace Technology Institute (ATI Programme)
notes: Independent UK body that sets aerospace technology strategy and manages the ATI Programme of matched R&D grants. To date has facilitated GBP 4.2B in R&D investment across 467 projects and 478 organizations, including 323 SMEs.
thesis: Funds UK aerospace R&D with a focus on net-zero aviation, hydrogen, and supply chain capability.
recent_activity: UK Government committed up to GBP 2.3B through the ATI from 2025 to 2035. May 2026 technology strategy launch under the Engineering Growth blueprint.

name: AgFunder
notes: AgFunder is a Silicon Valley agrifood deep tech VC and media platform. The firm runs multiple thematic funds and a research arm that publishes the widely cited Global AgriFoodTech Investment Report.
thesis: Backs AI, machine learning, robotics, and biotechnology companies that remove structural constraints in food, agriculture, human health, and planetary sustainability.
recent_activity: Released the AgFunder Global AgriFoodTech Investment Report 2025 showing $16B in global agrifoodtech funding, off only 4% from 2023.

name: AiiM Partners
notes: San Francisco climate venture firm focused on technology that makes industries more resource-efficient. Invests in digital infrastructure, energy, and supply chains at growth stage with a focus on commercialization-stage decarbonization.
thesis: Backs differentiated climate technologies in digital infrastructure, energy, and supply chains gaining faster market traction than peers.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Lyten acquired Northvolt assets in 2025 and Wildtype received FDA approval for cell-cultivated salmon.

name: Air Street Capital
award: Specialized AI-first fund investing at the earliest stages
notes: Backs AI-first companies including AI applied to drug discovery, materials, robotics, and scientific research. Publishes the annual State of AI Report. Outreach via the website contact channels.

name: Alaska Star Ventures
notes: Alaska Star Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Alaska Airlines, launched in 2021 to support the company's 2040 net zero target. The fund has deployed roughly $90 million across sustainable aviation, electric aircraft, and operational efficiency startups.
thesis: Backs technologies that decarbonize aviation or improve operational performance, with capital deployed alongside Alaska Airlines' own pilot programs and supply agreements.
recent_activity: Twelve's first batch of sustainable aviation fuel was expected during 2026, and Alaska Star Ventures continued to back hydrogen, electric, and AI-enabled operations companies.

name: AlchemistX
notes: Six-month enterprise-focused accelerator based in Silicon Valley. Backs technical founders building B2B companies in industrial, supply chain, robotics, and adjacent deep tech. Takes roughly 5% equity and offers about $30K net after tuition offset.
thesis: Backs technical founders moving from builder to CEO of an enterprise-sales-driven company.
recent_activity: Next flagship cohort begins January 22, 2026. OneBrief (Class 23) closed a $200M Series D at $2.15B and MoEngage raised $100M led by Goldman Sachs Alternatives.

name: AlleyCorp DeepTech
hq: New York, NY and Boston, MA
notes: Deep tech arm of Kevin Ryan's AlleyCorp studio operating across New York and Boston. Writes $500K to $2M checks at pre-seed and Series A in robotics, automation, energy, manufacturing, and defense.
thesis: Backs robotics, automation, and industrial AI founders building physical-world products through a studio plus capital model.
recent_activity: Ran the inaugural Deep Tech New York summit in January 2026, convening hardware founders, operators, and investors.

name: Almi Invest
notes: Almi Invest is Sweden's most active early-stage investor, established in 2009 with SEK 3.5B in total equity. The firm operates eight regional venture funds plus a national GreenTech fund and has backed nearly 600 startups.
thesis: Bridges the gap between angels and private VC across Tech, DeepTech, Life Science, Industry, and GreenTech. The GreenTech fund targets companies that can cut hundreds of thousands of tons of CO2e per year within 5 to 15 years.
recent_activity: Made 35 investments in 2025 and 6 more by February 2026. Portfolio company Billhop was acquired by Ramp in March 2026.

name: AlphaLab Gear
notes: AlphaLab Gear is a hardware-focused accelerator run by Innovation Works in Pittsburgh, founded in 2013 as one of the first hardware accelerators in the world. The program supports physical-product founders with fabrication space, supply chain expertise, and go-to-market mentorship.
thesis: Backs hardware founders refining products, strengthening supply chains, and reaching market through a structured cohort program plus follow-on Innovation Works capital.
recent_activity: AlphaLab Gear opened applications for its 2027 cohort and continued running virtual office hours for prospective hardware founders.

name: AlphaLab Health
award: Up to $100K investment, BSL-1 labs, clinical mentorship
notes: Joint accelerator program from Innovation Works and Allegheny Health Network in Pittsburgh. Funds therapeutics, diagnostics, medical devices, and digital health startups with up to $100K in investment, BSL-1 lab access, and clinical mentorship.
thesis: Lowers the entry barrier for life sciences founders through capital, clinical access, and lab infrastructure with AHN.

team_sample.name: Jeff Cohen
team_sample.title: Chief Physician Executive of Community Health and Innovation at AHN

team_sample.name: Matthew Bauer
team_sample.title: Director of Innovation and Incubator Operations at AHN

recent_activity: 2026 cohort included 20 companies, the largest since the program launched in 2020 and the largest Innovation Works cohort since 2008.

name: Amazon Climate Pledge Fund
notes: Amazon's $2B venture program backing companies that help Amazon hit its 2040 net-zero commitment. Invests globally across transportation and logistics, energy generation and storage, manufacturing and materials, circular economy, food and agriculture, and carbon removal. Committed at least $50M to female-founded climate startups.
thesis: Growth-stage checks ($10M-$100M+) into sustainable hardware and infrastructure that can integrate into Amazon's operations.
recent_activity: Backed Twelve's $83M AirPlant One expansion alongside Mitsui and Greycroft in 2025.

name: America's Frontier Fund (AFF)
notes: Nonprofit deep-tech fund founded in 2021, based in Arlington, VA. Backs foundational technologies critical to US national security across microelectronics, infrastructure, networks, compute, biology, energy, materials, AI, and advanced manufacturing.
thesis: Backs strategic deep tech companies that strengthen US technology leadership in dual-use sectors.
recent_activity: Most recent investment in Antheia (biotech) on January 27, 2026.

name: Andreessen Horowitz American Dynamism
notes: American Dynamism is the a16z practice investing in companies that support the US national interest, including aerospace, defense, public safety, manufacturing, energy, and housing. Fund 2 closed near $1.2B and the team writes checks from seed through growth.
thesis: Backs founders treating government as a customer or core stakeholder across aerospace, defense, energy, manufacturing, supply chain, and public safety.
recent_activity: Hosted the 2026 American Dynamism Summit in Washington DC and launched the 'Fight of the Future' AD50 list. Recent investments include Ulysses, Chariot Defense, and Heron Power.

name: Anzu Partners
notes: A $1B industrial and hardtech investor with offices in Washington DC, Boston, and Tampa. 50+ in-house operators, scientists, and engineers work alongside founders on business development, market positioning, and global growth. Invests through venture capital, private credit, and public market vehicles.
thesis: $2M-$10M checks into industrial technology, clean technology, and life sciences companies with strong technical moats.
recent_activity: GelSight expanded its Modulus surface roughness platform in May 2026. XGS Energy named Richard Chong as CFO. InterVenn added Alec Ford and Mitch Levine to its board.

name: Aramco Ventures
notes: Aramco Ventures is the $7.5B global corporate venture program of Saudi Aramco. The platform spans sustainability, prosperity, and digital industrial sub-funds and has backed 89 companies over 13 years.
thesis: Invests across high tech, enterprise applications, energy tech, environmental tech, and sustainability tech, with the Sustainability Fund directly supporting Aramco's 2050 net-zero ambition.
recent_activity: Aramco committed an additional $4B to the global venture capital program in 2024, taking the platform to $7.5B in commitments.

name: ArcelorMittal XCarb Innovation Fund
notes: The XCarb Innovation Fund is ArcelorMittal's decarbonization-focused venture fund, committing up to $100M per year. As of recent updates the fund has deployed roughly $188.5M across seven companies developing technologies for carbon-neutral steelmaking.
thesis: Backs companies developing technologies directly applicable and commercially scalable to decarbonizing steel and heavy industry, including molten oxide electrolysis, clean hydrogen, and industrial heat electrification.
recent_activity: Boston Metal expects to bring molten oxide electrolysis to commercial steel markets by 2026. TerraPower remains the fund's largest single bet at $50M.

name: Asahi Kasei Corporate Venture Capital
notes: Corporate venture arm of Asahi Kasei, the Japanese diversified chemicals, materials, healthcare, and electronics conglomerate. Invests in advanced materials, batteries, hydrogen, healthcare, and digital health.
thesis: Backs companies whose technology aligns with Asahi Kasei's materials, mobility, and health businesses.
recent_activity: Continued to expand US and Israel investment pace in 2025-2026 with focus on battery materials and hydrogen electrolyzer suppliers.

name: Astanor Ventures
notes: Astanor is a Brussels-based agrifood tech impact investor with €800M raised across more than 50 investments. The team has 20+ professionals across five offices in Europe and the US and tracks impact across six KPIs from CO2 avoided to farmers financed.
thesis: Backs agrifood founders building a resilient, sustainable food system from soil and sea to gut, across venture and growth stages.
recent_activity: Portfolio companies cumulatively avoided 213,000 metric tons of CO2 equivalent and financed 16,000 farmers annually as of the latest impact report.

name: Aster Capital
notes: Aster is a Paris-based deep tech, industry, and climate VC with 25 years of experience. The firm is backed by Schneider Electric, Solvay, Alstom, and Air Liquide and writes EUR 500K to EUR 5M checks from seed to Series B.
thesis: Invests in European startups industrializing breakthrough technology across energy, logistics, mobility, manufacturing, construction, chemicals, and agriculture.
recent_activity: Exited US portfolio company Swiftly in June 2025 and published industry research on French corporate venture activity.

name: Astera Institute
notes: Berkeley-based 501(c)(3) founded by Jed McCaleb. Operates three programs: Neuro and AGI, Radial (life sciences research), and Foundation (residencies and fellows). Supports public-benefit science and engineering work that lacks a natural home elsewhere.
thesis: Funds long-shot science and engineering programs aimed at high-leverage technology breakthroughs.
recent_activity: Committed $600M+ over the next decade to neuroscience research. Continues to operate Focused Research Organization grants in AGI, science, and climate.

name: Astia Angels
notes: Investment vehicle backing high-performing women-led companies across three pillars: Health, Climate, and Prosperity. Public Apply intake. Climate and Health pillars include hardtech and deep-tech companies.

name: At One Ventures
notes: At One Ventures is a San Francisco climate and deep-tech fund founded by Tom Chi, a Google X founding member. The firm manages $525M across two funds and backs companies upending unit economics in established industries while reducing their planetary footprint.
thesis: Nature-positive deep tech: disruptive technology that lowers cost and lowers environmental impact at the same time. About 25 percent of the portfolio is nature tech.
recent_activity: Launched a $375M climate tech fund (second fund), bringing AUM to $525M.

name: Atlas Venture
notes: Atlas Venture is a biotech-focused early-stage firm in Cambridge, Massachusetts. The firm builds new biotechnology companies in-house and leads first institutional rounds, with a portfolio spanning rare disease, oncology, and platform biology.
thesis: Concentrates on biotech company creation and Series A leads, founding many portfolio companies from concept inside the firm.

name: Atomico
notes: European multi-stage venture firm founded in London by Skype co-founder Niklas Zennstrom. Invests Series A through growth across climate, deep tech, frontier science, and consumer. Author of the annual State of European Tech report.
thesis: Backs European founders building globally relevant deep tech, climate, and frontier science companies.
recent_activity: Published the State of European Tech 2025 report and continues to invest from the firm's $1.1B Atomico V fund across climate and deeptech rounds.

name: Autodesk Foundation
notes: Philanthropic and impact investment arm of Autodesk. Provides grants, impact investments, software access, and employee expertise to for-profit and nonprofit organizations working on energy and materials, health and resilience, and work and prosperity.
thesis: Funds organizations applying design and making to decarbonization, climate adaptation, and quality job creation.
recent_activity: 2025 portfolio reached 29.9 million people with resilient solutions, abated 2.8 million metric tons of CO2e, and supported 438,000 quality jobs.

name: Autotech Ventures
notes: Mobility venture firm investing in ground transportation. Portfolio covers connected vehicle, autonomous, electrification, shared mobility, and aftermarket.

name: BARDA DRIVe
award: POC: $50K-$200K per 4-12 wk milestone; full BAA: $1M-$50M
deadline: ImmuneChip+ BAA open through 9/28/2028; POC programs (VANGUARD, Paratus, SPARK, VITAL) rolling
notes: The Division of Research, Innovation, and Ventures inside HHS BARDA. Funds early-stage companies addressing health security threats, including pandemic preparedness, sepsis, and biosecurity. Operates as an in-government accelerator with OTA contracting authority and a network of partner accelerators.
thesis: Non-dilutive R&D and proof-of-concept funding ($0.5M-$50M+) for medical countermeasures and health security technology.
recent_activity: Continues to run partnering opportunities and accelerator network programs targeting medical countermeasures.

past_awardees.name: Vaxart
past_awardees.description: Develops oral pill vaccines for COVID-19 and norovirus that do not require cold chain shipping.

past_awardees.name: Codagenix
past_awardees.description: Designs intranasal viral vaccines using a software platform that re-codes virus genomes.

past_awardees.name: Inhalon Biopharma
past_awardees.description: Develops inhaled antibody therapies that trap respiratory viruses in mucus before they infect cells.

past_awardees.name: Visby Medical
past_awardees.description: Builds a credit-card-sized molecular diagnostic device that delivers PCR test results in 30 minutes.

past_awardees.name: Geneoscopy
past_awardees.description: Develops noninvasive stool tests that detect colorectal cancer and inflammatory bowel disease.

past_awardees.name: Empatica
past_awardees.description: Builds medical wearables that detect physiological signs of infection days before symptoms appear.

name: BASF Venture Capital
notes: BASF Venture Capital is the corporate venture arm of BASF, established in 2001 and operating an EUR 250 million evergreen fund. The team invests EUR 1 million to EUR 5 million at seed through Series B from offices across Germany, Canada, the US, China, and Brazil.
thesis: Backs startups in CO2 reduction, circular economy, new materials, biotechnology, and software relevant to BASF's chemistry platform, typically after technical and market proof of concept.
recent_activity: Portfolio company pH7 Technologies raised a $25.6 million Series B in December 2025, and Heike Pfistner assumed the Managing Director role in March 2026.

name: BIRAC
notes: The Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council is an Indian government agency under the Department of Biotechnology. BIRAC has supported more than 5,000 startups with INR 4,200 crore in direct funding and facilitated INR 7,000 crore in total investment.
recent_activity: BIRAC announced the 3rd E-YUVA Fellows call in May 2026, launched the joint DBT-BIRAC Bio-AI Hubs call in November 2025, and opened the GCI Breakthrough Solutions proposal window.

name: BIRD Energy
notes: Joint US-Israel energy research and development fund administered by the Israel-US Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation. Provides cost-shared grants for binational energy projects involving paired US and Israeli companies.
thesis: Funds joint US-Israel commercialization of clean energy technologies through cost-shared grants.
recent_activity: Continued to announce annual cohorts of jointly funded US-Israel energy projects across long-duration storage, hydrogen, and solar through 2025-2026.

name: BIRD Foundation
award: Up to $1.5M per project (conditional, repaid via royalties); ~50% of project budget
deadline: Manual verification : site timed out. BIRD historically runs 2 cycles/yr (March + September submission cutoffs)
notes: The Israel-US Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation, established by the US and Israeli governments in 1977. Funds up to 50% of joint US-Israel R&D projects to a cap of $1.5M as a conditional grant. Runs BIRD Energy, BIRD Cyber, and BIRD HLS subprograms.
thesis: Conditional non-dilutive grants for any sector, requiring a joint US-Israel commercial venture between two companies.
recent_activity: 2026 Call for Proposals had executive summary submissions due May 14, 2026 and full proposals due July 9, 2026. A second US-Israel Energy Center call closed in February 2026 with two $6M consortia selected.

name: BMW i Ventures
notes: Corporate venture arm of BMW Group with offices in Mountain View and Munich. Operating since 2011 with 90+ portfolio companies and $1.1B+ AUM. Focus areas span batteries, EV charging, semiconductors, 3D printing, custom manufacturing, and dealer tech.
thesis: Backs companies reimagining automotive, manufacturing, and adjacent industrial categories with BMW as a strategic partner.
recent_activity: Closed $300M Fund III in April 2026 with an AI-across-automotive thesis. Invested in Featherless.ai for AI inference. Welcomed Cedric Char back as Principal in May 2026.

name: BP Ventures
notes: Corporate venture arm of BP, founded in 2006 and headquartered in London with offices in Houston and Tel Aviv. Invests in low-carbon mobility, advanced bioenergy, carbon management, power and storage, and digital change in energy.
thesis: Backs technology companies that align with BP's transition into an integrated energy company.
recent_activity: Continues to participate in growth rounds for charging, biofuels, and grid-edge companies through 2025-2026 alongside BP's broader transition investments.

name: BPI France
notes: Bpifrance is France's public investment bank serving as a one-stop shop for entrepreneurs. The institution combines banking, coaching, and direct investment, with a dedicated multi-billion-euro deep tech program.
thesis: Funds French companies through grants, loans, guarantees, and direct equity, with the Génération Deeptech plan supporting industrial and frontier science startups.
recent_activity: Hosting European Deeptech Week in Paris in March 2026. Launched Lesdeeptech.fr to support founders building deeptech startups in France.

name: Bain Capital Ventures Climate
notes: Bain Capital Ventures invests across AI, fintech, healthcare, security, and industrials, with climate and energy bets sitting inside the industrials practice rather than a dedicated climate fund. The firm led Crusoe's 2019 seed round and remains on its board, with later rounds backed by other Bain entities.
thesis: Backs growth stage industrial and infrastructure companies aligned with decarbonization, often building large positions over multiple rounds in capital intensive winners.
recent_activity: Crusoe closed a $1.375 billion Series E at a $10 billion-plus valuation, validating Bain's early conviction in AI compute infrastructure built on wasted energy.

name: Bee Partners
notes: Bee Partners is a 15-year-old pre-seed firm in San Francisco that backs technical founders before product-market fit. The firm has invested in nearly 100 companies and seen over $2B in follow-on capital, with a 60% Series A graduation rate.
thesis: Backs radical technical founders at the liminal moment before product and revenue, with concentration in AI agents for enterprises, accelerated hardware development, and physics-defying innovation.

name: Berkeley Angel Network
notes: The Berkeley Angel Network is an angel investing community for UC Berkeley alumni, faculty, and former faculty. The network has hosted pitches from more than 80 companies and pools $100K to $500K checks into Berkeley-affiliated founders.
thesis: Backs ventures formed by UC Berkeley students, alumni, or faculty, or built on Berkeley research, with a focus on capital-efficient teams.
recent_activity: The network is running pitch events on June 16, 2026 and previously hosted Skydeck Demo Day in April 2026 plus a Women in STEM panel.

name: Berkeley SkyDeck
notes: UC Berkeley's flagship startup accelerator, founded in 2012. Offers $200K per company through the SkyDeck Fund alongside mentorship from a network of 900+ advisors. Portfolio companies have raised over $2.7B with 25 Series A/B rounds and 17 acquisitions to date.
thesis: Six-month cohort-based program for global early-stage founders, with a strong deep-tech and Berkeley-faculty pipeline.
recent_activity: Batch 23 applications open in August 2026, continuing the program's twice-yearly cadence.

name: Bezos Earth Fund
notes: Jeff Bezos's $10B philanthropic climate commitment, with $2.3B distributed across 315 grants to date. Program areas include Conserving and Restoring Nature, Future of Food, Environmental Justice, Decarbonizing Energy and Industry, Economics and Markets, Next Technologies, and Monitoring and Data.
thesis: Funds science, technology, and conservation work that materially advances climate and biodiversity outcomes by 2030.
recent_activity: Recent grants include $34M for sustainable textile innovation, $27.4M for low-methane livestock (with Global Methane Hub), $24.5M for coastal protection in the Eastern Tropical Pacific, and $3.5M for advanced nuclear.

name: Bolt
notes: Bolt is a San Francisco pre-seed venture firm investing at the intersection of hardware and software. The firm runs from a $70 million current fund, has backed more than 70 companies, and leads or co-leads first rounds in over 90 percent of its portfolio.
thesis: Backs pre-seed, pre-product hardware founders with checks up to $500K, using an in-house prototyping facility to help teams iterate before product-market fit.
recent_activity: Bolt continued deploying its Fund III into hardware-software founders through 2025 and 2026, with active investing in robotics and connected devices.

name: Boomtown Accelerator
notes: Boomtown Accelerator is a Boulder, Colorado accelerator operating since 2013. The team has made 87 investments, runs custom corporate-partnered cohort programs, and operates the Boomtown Connectivity Lab for hardware and IoT development.
thesis: Builds bespoke accelerator programs for corporate partners across 16 industries, with hands-on hardware and software workshop support inside the Connectivity Lab.
recent_activity: Boomtown extended its multi-year hardware accelerator partnership with UpRamp and CableLabs while continuing to build custom cohort programs for corporate partners through 2026.

name: Booz Allen Ventures
notes: Booz Allen Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Booz Allen Hamilton, with a $300 million commitment tripled from prior levels. The team invests in early stage US startups in artificial intelligence, cyber, defense tech, quantum, and reindustrialization that can deliver disruptive capability to federal clients.
thesis: Backs dual-use commercial technology with clear paths to government deployment, often pairing capital with Booz Allen pull-through across critical missions.
recent_activity: Booz Allen Ventures invested in maritime autonomy company Ulysses in April 2026 and announced the ConductorAI investment in mid-2025, with plans for 20 to 25 new investments over the next five years.

name: Bosch Ventures (formerly RBVC)
notes: Robert Bosch's corporate venture arm with offices in Stuttgart, Frankfurt, Sunnyvale, Boston, Tel Aviv, and Shanghai. Invests in promising startups whose technology can connect to Bosch's mobility, manufacturing, sensors, and climate businesses.
thesis: $1M-$10M seed through Series B+ checks for technology relevant to Bosch's industrial and mobility platforms.
recent_activity: Recently migrated to bosch.ventures from bosch-ventures.com. Continues active investment from six global offices.

name: Boston Harbor Angels
award: $250K to $1.5M per round; often syndicates with other angel groups
notes: Boston Harbor Angels is a Boston angel group founded in 2004 that recently passed 20 years of investing. The group invests $250K to $1.5M per round across information technology, life sciences, consumer products, specialty materials, and aviation, often syndicating with other angel groups.
thesis: Pools accredited investor checks behind early-stage, high-growth New England founders across life sciences, medtech, cleantech, fintech, agritech, and IT.
recent_activity: Boston Harbor Angels participated in a $13 million seed round for ocular hydrogel platform GelMEDIX in February 2026.

name: Breakthrough Energy Ventures
notes: Bill Gates-founded climate-only venture firm backing patient capital into the five grand challenges: electricity, transport, buildings, manufacturing, and food. Backers include Bezos, Bloomberg, Branson, and Ambani. Manages over $4B across multiple funds.
thesis: Backs hardtech climate companies with potential to remove at least 500 megatons of greenhouse gases per year.
recent_activity: Raised $839M for Fund III. In early 2026, paused new investments from the Catalyst program and laid off staff as part of broader scale-back. Strategy now centers on Ventures and Catalyst portfolio companies.

name: Brookfield Global Transition Fund
notes: Brookfield Global Transition Fund is the climate transition platform inside Brookfield Asset Management. The fund targets renewables, sustainable industry, and business transformation at infrastructure scale.
thesis: Deploys infrastructure-size checks into the global energy transition, often as a controlling or anchor investor in scaling clean energy and sustainable industrial assets.

name: Buoyant Ventures
notes: Chicago-based early-stage climate VC founded in 2019, 100% female-owned and led. Backs digital tools, AI, software, and simple hardware that drive resource efficiency and decarbonization across large industries. Team of 11 with 4 partners and a portfolio of 13 companies.
thesis: Backs the intelligence layer for the physical world, where small efficiency gains compound into large climate and economic returns.
recent_activity: Promoted Laura Katzman to Partner in 2025; she has since led the firm's investments in Hammerhead, Gravity, and SkyFi.

name: Business Finland
notes: Business Finland is the Finnish public funding agency for innovation and internationalization. The agency offers Tempo grants, R&D funding, co-innovation projects with research organizations, and 6G and quantum partnership programs for Finnish companies.
thesis: Shares risk on innovative R&D and international expansion for Finnish companies and research organizations, with non-dilutive grants spanning from early Tempo awards through large research consortia.

name: CDMRP (DoD Congressionally Directed Medical Research)
notes: A Department of Defense biomedical research funding program established in 1992. Currently funds 31 research programs spanning cancer, neurological conditions (Alzheimer's, ALS, Parkinson's, MS), traumatic brain injury, spinal cord injury, lupus, autism, epilepsy, and hearing restoration.
thesis: Non-dilutive awards ($50K-$5M+) for disease-specific biomedical research aligned to congressional priorities.
recent_activity: FY26 funding webinars run through June-July 2026. Fifteen programs currently have active open funding opportunities including Alzheimer's, breast cancer, lung cancer, and vision research.

name: CDP Venture Capital
notes: CDP Venture Capital SGR is Italy's largest state-backed venture fund manager, with EUR 4.9 billion under management across 15 direct and indirect funds. The team makes seed through growth investments across deep tech, climate, life sciences, and digital, and runs the Digital Xcelerator program.
thesis: Backs Italian and Italy-relevant founders advancing strategic technology priorities including AI, cybersecurity, green transition, and digital health.
recent_activity: CDP Venture Capital announced a strategic partnership with TIM in 2026 and launched the Lumen II fund vehicle alongside Ulixes SGR and Lumen Ventures.

name: Callaghan Innovation
notes: New Zealand Crown agency that supports R&D-intensive businesses with grants, student internships, and technical services. Funds R&D Project Grants, R&D Experience Grants, and student programs across deep tech, climate, and life sciences.
thesis: Funds and supports New Zealand R&D-intensive businesses to commercialize science and engineering.
recent_activity: Announced 2025-2026 reforms to align with the new Public Research Organisations, with R&D grant programs continuing in parallel.

name: Cambridge Innovation Capital
notes: Cambridge Innovation Capital is a Cambridge-based venture firm affiliated with the University of Cambridge. The firm manages £0.6B and has invested £0.35B across 45+ life sciences and deep tech companies, mobilizing £3.2B+ in co-investment.
thesis: Backs life sciences and deep tech spinouts from the University of Cambridge and the surrounding cluster from seed through later rounds.
recent_activity: Bicycle Therapeutics is presenting clinical trial data at ASCO 2026. Nuclera launched an AI-driven antibody discovery service and CMR Surgical expanded its robotics platform into gynecology.

name: Cantos Ventures
notes: Cantos Ventures is a San Francisco pre-seed firm focused on near-frontier technology in physical industries. The fund backs founders working on space, robotics, energy, biology, and advanced manufacturing across roughly fifty portfolio companies.
thesis: Writes first checks into pre-seed deep tech where founders are applying near-frontier science to critical industries such as space, energy, manufacturing, and biology.

name: Capital Factory
notes: Capital Factory is a Texas-based accelerator and innovation hub with coworking locations in Austin, Dallas, Houston, San Antonio, and Washington DC. The program supports more than 1,000 venture-backed startups and runs DoD-funded dual-use technology tracks.
thesis: Backs Texas-anchored and dual-use startups with a $100K convertible note and access to corporate, government, and DoD partner programs including the Army Applications Lab.

name: Carbon to Value Initiative
notes: Carbontech accelerator and deployment program run jointly by NYU's Urban Future Lab, Greentown Labs, and Fraunhofer USA. Focused on CO2 capture, conversion to fuels, chemicals, and materials, plus sequestration and removal. Connects startups with the Carbontech Leadership Council, an executive group across industry sectors.
thesis: Bridges carbontech startups to industrial pilot partners and follow-on funding.
recent_activity: November 2025 named 10 startups for the fifth annual cohort, drawn from 165 applications across 33 countries. Program participants have raised over $600M in follow-on funding to date.

name: Cargill Ventures
notes: Strategic venture arm of agribusiness firm Cargill, focused on food, feed, agriculture, and biomaterials. Operates as part of Cargill's Breakthrough Capital platform alongside Black River Asset Management. Direct URL was retired and redirects to the corporate site.
thesis: Backs companies that strengthen Cargill's food, agriculture, and biomaterials supply chains.
recent_activity: Continues to deploy through Breakthrough Capital with 2025 activity concentrated in agricultural software and protein platforms.

name: Catapult Satellite Applications
notes: Satellite Applications Catapult is a UK innovation organization at Harwell Campus accelerating space data and downstream applications. It runs three missions: Beyond Earth (in-space economy), Autonomous & Connected Earth, and Resilient Earth.
thesis: Combines project funding, accelerator support, and shared testing facilities (drone testing, antenna and RF ranges, satellite AIT, in-orbit servicing labs) for UK space companies.
recent_activity: Supported companies have secured GBP 783M in private funding with 25 percent average annual turnover growth. Recent partnerships focus on autonomous systems testing and microgravity research with 20 organizations involved.

name: Caterpillar Venture Capital
notes: Corporate venture arm of Caterpillar founded in 2015, based in San Francisco. Backs energy transition, digital, robotics, autonomy, and advanced materials companies that align with Caterpillar's heavy equipment customers. Typical check ranges from $2M to $10M.
thesis: Backs early-stage companies in autonomy, robotics, energy transition, sustainability, and industrial technology.
recent_activity: Backed ElevenEs Series B first close in February 2026 (European battery manufacturing). 33 investments to date.

name: Chain Reaction Innovations (Argonne National Lab)
notes: A two-year fellowship program at Argonne National Laboratory for energy and science technology entrepreneurs. Part of the DOE Lab-Embedded Entrepreneurship Program (LEEP). Provides seed funding plus full-time access to Argonne facilities and researchers so founders can develop their technology toward commercialization.
thesis: Backs hard-science energy founders who need national-lab tools (batteries, hydrogen, quantum, advanced materials, biotech) to de-risk their technology.
recent_activity: Cohort 2026 finalists were selected from 11 finalists covering batteries, industrial process, performance materials, and biotech. Applications for Cohort 2027 open fall 2026.

name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative
notes: The Chan Zuckerberg Initiative is the philanthropy founded in 2015 by Priscilla Chan and Mark Zuckerberg. The organization has awarded roughly $7.22 billion in grants and deployed nearly $300 million into mission-aligned ventures across biomedical research, AI-for-science, and education.
thesis: Funds biomedical research, cell atlases, and AI-powered biology tools with the long-horizon goal of curing or preventing all disease this century.
recent_activity: The Chan Zuckerberg Initiative continued scaling CZ Biohub's AI-powered biology efforts and Learning Commons education programs through 2025 and 2026.

name: Cherry Ventures
notes: Cherry Ventures is a Berlin seed firm investing across European AI, energy, aerospace, healthtech, and fintech. The fund reports an 80% graduation rate from seed to Series A across its portfolio.
thesis: Writes seed checks into ambitious European founders solving meaningful societal, environmental, and business problems, with a generalist tilt toward AI and frontier tech.
recent_activity: In early 2026 Cherry led seeds into Riplo, Plato, Sphynx, and Heywa Labs across AI vertical applications.

name: Chevron Technology Ventures
notes: Corporate venture arm of Chevron based in Houston. Runs the Future Energy Fund for climate and emerging energy and the Core Venture Fund for industrial and oilfield tech. Active in seed through later-stage rounds globally.
thesis: Backs energy transition and industrial technologies with strategic relevance to Chevron's operations.
recent_activity: Page returned a 403 during this check; verify with Chevron PR.

name: Civilization Ventures
notes: Civilization Ventures is a San Francisco seed firm investing in early-stage life sciences and digital health. The firm started with a $1M pilot in 2017 and now manages over $200M across 70-plus partnered companies.
thesis: Backs founders building data-driven biology, genomics, diagnostics, synthetic biology, and digital health companies at seed.
recent_activity: Most recent investment was in 10x Science in April 2026. Portfolio includes one unicorn, three IPOs, and nine acquisitions.

name: Clean Energy Venture Group (CEVG)
notes: Clean Energy Venture Group is a Boston angel collective founded in 2005, with 35+ entrepreneurs and energy executives backing early-stage cleantech. The group has invested in 60+ climate tech companies and works alongside sister fund Clean Energy Ventures, which manages $415M+.
thesis: Provides seed capital and operating expertise to early-stage cleantech startups with line of sight to commercial product within 12 to 24 months.

name: Clean Energy Ventures
notes: Clean Energy Ventures is a Boston-based seed and Series A climate tech firm. The fund backs capital-efficient hardware and software companies with line of sight to multi-gigaton greenhouse gas reductions within three decades, often serving as the first institutional check.
thesis: Backs early-stage climate tech where each portfolio company can plausibly abate at least 2.5 gigatons of CO2 over its commercial lifetime.
recent_activity: In January 2026 the firm joined the launch of the $300M All Aboard Coalition Fund. Portfolio company NexDash closed €5M in November 2025 for AI-driven ground freight electrification.

name: Climate Capital
notes: Early-stage climate venture firm founded by Sundeep Ahuja in 2018. Writes small first checks across decarbonization, food and agriculture, deeptech, and climate adaptation. Portfolio exceeds 300 companies.
thesis: Backs early-stage climate founders with diversified first checks across every part of the decarbonization stack.
recent_activity: Restructured in 2025 to spin out Climate Capital Bio as Juniper and rebrand the index fund as CC Micro, with $10K to $25K check sizes for very early companies.

name: Climate Capital Syndicate
award: Rolling AngelList syndicate; $10K to $25K checks alongside lead investors
notes: Climate Capital Syndicate is an AngelList syndicate operated by Climate Capital alongside its rolling micro-fund. The syndicate mobilizes 2,000-plus operator and angel LPs into climate startups working on emissions reduction and adaptation, with typical SPV checks of $10K to $25K.
thesis: Co-invests alongside lead VCs in companies reducing greenhouse gas emissions or supporting adaptation, often serving as first or second money in.
recent_activity: The legacy syndicate now primarily handles SPV allocations for pro-rata rights on prior investments, with new deal allocations running through a separate syndicate structure.

name: ClimateHaven
award: 24/7 co-working plus prototyping space, no equity taken
notes: ClimateHaven is a nonprofit climate tech incubator in New Haven, Connecticut. The platform provides company-building support, mentorship, and dedicated workspace, with members tapping MakeHaven's 10,000 square feet of fabrication facilities next door.
thesis: Backs climate tech startups moving from lab to market, with a specialized Water Innovation Hub for water sector solutions.
recent_activity: Published an Annual Review in February 2026 plus a Water Innovation Hub piece on infrastructure constraints. The 2026 Water Innovation Hub application cycle is open.

name: Closed Loop Partners
notes: A New York investment firm building the circular economy through venture capital, private equity, catalytic capital, and an innovation center. Sectors include plastics and packaging, textiles, food and organics, and circular technology. Portfolio of 90+ investments across 10 countries with 50+ corporate partners.
thesis: Equity and project finance ($1M-$50M) for companies displacing virgin materials through reuse, recycling, and recovery.
recent_activity: Acquired Sutter Metals and Agri-Cycle, deployed credit to TemperPack and GreenMantra, launched a composting EPR consortium, and published the 2024 impact report.

name: Coatue Frontier
notes: Coatue is a global investment firm with five strategies spanning venture, growth, tactical credit, public markets, and CTEK, a blended public-private innovation fund. Frontier tech sits across these strategies, with major positions in AI infrastructure, defense, robotics, and biotech.
thesis: Backs category-defining frontier tech companies from late venture through growth, often making concentrated positions in AI compute, dual-use, and life sciences.
recent_activity: Coatue announced a partnership with Anthropic on enterprise intelligence in 2025-2026 and published market analysis on AI compute scaling.

name: Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy)
notes: Rebranded as Coefficient Giving in 2025. A San Francisco grantmaker advising Good Ventures (the foundation of Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz) and other donors. Funds high-impact work including biosecurity and pandemic preparedness, scientific research, AI safety, global health, and animal welfare.
thesis: Project-specific philanthropic grants for high-impact research and policy, including biosecurity countermeasures, gain-of-function policy, and next-generation PPE.
recent_activity: Rebranded from Open Philanthropy to Coefficient Giving in 2025. Cari Tuna took oversight of the Biosecurity and Pandemic Preparedness program in mid-2024.
correction_note: Rebranded mid-2025. Mission and team continue. Founders: Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz.

name: Conductive Ventures
notes: Conductive Ventures is a $450M Series A/B fund founded in 2017 and based in Palo Alto. The firm backs capital-efficient software, hardware, and tech-enabled businesses, often led by unconventional founders.
thesis: Concentrates on emerging category leaders moving industries from analog to digital and from digital to intelligent.
recent_activity: Ambiq listed on NYSE through an upsized IPO at $24 per share. Kalshi announced a $1B Series F at a $22B valuation led by Coatue.

name: Congruent Ventures
notes: Congruent Ventures is a San Francisco climate tech firm that backs early-stage North American teams from formation through Series A. The firm invests across hardware, software, and new business models tied to decarbonization.
thesis: Backs early climate tech founders working in atoms, bits, and business model innovation across energy, mobility, food and agriculture, and the built environment.
recent_activity: Published the 2026 50 by 2050 List recognizing 50 climate tech companies the firm sees driving the path to net zero.

name: Construct Capital
notes: Construct Capital is a Washington DC early-stage firm investing in foundational industries: manufacturing, logistics, defense, mobility, and energy. Founded by former General Catalyst partners Dayna Grayson and Rachel Holt, the firm closed Fund III at $300M.
thesis: Backs tech-first founders rebuilding underinvested foundational industries such as manufacturing, logistics, defense, and energy at the seed and Series A stages.
recent_activity: Closed Fund III at $300M. Portfolio activity in 2025 included Amca's $76M launch, Vammo's $45M Series B, and Kinetic's $21M round.

name: Continental Strategic Investments
notes: Corporate venture and strategic investment arm of Continental AG, the Hanover-based automotive supplier. Invests in automotive software, sensors, ADAS, materials, and adjacent mobility platforms.
thesis: Backs technology that strengthens Continental's automotive, industrial rubber, and tire businesses.
recent_activity: Continued to fund automotive AI and battery analytics startups in 2025-2026 as Continental restructured its powertrain business.

name: Convective Capital
notes: Specialty venture fund founded in 2022 by Bill Clerico investing exclusively in wildfire technology. Backs detection, prevention, response, and resilience companies as wildfire severity grows across the western US and globally.
thesis: Funds the next generation of wildfire prevention, detection, response, and recovery technology.
recent_activity: Closed Fund II in 2025 and continues to lead seed rounds in wildfire-focused hardware and AI companies as state and federal wildfire spending grows.

name: Conviction Partners
notes: Conviction is an AI-native venture firm founded by Sarah Guo, writing $1M to $25M checks and frequently leading first rounds. The team backs technical founders applying AI to industries from legal and biology to robotics and security.
thesis: Targets Software 3.0 founders building defensible products on top of frontier AI models, with deep technical and recruiting support from the partnership.
recent_activity: Conviction continued running its Embed grant program, the Mixture of Experts recruiting event, and the No Priors podcast through 2025 and 2026.

name: Cooperative Research Centres (CRC) Program
notes: The Cooperative Research Centres Program is an Australian federal grant program funding industry-led research collaborations. CRC Grants support 3-10 year programs; CRC-P grants run up to three years.
thesis: Up to 50 percent of project costs for consortia with at least three industry partners and two research institutions tackling industry-identified problems with PhD-level training.
recent_activity: Round 27 guidelines released March 6, 2026 with Stage 1 applications closing April 29, 2026. Round 28 launches in 2027 with roughly AUD $50M concentrated on AI commercialization.

name: Counteract Partners
notes: A London-based carbon removal specialist seed fund. Counteract One targets pre-seed and seed companies aiming to catalyze 5 gigatons of CO2e drawdown by 2050. Covers direct air capture, enhanced weathering, nature-based solutions, carbon utilization, green hydrogen, and agricultural sequestration.
thesis: £500K-£2M checks into pre-seed and seed carbon removal companies across DAC, biomaterials, soil, and coastal approaches.
recent_activity: Published 2026 perspective pieces on financing carbon sequestration curves and positioning carbon removal as a strategic industrial asset.

name: Creative Destruction Lab (CDL)
notes: Nonprofit founded in 2012 at the Rotman School at the University of Toronto. Runs a nine-month objectives-based mentorship program for seed-stage science and technology companies across 23 streams and 16 global locations. Streams include AI, Quantum, Climate, Energy, Space, Defence, Health, and Matter.
thesis: Backs technical founders with structured mentorship from operators, scientists, and investors, no equity taken.
recent_activity: Announced CDL-Doha with Qatar Investment Authority funding and CDL-San Sebastian launching in Spain on quantum and NLP. Applications open for 2026/27.

name: Cyclotron Road (at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab)
notes: Cyclotron Road is a fellowship program embedded at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab, run jointly with Activate. It supports entrepreneurial scientists across energy, AI, biomanufacturing, and critical materials with stipends, research funding, and LBNL facility access.
thesis: Backs early-stage deep tech founders whose work needs national-lab equipment and bench science to de-risk before raising venture capital.
recent_activity: Welcomed 12 new fellows in 2025 and marked its 10-year anniversary in 2026, with alumni having raised over $3B in follow-on funding.

name: DASA (UK Defence and Security Accelerator)
notes: The Defence and Security Accelerator is a UK government innovation pipeline under the Ministry of Defence, now part of UK Defence Innovation as of February 2026. It runs themed Innovation Focus Areas plus an Open Call.
thesis: Finds and funds exploitable defense and security innovation through grant competitions ranging from GBP 50K to GBP 3M.
recent_activity: Announced a competition with up to GBP 1.5M for conflict wound care innovations in January 2026. Published 2025 Impact Report and updated transparency data on funded contracts.

name: DCVC (Data Collective)
notes: Fifteen-year-old deep tech firm in Palo Alto and San Francisco backing computational biology, climate, industrial transformation, smart agriculture, space, and frontier compute. Team includes more published scientists than MBAs. Recently announced $1B in fresh funding.
thesis: Backs companies using deep computation and AI to solve high-stakes physical and biological problems.
recent_activity: Fervo Energy IPO announced May 2026. Published the 2025 Deep Tech Opportunities Report alongside the new fund close.

name: DCVC Bio
notes: DCVC's dedicated bio strategy, founded in 2018. Closed a $400M Fund III in September 2024 following a $350M Fund II in 2020. Focus is on computation-enhanced biology platforms rather than single drug candidates.
thesis: Backs AI-driven biology platforms producing new categories of therapeutics, agriculture, and bio-manufactured products.
recent_activity: Fund III ($400M) deployed across engineering biology platforms and AI-driven drug discovery.

name: DHS Silicon Valley Innovation Program
notes: The DHS Silicon Valley Innovation Program funds startups solving homeland security challenges through Other Transaction Authority awards. The program offers up to $2 million in non-dilutive funding over 24 months and has supported more than 60 portfolio companies to date.
recent_activity: Active topic calls in 2025 and 2026 include Synthetic Data Generator and Securing Soft Targets, with awarded vendors including Betterdata, DataCebo, MOSTLY AI, and Rockfish Data.

name: DMZ (Toronto Metropolitan University)
award: Non-dilutive, no equity, no fees; 6 to 12-month tracks
notes: DMZ is the Toronto Metropolitan University tech incubator, ranked the #1 university incubator worldwide by UBI Global. The organization has supported 2,600-plus startups that raised $3.1B and created 25,000 jobs across a global network in 15-plus countries.
thesis: Backs founders with non-dilutive support across cleantech, health tech, fintech, edtech, and physical product categories through an 18-month Incubator, 9-month Pre-Incubator, and specialized programs.
recent_activity: Incubator Fall 2026 cohort applications opened with a September 1 deadline. The Siakam EdTech Engine and Backing Canadian Small Businesses programs are running with new mentorship and Amex Canada grants.

name: DOE EERE
notes: The Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation, formerly the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, is a Department of Energy office funding research, development, and deployment across critical minerals, advanced energy, and consumer technology. The office issues solicitations from $500K through $50M.
thesis: Funds non-dilutive research, development, and demonstration in critical minerals supply chains, advanced energy, hydropower, manufacturing, and consumer-facing technology programs.
recent_activity: In May 2026 announced $45M for critical mineral supply chain projects, $52M for domestic manufacturing, and $10M for a high-performance computing innovation program.

name: DOE Loan Programs Office (LPO)
deadline: Rolling via Energy Dominance Financing Program (EDFP, Section 1706)
notes: Now operating as the Office of Energy Dominance Financing within DOE. Provides loan guarantees and direct loans for energy generation, grid reliability, advanced vehicles, tribal energy, advanced nuclear, manufacturing, and innovative energy technology. First-of-a-kind project financing at $100M to multi-billion scale.
thesis: Finances large, capital-intensive energy and manufacturing projects that struggle to secure commercial debt.
recent_activity: Closed a $26.5B loan package with Southern Company subsidiaries in February 2026, the largest in DOE history. Announced conditional commitment for a domestic medical isotope facility in April 2026 and $50M for tribal energy in March 2026.

past_awardees.name: Plug Power
past_awardees.description: Builds green hydrogen production plants and fuel-cell systems for trucks and forklifts.

past_awardees.name: Lithium Americas
past_awardees.description: Develops the Thacker Pass lithium mine and processing plant in Nevada to supply the US battery industry.

past_awardees.name: Holtec International
past_awardees.description: Restarts the Palisades nuclear power plant in Michigan and develops small modular reactors.

past_awardees.name: Ultium Cells (GM and LG joint venture)
past_awardees.description: Builds large-format lithium-ion battery cell factories supplying General Motors electric vehicles.

past_awardees.name: Convergent Energy and Power
past_awardees.description: Develops and operates battery energy storage projects for utility and commercial customers.

past_awardees.name: Sunnova Energy
past_awardees.description: Finances residential solar and battery systems through a partial loan guarantee covering homeowner loans.

past_awardees.name: Eos Energy Enterprises
past_awardees.description: Manufactures zinc-bromide battery systems for long-duration grid storage.

past_awardees.name: Li-Cycle
past_awardees.description: Recycles lithium-ion batteries at hub facilities that recover lithium, cobalt, and nickel.

name: DOE NETL
notes: National Energy Technology Laboratory of the US Department of Energy, headquartered in Pittsburgh with sites in Morgantown and Albany. Funds and conducts research on carbon management, hydrogen, fossil-to-clean transition, and critical minerals.
thesis: Funds carbon capture, hydrogen, and clean fossil energy research through cooperative agreements, FOAs, and SBIR.
recent_activity: Continues to issue funding opportunity announcements across point-source carbon capture, hydrogen hubs, and critical minerals extraction through 2025-2026.

name: DOE Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations
notes: The Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations sits inside the US Department of Energy and was created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. The office partners with industry to scale demonstrations across hydrogen, carbon management, advanced nuclear, energy storage, and grid technology.
thesis: Co-funds first-of-a-kind clean energy demonstrations at commercial scale, including Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs, Direct Air Capture Hubs, and the Industrial Demonstrations Program.
recent_activity: In February 2026 announced the first new US aluminum smelter construction since 1980. Terminated 223 projects in October 2025 and another 24 in May 2025, citing $10.5B in combined taxpayer savings.

past_awardees.name: HyVelocity (Gulf Coast Hydrogen Hub)
past_awardees.description: Industrial coalition led by ExxonMobil, Chevron, and AES building large-scale clean hydrogen production along the Gulf Coast.

past_awardees.name: MachH2 (Midwest Hydrogen Hub)
past_awardees.description: Industrial coalition producing clean hydrogen for steel, glass, and refining customers across Illinois, Indiana, and Michigan.

past_awardees.name: Cleveland-Cliffs
past_awardees.description: Builds a hydrogen-ready direct reduced iron plant and electric melting furnaces at its Middletown, Ohio steel mill.

past_awardees.name: Heidelberg Materials
past_awardees.description: Adds carbon capture and storage to its Mitchell, Indiana cement plant to cut CO2 from cement production.

past_awardees.name: Constellium
past_awardees.description: Converts its Ravenswood, West Virginia aluminum mill to electric induction heating instead of gas furnaces.

past_awardees.name: Sublime Systems
past_awardees.description: Builds cement using an electrochemical process that runs at room temperature instead of a 1,400 degree kiln.

past_awardees.name: Brimstone Energy
past_awardees.description: Produces low-carbon cement and supplemental alumina from calcium silicate rocks instead of limestone.

past_awardees.name: Climeworks (Project Cypress)
past_awardees.description: Builds a direct air capture facility in Louisiana that pulls CO2 from the atmosphere and stores it underground.

name: DOE Office of Science
notes: The DOE Office of Science is the largest US federal supporter of basic research in the physical sciences. The office runs six core programs and ten national laboratories spanning advanced computing, materials, biology, fusion, particle physics, and nuclear science.
recent_activity: The Office of Science announced $293 million in funding for the Genesis Mission focused on AI applications in science and energy, alongside active fusion energy and biological research solicitations.

name: DOE Tech Commercialization Fund (TCF)
award: Historically $100K-$1.5M per topic; 50% non-federal match required
deadline: FY26 CLIMR Commercialization Enabling: Full Application 6/16/2026 (concept paper closed 4/1)
notes: The Technology Commercialization Fund is a DOE program established by Congress in 2005 to move research from the national labs into commercial markets. It funds cooperative arrangements between DOE facilities and private or academic partners on a cost-share basis.
thesis: Bridges lab-to-market for DOE national-lab innovations through 50 percent matching grants of $250K to $1.5M.
recent_activity: Awarded $35M across 42 projects in August 2025 (FY25), with $21M in matching funds. FY26 lab call CLIMR opened February 2026; concept papers due April 1, full applications due June 16, 2026.

past_awardees.name: Lawrence Berkeley National Lab (Cradle to Commerce program)
past_awardees.description: National lab program that has helped energy startups spinning out of Berkeley Lab raise more than $15M in 18 months.

past_awardees.name: Argonne (OpenMC commercialization)
past_awardees.description: National lab project commercializing open-source nuclear reactor simulation software for advanced reactor licensing.

past_awardees.name: Energy Program for Innovation Clusters (EPIC) recipients
past_awardees.description: Funds incubators and accelerators that support clean energy startups in regional innovation clusters.

past_awardees.name: NREL spinout licensing projects
past_awardees.description: National lab program commercializing solar, wind, and grid technologies developed at the National Renewable Energy Laboratory.

past_awardees.name: Pacific Northwest National Lab (Visual IP Search)
past_awardees.description: National lab project upgrading a tool that helps companies search and license patents across all 17 DOE labs.

past_awardees.name: Oak Ridge National Lab (industrial partnerships portfolio)
past_awardees.description: National lab program funding industry-matched projects in advanced manufacturing, materials, and AI for energy.

name: DSM-Firmenich Venturing
notes: DSM-Firmenich Venturing is the corporate venture arm of the Swiss nutrition and materials firm DSM-Firmenich. Since 2001 the group has backed more than 100 startups and currently holds around 40 active investments across health, nutrition, personal care, and food ingredients.
thesis: Backs early-stage companies in health, nutrition, personal care, food, and beverage ingredients across North America, Europe, and Israel.
recent_activity: In 2026 DSM-Firmenich agreed to divest its Animal Nutrition and Health business to CVC Capital Partners.

name: DTRA
notes: Defense Threat Reduction Agency, the US Department of Defense agency responsible for countering weapons of mass destruction. Funds research and development in chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and high-yield explosive defense.
thesis: Funds technology development across chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear defense missions.
recent_activity: Continues to award Joint Science and Technology Office and SBIR contracts across CBRN sensing, decontamination, and medical countermeasures through 2025-2026.

name: Decisive Point
notes: A veteran-led venture firm investing in critical technologies for defense, energy, and infrastructure. Offices in New York, Washington DC, and Boston. Provides federal acquisition expertise, government relations support, and customer discovery alongside capital.
thesis: $1M-$10M seed and Series A checks into dual-use founders building for defense, energy, and infrastructure customers.
recent_activity: Operates NSIN Propel accelerators in partnership with the DoD National Security Innovation Network.

name: Deep Science Ventures
award: Up to 18 months of funding for founders, around 10% equity
notes: Deep Science Ventures is a London venture creator that identifies scientific bottlenecks, recruits scientist and engineer founders, and funds them to build companies. The firm reports 58 companies launched, $220M raised, and a $700M portfolio valuation across agriculture, computation, climate, pharma, and education.
thesis: Reverse-engineers ventures from sector-level outcome targets, then recruits founder-scientists and provides up to 18 months of funding in exchange for roughly 10% equity.
recent_activity: Launched CureAge in February 2025 and announced 2026 partnerships with Renaissance Philanthropy on crop resilience and with SPRIND and Builders Vision on entrepreneurship-focused doctoral training.

name: Deerfield Management
notes: Deerfield Management is a New York healthcare investment firm and one of the largest dedicated life sciences capital providers. The firm is active across public and private biotech and runs a venture fund alongside its hedge platform and philanthropic arm.
thesis: Provides flexible capital across the healthcare ecosystem, from public biotech positions to private growth equity and venture-stage life sciences companies.
recent_activity: Closed a healthcare venture fund of over $600M. Sold Melinta Therapeutics in 2025 and launched a 2025 mobility research alliance with Hospital for Special Surgery.

name: Defense Angels Network
award: Public-facing AngelList syndicate; member-driven individual checks beyond the syndicate vehicle
