notes: Operator-led angel group for defense and national security companies. Focus areas: Advanced Energy and Materials, Aerospace, AI/Autonomy, Communications, Computing, and Human Systems. Portfolio includes Umbra and Mach Industries. Founders can apply to the syndicate via AngelList.

name: Defense Innovation Unit (DIU)
hq: Mountain View, CA / Austin, TX / Boston, MA / DC
deadline: No open Commercial Solutions Openings as of May 2026 : between cycles. Register for notifications.
notes: The Defense Innovation Unit accelerates DoD adoption of commercial technology. With offices in Mountain View, Austin, Boston, and DC, it matches national security problems to commercial solutions via Other Transaction Authority contracts.
thesis: Brings commercial-first technology into the Department of Defense in 24 months or less through flexible OTA contracting.
recent_activity: Companies under contract are backed by top-tier investors representing $20.1B in private capital. Continues regional outreach to underrepresented areas.

past_awardees.name: Anduril Industries
past_awardees.description: Builds autonomous drones, counter-drone systems, and AI defense software for the US and allied militaries.

past_awardees.name: Shield AI
past_awardees.description: Builds AI pilots for drones that fly without GPS or radio links, including the V-BAT vertical takeoff aircraft.

past_awardees.name: Saildrone
past_awardees.description: Operates wind- and solar-powered uncrewed surface vessels that collect maritime intelligence.

past_awardees.name: Vannevar Labs
past_awardees.description: Builds AI software that processes open-source intelligence and foreign-language signals for defense customers.

past_awardees.name: Aalyria Technologies
past_awardees.description: Builds software that manages mesh communications networks across satellites, aircraft, ships, and ground stations.

past_awardees.name: Skydio
past_awardees.description: Builds autonomous drones used by police, infrastructure inspectors, and military units, including in Ukraine.

past_awardees.name: Intellihot
past_awardees.description: Builds commercial heat pump water heaters for military bases and large facilities.

name: Demeter Partners
notes: Demeter Partners is a Paris energy transition investor that merged with Cerea Partners in January 2025 to form Demea Invest. The combined firm manages €3B across venture, growth, infrastructure, mezzanine, and private debt with 70 staff across Paris, Lyon, Bordeaux, Düsseldorf, Madrid, and Montreal.
thesis: Backs European energy transition and agribusiness companies from venture through growth, infrastructure, and private debt, including a dedicated Paris Green Fund for urban ecological transition.
recent_activity: Merged with Cerea Partners in January 2025 to form Demea Invest, with 450 supported companies across the combined platform.

name: Deviation Capital (formerly Two Sigma Ventures)
notes: Two Sigma Ventures rebranded to Deviation Capital in 2025. The firm operates as a data-driven, science-and-engineering oriented venture firm spun out of Two Sigma, backing founders across deep tech, climate, life sciences, and industrial AI.
thesis: Applies quantitative and engineering rigor to early stage venture, with active practices in climate, life sciences, and industrial AI.
recent_activity: The firm completed its rebrand from Two Sigma Ventures to Deviation Capital in 2025, redirecting traffic from its prior domain.
correction_note: Spun out from Two Sigma in 2025 as an independent venture firm under the Deviation Capital brand.

name: E2MC Ventures
notes: E2MC Ventures is a Luxembourg-anchored space economy venture firm. The team backs upstream and downstream founders in launch, in-orbit services, Earth observation, and space-enabled defense, combining capital with specialist domain experience.
thesis: Invests at seed and Series A in pioneers building the space economy, from satellite design and propulsion to in-space manufacturing and geospatial AI.
recent_activity: E2MC launched the Orbital Edge Accelerator with the ISS National Lab in April 2025 and participated in the SatShow investor panel in March 2026.

name: E8 Angels
award: Membership-based group with pooled angel checks
notes: E8 Angels is a non-profit cleantech angel network founded in Seattle in 2006. With 140-plus members across the US and Canada, the group has invested more than $75M in 180-plus early-stage clean technology companies and runs the affiliated Decarbon8-US philanthropic fund.
thesis: Targets pre-seed to Series A cleantech in renewables, energy storage, sustainable transport, the built environment, and agtech with a dual financial and impact return profile.

name: EIT Climate-KIC
notes: The EU's climate innovation community, an independent non-profit foundation registered in the Netherlands with branches across Europe. Runs place-based transformations, learning programs (Climate KIC Academy), entrepreneurship support, and the Europe 2030 missions. Operates accelerator and venture programs alongside policy work.
thesis: €10K-€500K of program funding plus deep ecosystem support for climate ventures, regions, and cities.
recent_activity: Active 2025-2026 programs include Pathways 2 Resilience (100 European regions), NetZeroCities, Deep Demo work in Ireland and Slovenia, and the Tanzania Adaptation Innovation Cluster.

name: EIT Health
notes: The EU's medtech and digital health Knowledge and Innovation Community, headquartered in Munich. Connects roughly 100 member organizations across business, research, education, and healthcare delivery. Has supported nearly 3,000 startups and trained 65,000+ professionals since 2016.
thesis: Project funding plus accelerator support for healthcare and biotech founders across the EU.
recent_activity: EIT Jumpstarter 2026 applications recently opened. Published a Think Tank biotech report warning of structural barriers to European biotech competitiveness.

name: EIT InnoEnergy
notes: European clean tech innovation engine launched in 2010 by the European Institute of Innovation and Technology. Operates across 21 EU countries and the US. Has supported 540+ companies, with 160+ active portfolio companies that have raised over EUR 35B to date.
thesis: Industrializes clean tech across batteries, hydrogen, green steel, solar, EV charging, and circular economy.
recent_activity: Stegra secured EUR 1.4B for green steel plant completion. ROSI raised EUR 20M+ for solar panel recycling. &Charge raised EUR 5M Series A for EV charging.

name: EIT Manufacturing
notes: EIT Manufacturing was a pan-European manufacturing innovation network operating under EIT co-funding, headquartered in Paris-Saclay. The central organization ceased operations on April 10, 2026, though regional offices in Vienna, San Sebastian, Milan, and Gothenburg remain active.
thesis: Trains manufacturers, runs AGORA and Tech Radar innovation platforms, and supports startups via Venture Building, BoostUp!, and Access2Tech Europe.
recent_activity: Central ASBL entity wound down on April 10, 2026. Regional offices continue operating. Beneficiaries are directed to the appointed liquidator for outstanding matters.
correction_note: Central EIT Manufacturing ASBL ceased operations April 10, 2026. Regional offices and programs may continue independently.

name: ENISA
notes: ENISA is the Spanish state-owned company offering participative loans to SMEs and startups. Loans range from €25K to €1.5M with up to 9-year maturity and no personal collateral required, providing non-bank quasi-equity to technology companies.
thesis: Provides participative loans as non-dilutive quasi-equity to innovative Spanish SMEs and startups, with returns linked to company profitability.
recent_activity: Unified historical lines into a single Startups y Pymes Innovadoras line backed by the €303M FEPYME fund for 2025 and 2026, plus a €40M European Investment Fund agreement.

name: EQT Ventures
notes: EQT Ventures is the early-stage technology venture arm of European private equity firm EQT, with EUR 2.265 billion committed across three funds. The team backs Generation Defining Companies from offices in Stockholm, London, Paris, Berlin, Amsterdam, and New York.
thesis: Backs European founders with the potential to define the next decade across deep tech, climate, AI robotics, and digital health, deploying capital plus operator advisory.

name: ESA BIC (European Space Agency Business Incubation Centres)
notes: A pan-European network of business incubators operated by the European Space Agency. Provides workspace, ESA technical mentorship, and €50K-€500K of funding to space-relevant startups. Over 20 centres across Europe have incubated 1,400+ companies.
thesis: Two-year incubation for early-stage founders applying space technology or developing downstream applications.
recent_activity: Celebrated 20 years of ESA BIC in Europe and continued operator-led programming through 2026, including Ad Astra Summit (Germany) and InCubed-supported Czech satellite monitoring.

name: ETF Partners
notes: ETF Partners (The Environmental Technologies Fund) is a London-based sustainability VC founded in 2005. The firm has 56 portfolio companies across environmental tech, sustainability tech, and enterprise software.
thesis: Backs European growth-stage companies whose technology drives sustainable prosperity across mobility, energy, and resource efficiency.
recent_activity: Latest disclosed investment was QuantWare in May 2026, supporting its $176M financing round. Zeelo named to the Boston Business Journal 2026 Best Places to Work list.

name: EU EIC Accelerator
award: Grant up to €2.5M + equity up to €10M = €17.5M typical ceiling
deadline: Final 2026 cutoff: November 4, 2026 at 17:00 Brussels
notes: European Innovation Council's flagship deep tech program under Horizon Europe. Provides up to EUR 2.5M lump-sum grants plus EUR 1-10M in equity through the EIC Fund. Targets companies at TRL 6-8 with one-application-per-company limits across 2021-2027.
thesis: Funds EU-based startups and SMEs with market-ready deep tech and scale-up potential.
recent_activity: 923 full applications submitted for the October 2025 deadline. 61 startups selected in February 2026 across the Challenges (EUR 220M) and Open (EUR 414M) tracks.

past_awardees.name: Mecuris
past_awardees.description: Designs 3D-printed prosthetics and orthotics customized to each patient with cloud software.

past_awardees.name: Sironax
past_awardees.description: Develops drugs targeting cell death pathways for neurodegenerative diseases.

past_awardees.name: Verkor
past_awardees.description: Manufactures lithium-ion battery cells in France for European electric vehicle makers.

past_awardees.name: Pasqal
past_awardees.description: Builds neutral-atom quantum computers and runs cloud quantum computing services.

past_awardees.name: Carbon Atlantis
past_awardees.description: Captures CO2 from the air using high-temperature electrochemical reactors.

past_awardees.name: Aerones
past_awardees.description: Builds robots that inspect and clean wind turbine blades without taking turbines offline for long.

past_awardees.name: Quantum Brilliance
past_awardees.description: Builds room-temperature diamond-based quantum computers small enough to sit next to classical servers.

past_awardees.name: Phytoponics
past_awardees.description: Builds deep-water hydroponic systems that grow vegetables with less water and no soil.

name: EU EIC Pathfinder
award: Standard grants up to €4M; Booster grants up to €50K
deadline: Pathfinder Open: closed May 12, 2026; Pathfinder Challenges: October 28, 2026
notes: EIC Pathfinder is the European Innovation Council's funding program for early-stage deep tech research at TRL 1-4. It supports consortia and individual applicants developing the scientific basis for breakthrough technologies.
thesis: Backs high-risk, pre-commercial science via Pathfinder Open and themed Pathfinder Challenges, with up to EUR 4M per grant.
recent_activity: 2026 Pathfinder Open call closes May 12, 2026 with 2,103 proposals received. Pathfinder Challenges close October 28, 2026 with a EUR 96M budget. EUR 118M went to 30 projects under the 2025 Challenges call.

name: EU Innovation Fund
award: Up to 60% of project costs. €20M-€100M+ project sizes.
deadline: IF25 Net-Zero Technologies call closed 4/23/2026 (results Q3 2026). Next call expected late 2026.
notes: The Innovation Fund is one of the world's largest low-carbon technology demonstration programs, funded by EU ETS revenues from 2020-2030. Total budget reaches EUR 40B at a EUR 75/tonne carbon price.
thesis: Backs clean tech demonstration and flagship projects in energy-intensive industries, renewables, storage, mobility, buildings, hydrogen, and CCUS.
recent_activity: Announced EUR 2.7B for 54 clean industry projects in March 2026 and over EUR 1B for European hydrogen projects in May 2026. Approximately 260 projects funded with EUR 15B awarded to date.

past_awardees.name: Stegra (formerly H2 Green Steel)
past_awardees.description: Builds a green steel plant in northern Sweden that uses hydrogen instead of coal to reduce iron ore.

past_awardees.name: Cemex with Linde (CO2LLECT)
past_awardees.description: Captures 1.3 million tons of CO2 per year from a Cemex cement plant in Germany and pipes it to storage.

past_awardees.name: Stella Maris CCS (Altera)
past_awardees.description: Stores up to 42 million tons of CO2 in offshore reservoirs through a UK-Norway carbon capture project.

past_awardees.name: Holcim Belgium (GO4ZERO)
past_awardees.description: Retrofits a cement plant in Belgium with carbon capture and a new oxyfuel kiln to produce near-zero-emission cement.

past_awardees.name: Carbfix
past_awardees.description: Mineralizes captured CO2 into solid rock by injecting it into Icelandic basalt formations.

past_awardees.name: Northvolt
past_awardees.description: Manufactures lithium-ion battery cells in Sweden and Germany for European electric vehicle makers.

past_awardees.name: Stockholm Exergi (BECCS Stockholm)
past_awardees.description: Captures CO2 from a biomass-fueled heat and power plant in Stockholm and stores it under the North Sea.

past_awardees.name: Verbio (eSAF Bitterfeld)
past_awardees.description: Builds a German plant that produces synthetic aviation fuel from biogenic CO2 and renewable hydrogen.

name: Earlybird Health
notes: Earlybird Health is the dedicated healthcare fund within Earlybird Venture Capital, with offices in Munich, Berlin, and London. The fund closed a fresh EUR 173M vehicle to back European companies from early development to advanced clinical stages.
thesis: Invests across digital health, diagnostics, medical devices, R&D tools, and biopharma in Europe, often through to regulatory approval.
recent_activity: Promoted Rabab Nasrallah and Christoph Massner to Partner in October 2025. Celebrated the ImCheck Therapeutics acquisition at up to EUR 1B.

name: Earlybird-X
notes: Earlybird-X was the deep tech fund within Earlybird Venture Capital, focused on European university spinouts in hardware, robotics, AI, and industrial technology. In late 2025 the founding partners launched a €60M independent successor called U2V to continue backing deeptech spinouts.
thesis: Backs deep tech spinouts from leading European universities including TU Munich, RWTH Aachen, Imperial, Oxford, Cambridge, École Polytechnique, and ETH Zurich.
recent_activity: Founding partners spun out in late 2025 to launch U2V, a €60M independent deep tech university-spinout fund. Earlybird closed its broader €360M Fund VIII in April 2026.

name: Earthshot Ventures
notes: Earthshot Ventures is an early-stage climate fund spun out of Elemental Excelerator. The firm invests pre-seed to Series B in energy, AI compute, and critical mineral companies, looking for fast paths to revenue.
thesis: Backs technologies defining the next industrial era, with focus on compute efficiency for AI, energy infrastructure, and mineral supply chains where products are cheaper, better, or faster than the status quo.
recent_activity: Active early 2026 with portfolio updates around AI compute efficiency and critical mineral plays.

name: Eclipse Ventures
notes: Eclipse Ventures is a Palo Alto firm investing in companies transforming the physical economy. Its 'reindustrialization' thesis spans supply chain, manufacturing, materials, and defense.
thesis: Backs founders rebuilding critical systems in supply chain, manufacturing, robotics, and defense at seed through Series B+.
recent_activity: Bright Machines, Eclipse's first buildco from 2018, has scaled past 200 employees with follow-on from BlackRock, Microsoft, and Nvidia.

name: Elemental Excelerator (Elemental Impact)
notes: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit climate investor with 17 years of history backing early-stage climate and environmental tech. Combines catalytic capital with project expertise, local partnerships, and investor mobilization. Portfolio companies report $110 of follow-on capital per dollar invested and are 2.5x more likely to survive from early to late commercial stage than peers.
thesis: Catalytic capital up to $500K (project plus equity blended) for climate startups deploying in real communities, especially energy, industry, food, water, and transportation.
recent_activity: Hosted Elemental Interactive 2026 ('Be Bold and Build') and announced first 2026 investments. Released 'A Year of Extremes' 2025 annual report.

name: Emerson Ventures
notes: Emerson Ventures is the corporate venture arm of automation firm Emerson Electric, based in St. Louis. The fund invests in discrete automation, industrial software, energy transition, and edge IoT, complementing Emerson's majority stake in AspenTech.
thesis: Backs startups in industrial software, automation hardware, and energy transition technologies that align with Emerson's process and discrete manufacturing platform.
recent_activity: Invested in EECOMOBILITY in late 2024 for AI battery production testing, supporting EV and storage manufacturing scale-up.

name: Enel X Innovation
notes: Enel X Innovation operates within Enel's Innovation Hubs network, scouting startups in distributed energy, EV charging, and grid technologies for the Italian utility. Enel plans roughly €20B of renewable investment between 2026 and 2028 as part of a €53B capex plan.
thesis: Sources and partners with startups whose technologies advance Enel's distributed energy, EV charging, and grid platforms across Europe and Tier 1 markets.
recent_activity: Enel's 2026-2028 strategic plan targets 80+ GW of installed renewable capacity by 2028, up from 68 GW at year-end 2025.

name: Energize Capital (Energize Ventures)
notes: Energize Capital is a Chicago-based growth fund backing software and services for the digitized, decarbonized economy. Backed Arcadia and Volta historically; current focus is asset-light digitally-enabled solutions across energy, climate, and industrials.
thesis: Software and services that enable energy transition, with growth-stage check sizes of $10M-$75M.
recent_activity: Closed a $330M second fund. Led $21M Series A in Halcyon (AI-native energy markets) and $16M Series A in Nixtla (foundational time-series AI).

name: Energy Impact Partners (EIP)
notes: A global energy transition investor with over $4.5B in assets under management and 80+ corporate utility and industrial partners. Invests across venture, growth, private equity, and credit, primarily at Series B and later. Offices in New York, San Francisco, Washington DC, Atlanta, Palm Beach, London, Cologne, and Oslo.
thesis: Backs energy transition companies with capital plus access to a coalition of 80+ utility and industrial partners who can pilot and deploy their technology.
recent_activity: Active investor through 2026 with 10 new investments in the trailing 12 months across a portfolio of 133 companies, including 6 unicorns and 5 IPOs.

name: Energy Innovation Capital
notes: Energy Innovation Capital is a Houston-anchored energy technology venture firm with more than $275 million under management. The team backs commercialization-stage industrial and energy transition companies from offices in Houston, San Francisco, Boston, and New York.
thesis: Backs companies developing the next generation of abundant, clean, and accessible energy technology, leaning on operator and corporate venture backgrounds for diligence.
recent_activity: EIC continued investing across its third fund with 27 active portfolio companies and ran the EIC Rose Rock affiliate vehicle through 2025 and 2026.

name: Engineering Capital
notes: Palo Alto pre-seed fund founded by Ashmeet Sidana that backs technical insights from engineering-led founders. Writes early checks in enterprise infrastructure, security, AI tooling, and industrial software before traditional venture firms participate.
thesis: Funds founders with novel technical insights at the moment of conception, before product-market fit is visible.
recent_activity: Continues to lead pre-seed rounds in AI infrastructure and developer tooling, with portfolio companies CodeRabbit and Kentik scaling enterprise traction in 2025.

name: Eni Next
notes: Eni Next is the corporate venture arm of Italian energy major Eni. The fund invests in high-growth startups developing low-carbon energy technologies including fusion, long-duration storage, clean hydrogen, and direct ocean capture.
thesis: Backs growth-stage startups whose technologies plug into Eni's energy transition roadmap across fusion, storage, hydrogen, and carbon capture.
recent_activity: In June 2025 Eni Next partnered with Azimut on a €100M Luxembourg-domiciled fund targeting US cleantech in decarbonization, energy efficiency, sustainable mobility, and the circular economy.

name: Enterprise Singapore
notes: Enterprise Singapore is the Singapore government agency for enterprise development. The agency offers advisory, financial, and partnership support, with startup channels including Startup SG Tech and Startup SG Equity.
thesis: Helps Singapore companies build capability, innovate, internationalize, and meet quality standards, with dedicated Centres of Innovation and a Global Innovation Alliance.
recent_activity: Held the 2026 Annual Media Briefing covering 2025 partnerships and 2026 plans. Launched myEnterprise (Beta) for personalized trade insights and the Budget 2026 campaign explaining new business measures.

name: Equal Ventures
notes: Equal Ventures is a New York seed firm that backs founders deploying technology into legacy industries including climate, insurance, retail, and supply chain. The team draws on Carlota Perez's framework of technological revolutions to make concentrated, high-conviction bets before consensus.
thesis: Concentrates checks at seed in category-defining founders bridging the digital divide in traditional sectors, including climate and energy transition.
recent_activity: Equal published its 26 Predictions for 2026 in December 2025 and posted a Climate and Energy Investor role in early 2026 as it expanded the climate practice.

name: Equinor Ventures
notes: Equinor Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Norwegian energy company Equinor. The unit invests in technologies and business models that support sustainable energy outcomes across Europe and North America.
thesis: Backs renewables, low-carbon solutions, hydrogen, carbon capture and storage, and offshore wind technologies aligned with Equinor's transition.
recent_activity: Led CRI's $30M round for CO2-to-methanol technology. Backed Spanish solar-to-hydrogen developer Hysun with EUR 3M in equity.

name: Extantia Capital
notes: Extantia Capital is a Berlin climate fund targeting gigaton-scale decarbonization in industry and energy. The firm backs companies re-engineering industries for faster, cheaper, lower-carbon production.
thesis: Invests in pioneers reducing industrial CO2 at scale, with focus on energy transition, clean industrial technology, and structural decarbonization finance.
recent_activity: Invested in Bees & Bears (energy transition financing infrastructure) in October 2025.

name: ExxonMobil Ventures
notes: ExxonMobil Ventures is the corporate venture arm operating within ExxonMobil's Low Carbon Solutions business. The unit invests in carbon capture, hydrogen, biofuels, and lithium, supporting roughly $20B of lower-emission corporate investment between 2025 and 2030.
thesis: Backs growth-stage energy transition companies aligned with ExxonMobil's Low Carbon Solutions platform across carbon capture, hydrogen, lithium, and low-carbon data center power.
recent_activity: Carbon capture customer contracts now total roughly 9 million metric tons CO2 per year. Additional CCS projects with Linde, Nucor, and NG3 start up in 2026.

name: FAS / FROs (Focused Research Organizations)
notes: Convergent Research is a Boston-based organization that identifies scientific bottlenecks and launches nonprofit Focused Research Organizations (FROs) to build foundational capabilities. FROs run as 5-7 year, $10M-$50M+ initiatives that fit neither academia nor venture capital.
thesis: Funds industrial-scale science where mission-control teams build essential infrastructure that the rest of the research community can build on.
recent_activity: Released the Fundamental Development Gap Map v1.0 in April 2025 and partnered with the UK's ARIA in October 2025 to launch FROs in the UK.

name: FINEP
notes: Brazilian Innovation Agency (Financiadora de Estudos e Projetos), a public company under the Ministry of Science, Technology, and Innovation. Funds science and technology projects through non-reimbursable grants, subsidized loans, and equity investments.
thesis: Funds Brazilian science and technology projects across deep tech, climate, biotech, and industrial innovation.
recent_activity: Announced expanded reimbursable financing programs aligned with the New Industry Brazil plan, including dedicated envelopes for green hydrogen and bioeconomy through 2025-2026.

name: FORGE
hq: Boston, MA (plus Pioneer Valley and Merrimack Valley)
award: Manufacturing Readiness Workshops, supplier matchmaking, product development grants
notes: Boston-based 501(c)(3) connecting physical-product startups to New England manufacturers, design firms, and engineers. Runs Manufacturing Readiness Workshops, supplier matchmaking, and product development grants from sites in Somerville, Springfield, Lowell, Beverly, and New Haven.
thesis: Connects hardware founders to regional New England manufacturing capacity through advisory, matchmaking, and grants.
recent_activity: Hosting "The Demo Is Not the Product: What Robotics Companies Learn Too Late" at Winthrop Center on May 27, 2026 alongside ongoing Manufacturing Readiness Workshops.

name: Ferrovial Ventures
notes: Innovation and venturing arm of Ferrovial, the Madrid-based global infrastructure operator. Invests in smart mobility, infrastructure technology, sustainability, and digitalization aligned with Ferrovial's highways, airports, construction, and energy businesses.
thesis: Backs technology that strengthens Ferrovial's highway, airport, construction, and energy operations.
recent_activity: Received the National Construction Confederation Award in May 2026 and the CODESPA Award for a social infrastructure water access project. Continued share buybacks documented through May 2026.

name: Fifty Years
notes: Pre-seed and seed venture firm founded by Seth Bannon and Ela Madej in San Francisco. Backs technical founders working on problems aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, including biotech, climate, and frontier industrial science.
thesis: Funds mission-driven scientific founders building companies that address large global problems within a 50-year horizon.
recent_activity: Continues to anchor first checks in technical founders across synthetic biology, fusion, and industrial decarbonization through its fourth fund.

name: Fika Ventures
notes: Los Angeles seed firm investing in B2B companies across vertical AI, fintech, supply chain, manufacturing, and healthcare. Leads $2M to $8M seed rounds and partners closely with operator-founders.
thesis: Backs subject-matter expert founders building category-defining B2B software for legacy industries.
recent_activity: Announced $160M Fund IV in 2025 and published the 2026 Fintech Focus Areas memo. Hosted the 10th annual First Look Retreat in LA with 500+ attendees. SGNL was acquired by CrowdStrike for $740M.

name: First Star Ventures
notes: First Star Ventures is a Cambridge, Massachusetts virtual accelerator and fund offering direct DARPA and defense-buyer access. A single application is reviewed by three specialized defense investors, with $250K to $1M first checks for dual-use and national security hardware founders.
thesis: Backs research-driven founders at the intersection of robotics with spatial intelligence, genomics and generative AI, and AI-driven creative reasoning, with direct routes to defense customers.

name: Flagship Pioneering
notes: Flagship Pioneering is a Cambridge, MA venture-creation firm operating for 25+ years and known for founding Moderna. The firm builds biotech platforms in-house through programs like Pioneering Medicines and Pioneering Intelligence.
thesis: Invents and incubates scientific platforms internally rather than backing external founders. Best fit for scientific co-founders rather than external companies seeking capital.
recent_activity: Recent 2025-2026 partnerships include Pfizer (discovery agreements), GSK (Vesalius Parkinson's), Novo Nordisk (Valo Health cardiometabolic), Thermo Fisher (Mirai Bio), and AWS for drug discovery.

name: Forbion
notes: Forbion is a European life sciences venture firm headquartered in Naarden, Netherlands, with offices in Germany and the US and more than 50 professionals. The firm manages roughly EUR 5 billion across 11 funds spanning early venture, growth, and a dedicated bioeconomy strategy.
thesis: Backs biotech founders across human health and planetary health, from early therapeutics through growth-stage commercialization, with check sizes scaling into the EUR 30M+ range.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Dyne Therapeutics began a Phase 3 trial in May 2026, and Mestag Therapeutics dosed its first patient the same month.

name: Founder Institute
notes: Idea-stage global program with 200+ chapters across 100+ countries. Operates a structured sprint-based curriculum and a mentor network of roughly 40,000 investors and operators. Better fit for first-time founders than for established hardtech R&D ventures.
thesis: Helps pre-launch founders go from idea to fundable company through structured execution and mentorship.
recent_activity: Launching AI tools and agents for market research, customer outreach, and fundraising assistance in May 2026. Continued expansion of chapters in developing regions.

name: Founders Factory
notes: Founders Factory is a London venture studio and accelerator backed by corporate partners across fintech, health, industrials, and deep tech. The firm has supported more than 450 startups and was named the UK's top startup hub by the Financial Times in 2025.
thesis: Provides GBP 250K, a six-month studio program, and 60+ operators to founders, often in partnership with strategic corporate backers.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Monolith was acquired by CoreWeave in 2025. Active accelerator programs include Northwestern Medicine, Aviva, Rio Tinto, and HSBC.

name: Founders Fund
notes: Peter Thiel co-founded firm established in 2005. Early backer of SpaceX, Palantir, and Anduril. Aggressive on dual-use, defense, frontier, and biotech bets across seed through growth.
thesis: Backs founders building category-defining companies in defense, space, biotech, and frontier deep tech.
recent_activity: Led Impulse Space's $150M round in 2024 and participated in the Series C in June 2025. Brian Singerman departed as GP and is raising GPx with Lee Linden, with Thiel as a backer.

name: France 2030
notes: France 2030 is the €54 billion French national investment plan covering decarbonization, semiconductors, health, agriculture, defense, and space. The program funds Priority Research Programmes (PEPRs), industrial scale-up, and acceleration strategies across strategic sectors.
thesis: Deploys non-dilutive capital and matched investment across strategic French sectors including energy, mobility, aerospace, semiconductors, biotech, healthcare, and agriculture.
recent_activity: As of 2026 more than 20 PEPRs have launched, deploying roughly €10B. France's broader AI strategy includes €109B in announced infrastructure commitments alongside France 2030.

name: Frazier Life Sciences
notes: Seattle-based life sciences venture firm operating since 1991 with separate Life Sciences and Healthcare growth-buyout strategies. The Life Sciences team funds private and public biopharmaceutical companies from discovery through commercial stage.
thesis: Backs therapeutics platforms, biopharmaceutical companies, and drug-discovery teams across private and public markets.
recent_activity: Continues to invest Life Sciences Fund XII across biopharma, with sustained pace of crossover and IPO-stage commitments through 2025.

name: Future Energy Ventures
notes: Future Energy Ventures is a global energy transition fund anchored by E.ON, with offices in Germany and the US. The firm closed a EUR 235M Fund II and a $272M grid-resilience vehicle joined by additional industrial LPs.
thesis: Invests in digital, scalable, asset-light energy technologies that accelerate the transition to renewables and grid resilience.
recent_activity: Active investor with 70 portfolio companies and 4 new investments in the trailing twelve months as of February 2026.

name: Future Planet Capital
notes: Future Planet Capital is a London impact venture firm backing university-linked spinouts aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. The firm manages roughly $460M across 160 portfolio companies and reports founding investments in eight unicorns.
thesis: Invests in profitable, high-growth spinouts from top universities and research centers solving climate, health, security, and education problems.

team_sample.name: Jessica Hill
team_sample.title: Director of Investor Relations and Strategic Partnerships

name: Future Ventures
notes: A Burlingame frontier deep tech fund co-founded by Steve Jurvetson and Maryanna Saenko. Portfolio companies have generated $1.7 trillion in aggregate value across SpaceX, Tesla, Planet Labs, Commonwealth Fusion, D-Wave, Boring Company, and Upside Foods. Focuses on mission-driven founders forming entirely new industries.
thesis: $2M-$10M seed and Series A checks into nuclear fusion, agriculture, AI, climate, reproductive health, and other frontier categories.
recent_activity: Active across energy, robotics, and climate, with Nico Enriquez (former ARPA-E fellow) leading newer hardtech work.

name: GM Ventures
notes: GM Ventures is the corporate venture arm of General Motors. The team has backed 56 portfolio companies producing four unicorns, three IPOs, and 15 acquisitions including Turo, Proterra, and SES.
thesis: Concentrates on four pillars: Electrification Ecosystem, Connecting the Customer, Autonomous Experience, and Digital Enterprise, plus selected breakthrough plays.
recent_activity: Kurt Baumgarten appointed Interim Managing Director in February 2026. John Lyons named to Global Corporate Venturing's 2026 Rising Stars 50 for EV battery work.

name: Galvanize Climate Solutions
notes: Multi-strategy climate asset manager founded by Tom Steyer and Katie Hall. Operates seed, venture and growth, credit, real estate, and global equities strategies. Focuses on AI, electrification, and the industrial reinvestment cycle driving capital-intensive infrastructure.
thesis: Backs companies and assets capturing the structural shift toward firm power, electrification, and resilient infrastructure.
recent_activity: Tom Steyer stepped back from day-to-day in 2025 to run for governor of California. Firm continues publishing research on firm power and climate-language precision.

name: General Catalyst
notes: Multi-stage venture firm headquartered in Cambridge with $51B+ in assets under management. Operates Create, Seed, and Grow strategies across AI, defense, health assurance, and industrial resilience. Backed Anduril, Helion, Commure, and Stripe.
thesis: Builds applied AI and resilience companies through a multi-stage platform that pairs hatched companies with growth capital.
recent_activity: Committed $5B to India over five years, hit $51B in AUM by Q1 2026, and placed 11 portfolio companies on the Forbes AI 50 2026 list. Customer Value Fund achieved investment-grade status.

name: Generate Capital
notes: A North American sustainable infrastructure investment platform that funds and operates climate infrastructure assets. Combines project development, multi-technology operations, and flexible financing across distributed energy, data centers, energy efficiency, and specialty infrastructure including waste-to-value and vertical farms.
thesis: Project equity and corporate equity ($10M-$200M) for deployment-ready climate infrastructure, not lab-stage R&D.
recent_activity: Announced a Vertiv data center partnership and a $100M credit facility to Soluna in 2025-2026, plus a $1B credit raise in November 2025.

name: Generation Investment Management
notes: Generation Investment Management is a London-based sustainable investing manager founded in 2004 by Al Gore and David Blood. The firm runs growth equity, public equity, and the Just Climate strategy, integrating sustainability research into every investment decision.
thesis: Operates as a long-horizon sustainable investor, building concentrated positions in high-quality companies where sustainability integration drives compounding returns.

team_sample.name: Lila Preston
team_sample.title: Partner and co-Head of Growth Equity

recent_activity: Generation published its Sustainability Trends Report 2025 and the Senior Partner Letter 2026, reaffirming its sustainability mandate despite market headwinds.

name: Golden Seeds
award: One of the largest women-focused angel groups in the US; nine chapters
notes: Backs women-led startups with public application portal. Active in medical devices, biotech, and AI. Recent portfolio includes Avisi Technologies and Osteoboost (medical devices). Nine chapters and members in 28 states plus DC.

name: Greentown Go
award: Corporate-partnered open innovation cohort with pilot opportunities
notes: Greentown Go is the open-innovation cohort program inside Greentown Labs, the Somerville and Houston climatetech incubator. The program runs five tracks: Go Build for buildings, Go Energize for energy, Go Grow for food and ag, Go Make for manufacturing, and Go Move for transport.
thesis: Pairs climatetech founders with corporate partners for short, pilot-focused cohorts, prioritizing teams that can land paid commercial agreements during the program.
recent_activity: Go Build 2026 runs with Amazon and the Global CO2 Initiative, while Go Make 2026 runs with Shell and Technip Energies, alongside the Climatetech Summit 2026.

name: Greentown Labs
notes: Greentown Labs is a 501(c)(3) climatetech incubator with locations in Somerville, MA and Houston, TX. It rents bench, fume hood, and prototyping space to early-stage climate hardware companies, paired with corporate-partner programs and community events.
thesis: Provides physical infrastructure and corporate access for hardware-heavy climate startups across agriculture, buildings, electricity, manufacturing, resilience, and transportation.
recent_activity: Membership applications are open and the Climatetech Summit 2026 is on the calendar. Programs include Go Build, Go Energize, Go Grow, Go Make, Go Move, First Valley Fellowship, and the Carbon to Value Initiative.

name: HAX (SOSV)
notes: Pre-seed hardtech program within SOSV, headquartered in Newark, NJ after relocating from Shenzhen. Backs founders at the intersection of science and engineering with checks plus an embedded engineering team. Portfolio spans 440 startups with $11B in aggregate value.
thesis: Targets industrial-scale problems in energy, compute, transportation, health, and manufacturing.
recent_activity: Hiring a site lead for the HAX Plasma Forge and publishing on plasma processes for next-generation semiconductor manufacturing.

name: HBS Alumni Angels
notes: Twenty chapters worldwide. Open to founders without any Harvard affiliation: applications go through local chapters. Active in medtech and broader hardtech, including portfolio companies like Actuated Medical.

name: HCVC (Hardware Club)
hq: Paris, France and San Francisco, CA
award: Early-stage checks plus access to the 500+ member Hardware Club community
notes: HCVC is a Paris and San Francisco hardtech venture firm founded in 2015 by Alexis Houssou. The team backs founders industrializing scientific and technological progress across robotics, AI, computer vision, healthtech, aerospace, energy, mobility, and infrastructure.
thesis: Backs pre-seed and seed hardtech founders globally, pairing early capital with the 500-plus member Hardware Club community for sourcing, hiring, and customer development.
recent_activity: HCVC backed Dyna Robotics' $120 million Series A, Chef's $43M Series A, and Renaissance Fusion's EUR 32 million round, plus seed investments into Chiral, Navier, and Alpine Eagle.

name: Halcyon
award: Up to $100K plus hybrid residency in DC and LA
notes: Halcyon is a Washington DC and Los Angeles accelerator founded in 2014 that supports impact-driven founders in climate, health, and equity tech. The program provides up to $100K plus hybrid residency and runs global, regional, and Caribbean climate fellowships.
thesis: Provides equity-light fellowships, residency, and up to $100K to early-stage social entrepreneurs solving problems in climate, health, and equity tech.
recent_activity: Global Climate Fellowship 2026 supports international founders working on climate adaptation and resilience. DMV Climate Innovation Fellowship offers up to $100K.

name: Halcyon Incubator
notes: Halcyon is a Washington, DC impact accelerator operating since 2014. It supports founders across climate, health, and EquityTech with residency, community, and equity-free funding.
thesis: Backs impact founders advancing equity and inclusivity across climate resilience, health access, and economic mobility.
recent_activity: Maintains a global community of 650+ impact-driven entrepreneurs and runs an Expert Network plus annual cohorts.

name: HardClimate
award: Co-founder studio model plus early-stage capital
notes: Paris-based venture studio that launches and de-risks industrial climate companies. Spins out technology from R&D labs, embeds operationally as co-founders, and invests for commercial maturation in energy storage, CO2 conversion, and resource optimization.
thesis: Spins hard industrial climate technologies out of European R&D labs and operates them as co-founder for measurable decarbonization.
recent_activity: FeX Energy closed a USD $35M seed round in September 2025. HardClimate continues to source new ventures from McGill, Universite de Sherbrooke, and other research institutions.

name: Harlem Capital
award: 70+ investments across 10+ cities; mission to back 1,000 diverse founders over 20 years
notes: NYC-based VC backing diverse founders. Public Pitch intake at harlem.capital/pitch. Sector-agnostic but increasingly active in hardware, climate, and medtech rounds.

name: Harpoon Ventures
notes: Harpoon Ventures is a San Diego firm founded in 2018 by a former Navy SEAL and two-time Olympic medalist. The firm is anchored by major US pensions and endowments and has helped portfolio companies win over $1B in US government contracts.
thesis: Backs founders building in AI, deep tech, software infrastructure, and cybersecurity to restore American technological leadership and strategic advantage.
recent_activity: Portfolio raised significant capital in 2026: Astranis closed $450M in equity and debt; n8n hit $5.2B valuation with SAP; Firestorm Labs raised $82M; Aalo Atomics received DOE approval for its 10 MW reactor.

name: Heartcore Capital
notes: Heartcore Capital is a European venture firm founded in 2007 with offices in Copenhagen, Stockholm, Berlin, and Paris and EUR 800 million in cumulative committed capital. The team backs European founders at inception through Series A across consumer, climate, infrastructure, and synthetic biology.
thesis: Closed Fund V at EUR 170 million in December 2024 with an expanded mandate covering infrastructure, synthetic biology, and climate tech alongside the historical consumer focus.
recent_activity: Heartcore made five new investments in 2025 as it began deploying Fund V into climate and synthetic biology founders alongside its consumer book.

name: Heidelberg Materials Sustainability Ventures
notes: Corporate venture and innovation arm of Heidelberg Materials, one of the largest global producers of cement and aggregates. Invests in technologies for cement decarbonization, carbon capture and storage, alternative binders, and circular construction.
thesis: Backs technologies that decarbonize cement and concrete production and capture CO2 across the building materials value chain.

team_sample.name: Jan Theulen
team_sample.title: Director, Alternative Resources and Sustainability Ventures

recent_activity: Brevik CCS plant in Norway entered operations in 2025 as the world's first industrial-scale CCS cement facility. Continues to back early-stage CCU and low-carbon cement startups.

name: Hello Tomorrow Global Challenge
notes: A Paris-based deep tech organization running an annual global pitch competition, an industry summit, innovation consulting for corporations, and partnerships with 500+ universities and accelerators. Has 72 representatives across 30 countries.
thesis: Showcase, prizes (up to €100K), and corporate introductions for deep tech founders across health, planet, and frontier sciences.
recent_activity: Currently running the L'Oréal Green Sciences Incubator Startup Call, ASML Emerging Innovator Award, Deep Scale Climate Tech Accelerator, and ESA Φ-Lab Grand Marathon 2025.

name: High-Tech Gruenderfonds
notes: High-Tech Gruenderfonds (HTGF) is Germany's largest seed investor, structured as a public-private partnership headquartered in Bonn. The fund backs technology founders across industrial and deep tech, life sciences and chemistry, and digital tech with checks of EUR 600K to EUR 4 million.
thesis: Backs pre-seed and seed stage German technology founders, then leverages a deep corporate LP base for follow-on capital and customer access.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Tubulis was acquired by Gilead for up to $5 billion in April 2026, EGYM merged with Playlist on $785 million in new capital, and ATMOS Space Cargo raised a EUR 25.7 million Series A.

name: Highway1
notes: Hardware accelerator operated by PCH International in San Francisco. Supports physical product startups with engineering, design, and manufacturing expertise to move from prototype to factory floor. Over 100 alumni companies to date.
thesis: Helps hardware startups cross the gap from prototype to manufacturing through PCH's global supply chain network.
recent_activity: Continues to run cohort-based engagements pairing hardware founders with PCH's manufacturing engineering teams.

name: Hitachi Ventures
notes: Munich-based corporate VC connected to Hitachi. Over $1B in AUM across four funds and 49 portfolio companies on four continents. Focus areas span digital and AI infrastructure, industry 4.0, decarbonization, and tech-bio.
thesis: Backs founders building category-defining digital, industrial, environmental, and healthcare companies.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Teramount acquired by Molex (April 2026). Co-hosted San Francisco Climate Week event (May 2026). Published the AINomics research series.

name: Holcim MAQER Ventures
notes: Holcim MAQER Ventures is the corporate venture arm of cement major Holcim, based in Zug, Switzerland. The team operates three channels at once: venture capital, venture clienting, and an accelerator program for construction-tech founders.
thesis: Backs startups that decarbonize cement, advance circular materials, and digitize the construction process, often pairing investment with pilot deployments inside Holcim plants.
recent_activity: Holcim invested alongside Amazon in carbon mineralization startup Paebbl in October 2024, backed Sublime Systems in September 2024, and continues to fund electric and 3D-printed cement startups through 2026.

name: Honeywell Ventures
notes: Honeywell Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Honeywell, launched in 2017 with an initial $100M fund. It invests in companies that support Honeywell's primary businesses: Aerospace Technologies, Industrial Automation, Energy and Sustainability Solutions, Building Automation, and Honeywell Connected Enterprise.
thesis: Backs early and growth-stage deeptech across IoT, software, life sciences, digital health analytics, material science, and advanced manufacturing aligned to Honeywell business units.
recent_activity: Has made 70+ investments to date, helping participated in Quantinuum's $840M capital raise at a $10B pre-money valuation.

name: Horizon Europe (Cluster 4/5/6: Digital, Climate, Bioeconomy)
notes: EU's flagship research and innovation framework with a EUR 93.5B budget across 2021-2027. Organized into three pillars: Excellent Science, Global Challenges and Industrial Competitiveness (six clusters including Digital/Industry/Space, Climate/Energy/Mobility, and Food/Bioeconomy), and Innovative Europe.
thesis: Funds consortia of European researchers and companies addressing climate, health, security, and digital competitiveness.
recent_activity: European Commission proposed the next framework program for 2028-2034 at EUR 175B, with moonshot projects in clean aviation, space economy, and next-generation AI.

name: Howard Hughes Medical Institute
notes: Howard Hughes Medical Institute is a major US biomedical research philanthropy based in Chevy Chase, Maryland. The Institute funds investigators on long-duration awards and runs the Janelia Research Campus.
thesis: Funds people, not projects, supporting biomedical researchers pursuing fundamental questions with multi-year, renewable awards.
recent_activity: Opened a special 2026 Investigator competition restricted to institutions with one or fewer current HHMI Investigators, part of the Institute's $2B diversity initiative.

name: Hoxton Ventures
notes: Hoxton Ventures is a London-based European seed firm with a Silicon Valley operating model. The firm ranks in the top ten of the Founders Choice VC leaderboard and is one of five firms NVIDIA selected to work with in Europe.
thesis: Takes anti-thematic bets on founders building in non-obvious categories, then pushes them to tackle the US market early.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Avantia Law acquired by Carta. CuspAI raised $100M. Selected by NVIDIA to help accelerate the UK AI startup base.

name: Hustle Fund Angel Squad
award: SPV-based investments starting at $1K per deal
notes: Community-syndicate hybrid run by Hustle Fund with 2,000+ angels across 40+ countries. Members receive SPV access to deals Hustle Fund has already diligenced, with no syndicate lead carry. Starting check size is $1,000 per deal.
thesis: Opens diligenced Hustle Fund deal flow to a global angel community with no syndicate lead carry.
recent_activity: Continued to run networking events across 15+ cities including San Francisco, New York, Miami, Singapore, and Toronto with a 14-day complimentary trial for new members.

name: Hyundai CRADLE
notes: Hyundai CRADLE is the open innovation and corporate venture unit of Hyundai Motor Group. The team operates from Seoul, Silicon Valley, Berlin, Tel Aviv, Shanghai, and Singapore and invests across mobility, robotics, energy, and AI.
thesis: Sources, invests in, and pilots startup technology that advances Hyundai Motor Group's mobility, robotics, and energy roadmap.

name: IARPA
notes: IARPA is the Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity within the Office of the Director of National Intelligence. The agency funds high-risk research benefiting the US intelligence community across AI, quantum, machine learning, and synthetic biology.
thesis: Funds high-risk, high-payoff non-dilutive research across AI, quantum computing, machine learning, and synthetic biology to fill US intelligence community capability gaps.
recent_activity: In 2026 IARPA launched five Emerging Technology Accelerator programs: ARCADE, COSMIC, DECIPHER, LOCUS, and MOVES, focused on AI-driven intelligence capabilities.

name: IP Group
notes: IP Group is a publicly listed UK firm that commercializes university science. The company invests for the long term across HealthTech, DeepTech, and CleanTech, building spinouts from Oxford, Cambridge, and other research institutions.
thesis: Backs differentiated, defensible science with patient capital, holding positions through public listings and strategic exits across life sciences, deep tech, and climate.
recent_activity: IP Group launched the Climate Catalyst Fund with Australia's Clean Energy Finance Corporation targeting A$150 million, and First Light Fusion secured a GBP 25 million round in April 2026.

name: In-Q-Tel
notes: A not-for-profit strategic investor that accelerates technology adoption for the US Intelligence Community and allied agencies. Operating for over 25 years with 50+ portfolio companies now valued above $1B. Pairs investment with a pilot deployment inside CIA, NSA, NGA, DIA, FBI, NRO, DHS, Cyber Command, Space Force, or partner agencies in the UK and Australia.
thesis: Strategic investment plus an embedded pilot contract with an intelligence or defense customer.
recent_activity: Expanded partnerships with the US Army, Office of Strategic Intelligence and Analysis, and the National Nuclear Security Administration.

name: Inflection
notes: AI infrastructure venture firm with offices in New York and Los Angeles. Invests in defense, advanced energy, frontier computing, and enterprise data platforms. Portfolio includes xAI, CoreWeave, Palantir, Anduril, and SpaceX.
thesis: Backs the infrastructure layer of the new industrial era: AI compute, energy, defense, and enterprise data platforms.
recent_activity: Continues to commit to large frontier AI and defense rounds through 2026 with portfolio companies xAI, Anduril, and CoreWeave at the center of major financings.

name: Initialized Capital
award: Seed-stage checks in the multi-million-dollar range; portfolio includes 28+ unicorns
notes: Operator-led seed fund with offices in San Francisco and New York. Explicit hard-tech, infrastructure, and sustainability mandate alongside software. Recent investments in seed rounds between $4.8M and $7.1M. Founder outreach through direct contact channels.

name: Initiate Studios
notes: Initiate Studios is a Pittsburgh-based venture studio and seed fund tied to Carnegie Mellon University and the broader Pittsburgh robotics base. The firm backs industrial AI and robotics startups at pre-seed and seed.
thesis: Concentrates on company creation and seed checks for industrial AI, automation, and robotics founders coming out of CMU and Pittsburgh.

name: Innosuisse
notes: Innosuisse is the Swiss Confederation's federal innovation agency, funding collaborative R&D between startups and research institutions. The agency offers project grants, BRIDGE Proof of Concept and Discovery awards, plus startup coaching for Swiss-based founders.
recent_activity: Innosuisse continued operating its BRIDGE program with the Swiss National Science Foundation and supporting Swiss deep tech founders through 2025 and 2026.

name: Innovate UK Smart Grants
award: Historical: £100K-£2M project size; up to £25M consortia
deadline: Manual verification required : site unreachable during automated check
notes: Innovate UK's flagship rolling competition for UK businesses developing game-changing innovation. Funded through UK Research and Innovation. Awards typically range from £25K to £500K for single applicants or collaborations across any technology area.
thesis: Open, technology-agnostic non-dilutive grants for ambitious UK R&D with strong commercial potential.
recent_activity: Innovate UK Business Connect (formerly KTN) is the primary delivery and signposting partner. Multiple active calls including DeepTech Catalyst Bio 2026 and Contracts for Innovation.

past_awardees.name: Levidian Nanosystems
past_awardees.description: Cracks methane into hydrogen and high-grade graphene using a microwave plasma process.

past_awardees.name: Mission Zero Technologies
past_awardees.description: Captures CO2 from the air using an electrochemical process that runs at room temperature.

past_awardees.name: Wayve
past_awardees.description: Builds end-to-end AI driving software for cars that learns from camera and sensor data.

past_awardees.name: Oxford Quantum Circuits
past_awardees.description: Builds superconducting quantum computers and runs them as cloud services for enterprise customers.

past_awardees.name: Tokamak Energy
past_awardees.description: Builds compact spherical tokamak fusion reactors and high-temperature superconducting magnets.

past_awardees.name: Nyobolt
past_awardees.description: Builds fast-charging lithium-ion batteries using niobium-based anode materials.

past_awardees.name: Carbon Re
past_awardees.description: Builds AI software that helps cement and steel plants cut fuel use and carbon emissions in real time.

name: Innovation Crossroads (Oak Ridge National Lab)
notes: DOE-backed two-year fellowship at Oak Ridge National Laboratory for hardtech founders in energy and advanced manufacturing. Fellows get a personal stipend, health and travel allowances, and a sizable grant for collaborative R&D at ORNL. Program-wide outcomes include 51 startups and $326M+ in follow-on funding.
thesis: Pairs founder-scientists with ORNL facilities and staff to commercialize energy and advanced manufacturing IP.
recent_activity: Cohort 2025 welcomed six entrepreneurs spanning hexagonal boron nitride synthesis, low-solvent carbon fiber, and wildfire-hardened power lines. LEEP Demo Day in 2026 hosted 23 founders in Knoxville.

name: Innovation Endeavors
notes: Innovation Endeavors is Eric Schmidt's Palo Alto frontier tech firm. The fund invests around the convergence of sensors, compute, and engineering across intelligent software, computing infrastructure, the physical economy, and engineered health.
thesis: Backs founders riding the convergence of ubiquitous sensors, machine learning, and engineering breakthroughs (robotics, 3D printing, CRISPR) into industries that move atoms.
recent_activity: Recent investment in Cadstrom, AI-enabled tooling for electrical engineers.

name: Innovation Industries
notes: Innovation Industries is a Dutch deep tech VC based in Eindhoven, spun out of TNO and other Dutch research institutes. The firm is active in semiconductors, photonics, medtech, robotics, and quantum.
thesis: Backs European deep tech companies with sustainable competitive advantages and measurable environmental, social, and economic impact across hardware-heavy sectors.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Axelera AI raised more than $250M to scale AI inference compute. Groove Quantum raised €16M for spin-qubit semiconductor processors.

name: Innovation Norway
notes: Innovation Norway is the Norwegian government's enterprise development agency. The agency backs Norwegian companies with grants, loans, and guarantees, with a phased Start-Up Grant program and a strong ocean and energy emphasis.
thesis: Funds Norwegian companies whose growth depends on innovation, with the Green Platform supporting carbon capture, renewables, ocean tech, and sustainable solutions.
recent_activity: Continues running phased Start-Up Grants (Phase 1 up to NOK 150K, Phase 2 up to NOK 1M) and Green Platform calls for ocean and energy technology.

name: Innovation Works (Pittsburgh)
award: Seed-stage investment plus accelerator portfolio support
notes: Innovation Works is Pittsburgh's most active early-stage investor, operating since 1988. The organization has supported 780+ companies and invested $93M across 372 seed-stage companies since 1999, and runs AlphaLab, AlphaLab Gear, AlphaLab Health, and Robotics Factory.
thesis: Backs early-stage hardware, software, robotics, and life sciences founders in Southwestern Pennsylvania through a combination of seed checks, accelerator programs, and manufacturing assistance.
recent_activity: AlphaLab opened applications for the 2027 cohort in April 2026. Portfolio companies have raised $3.54B in follow-on funding with 77 exits to date.

name: Israel Innovation Authority
notes: Statutory independent agency that plans and executes Israel's innovation policy. Runs the R&D Fund (the country's largest financial R&D incentive), international R&D collaboration programs, deep tech matched funding, and incubator programs. Covers hardware, software, life sciences, cyber, IoT, fintech, cleantech, and more.
thesis: Funds Israeli companies' R&D with conditional grants and bridges financing gaps in deep tech.
recent_activity: 2025-2026 International R&D and Pilot Collaborations call open. Startup for High-Tech initiative supporting new branches in priority Israeli regions.

name: JERA Ventures
notes: Corporate venture arm of JERA, Japan's largest power generation company and a joint venture of TEPCO Fuel & Power and Chubu Electric. Invests in hydrogen, ammonia co-firing, renewables, and grid technologies aligned with JERA's 2050 net zero plan.
thesis: Backs energy-transition technologies that support JERA's path to zero-emission thermal power and renewables.
recent_activity: Continued to commit to ammonia supply chain projects and battery storage platforms through 2025-2026.

name: Just Climate
notes: Just Climate is a London-based investment firm established by Generation Investment Management. The team backs climate solutions in hard-to-abate sectors with infrastructure-scale checks, focused on transformational rather than incremental decarbonization.
thesis: Backs low or no carbon solutions that can deliver outsized emissions reductions this decade, deploying growth equity into power, food, and industrial systems.
recent_activity: Just Climate invested in Afresh's $34 million round in April 2026, backed Zanskar's $40 million development capital facility, and led Tropic's $105 million Series C in March 2026.

name: KKR Global Impact
notes: KKR Global Impact is the impact platform within KKR. The strategy invests in mid-market climate action, learning, and lifelong wellness companies globally and runs a separate $3B-plus Global Climate Transition Fund.
thesis: Backs scalable mid-market businesses delivering measurable outcomes across climate action, education and workforce, and lifelong wellness, with growth-stage check sizes.

team_sample.name: Ken Mehlman
team_sample.title: Global Head of Public Affairs and Co-Head of KKR Global Impact

recent_activity: Global Climate Transition Fund began its investment period in July 2024 with $3.053B in total commitments running through July 2030.

name: Keiretsu Forum
notes: Global angel investor network founded in 2000 by Randy Williams with 3,000+ members across 60 chapters in 26 countries. Members have invested over $1B in 950+ companies across life sciences, biotech, and high-growth hardware.
thesis: Pools accredited angel investors across 60 chapters into coordinated deal-flow and due-diligence on early-stage rounds.
recent_activity: Ranked #1 most active global angel investor group by Pitchbook for the sixth straight year. Continues to expand chapter network and syndicate with Keiretsu Capital.

name: KfW Capital
notes: Subsidiary of German state development bank KfW that invests in European venture capital funds. Acts as a cornerstone limited partner in deep tech, climate, and life science funds across Germany and the EU as part of the German Future Fund.
thesis: Anchors European VC funds backing deep tech, climate, and biotech to keep capital flowing into European innovation.
recent_activity: Committed additional capital to climate-focused funds under the German Future Fund's deep tech and climate envelope through 2025-2026.

name: Khazanah Nasional
notes: Khazanah Nasional is Malaysia's $26B sovereign wealth fund. The fund delivered +5.2% in 2025 and a +6.1% annualized return over seven years, allocating across domestic public markets (50.7%), global public markets, private markets, and real assets.
thesis: Prioritizes energy transition, digitalization, and connectivity in 2026 while strengthening portfolio resilience through diversification and risk management.
recent_activity: Increased its EDOTCO stake from 10.6% to 31.7% in March 2026, completed the XL Axiata and Smartfren merger at roughly $6.5B in April, and finalized the RM18.4B privatization of Malaysia Airports in February.

name: Khosla Ventures
notes: Vinod Khosla's Menlo Park venture firm, known for early bets on frontier deep tech and clean technology. Practices what they call venture assistance and value 'brutal honesty over hypocritical politeness.' Portfolio spans AI, clean tech, hard science, healthcare, enterprise software, and financial infrastructure.
thesis: Bold, early, and contrarian bets across seed through growth ($1M-$50M+) in AI, fusion, materials, agriculture, and healthcare.
recent_activity: Continues to lead rounds in OpenAI, Commonwealth Fusion, Waabi, and other portfolio companies.

name: Kindred Capital
notes: Kindred Capital is a London seed firm with an equitable carry model that makes every funded founder a co-owner of the fund. The team is partner-only and active in climate, robotics, and infrastructure technology.
thesis: Backs pre-seed and seed mission-driven founders in the UK and Europe, sharing carry with portfolio founders to align long-term incentives.
recent_activity: Launched Fund III. Portfolio company RobCo raised $43M led by Lightspeed; Cradle raised $24M Series A led by Index Ventures.

name: Komatsu Ventures
notes: Komatsu is a Tokyo industrial OEM founded in 1921 making construction and mining equipment. Its corporate investing activity covers AI and robotics, with portfolio names including IronPlanet and Skycatch and typical deal sizes of $10M-$50M.
thesis: Strategic equity in automation, remote operation, and zero-emission construction and mining technologies.
recent_activity: Continues to focus on automation, remote operation, and zero-emission heavy equipment. Komatsu's main website does not publicly profile its venture team.

name: LG Technology Ventures
notes: LG Technology Ventures is the Silicon Valley venture arm of LG, founded in 2018 with nearly $1 billion under management. The team has built a portfolio of 80-plus active investments and ten exits or IPOs across AI, biotech, cleantech, and devices.
thesis: Backs early and growth stage founders whose technology aligns with LG affiliates in AI, mobility, materials, and digital health, with the global LG Group network as a strategic partner.

name: LabCentral
award: Lab bench rates with 25% reduction for new members; LabCentral First offers 6 months free
notes: LabCentral is a Cambridge, Massachusetts nonprofit biotech incubator in Kendall Square hosting roughly 100 resident teams. LabCentral First offers 18 first-time biotech founders six months of free lab space, and the AI BioHub Accelerator runs with C10 Labs and MassTech.
thesis: Provides shared wet lab space, equipment, and programming to early-stage biotech founders at a 25% reduced bench rate, with sponsored Golden Ticket residencies through corporate partners.
recent_activity: 2025 reported 30 new companies admitted and 737 jobs created. Sanofi and Ono 2026 Golden Ticket applications closed in May 2026.

name: Lakestar
notes: Lakestar is a Zurich-based European venture firm investing from seed through growth in deep tech, healthcare, fintech, and digitalisation. The team backs visionary founders across the continent with offices in Zurich, Berlin, London, and Stockholm.
thesis: Backs frontier technology founders building defensible engineering moats, from Cambridge AI spinouts to European aerospace and defense.
recent_activity: Lakestar led a $14 million seed round for Cambridge AI spinout Matta in December 2025 and co-launched the 2026 European Deep Tech Report with Walden Catalyst and Dealroom.

name: Launch NY
award: Quarterly seed deployments measured in the millions; ECO Incubator adds cleantech-specific support
notes: Most active seed fund in Upstate New York, with 110+ portfolio companies. Public Apply Now intake. Runs the ECO Incubator for cleantech founders and the Founders Go Big mentorship program. Received a $250K JPMorganChase grant in May 2026.

name: LaunchCapital
award: Long-running early-stage fund; sector-agnostic but writes selectively into hardtech
notes: Offices in Boston, New Haven, and New York. Open Get In Touch intake. Sector-agnostic with consistent activity in medtech, materials, and consumer hardware.

name: Leaps by Bayer
notes: Leaps by Bayer is Bayer's strategic impact investment unit, founded in 2015. The team has invested over $1.9B in more than 60 ventures targeting ten defined leaps in health and agriculture.
thesis: Targets breakthrough biotech across cell and gene therapy, regenerative medicine, sustainable agriculture, and digital health.

team_sample.name: Juergen Eckhardt
team_sample.title: Executive Vice President, Head of Leaps by Bayer

name: Lemnos
award: Up to $250K (typically $100K for 10%) plus hands-on engineering support
notes: Hardware-focused pre-seed firm founded in 2011 by MIT graduates Jeremy Conrad and Helen Zelman Boniske. Writes up to $250K typically for 10 percent, embeds with engineering support, and focuses on capital-efficient hardware scaling.
thesis: Backs the earliest hardware founders with engineering-first discipline across prototyping, manufacturing planning, and capital efficiency.
recent_activity: Continues to lead hardware pre-seed rounds in robotics, geothermal, and imaging through 2025-2026.

name: Lightrock
notes: London-headquartered global impact growth platform backed by the LGT Group. Invests in growth-stage companies across People, Planet, and Productivity themes including decarbonization, accessible healthcare, sustainable food, and financial inclusion.
thesis: Backs growth-stage technology companies that produce measurable impact across decarbonization, healthcare, and inclusion.
recent_activity: Launched Accelerate7 fund following a $500M final close in May 2026. Portfolio companies recognized by TIME100 and the Financial Times in April 2026. Vinted secondary at EUR 8B valuation.

name: Lightsmith Group
notes: The Lightsmith Group is a New York climate resilience growth equity firm. The team closed the first dedicated adaptation and resilience fund at $186M and focuses on infrastructure and energy resilience and supply chain resilience.
thesis: Backs growth-stage companies whose products help societies and supply chains adapt to unavoidable climate impacts, with a focus on infrastructure resilience and supply chain risk.
recent_activity: Led a $20M round in supply chain visibility company Tive in January 2026.

name: Lightspeed Defense
notes: Lightspeed Defense is the defense and dual-use practice inside Lightspeed Venture Partners. The firm has invested roughly $915 million across nearly 50 defense-related companies, with the practice anchored by an early seed in Anduril.
thesis: Backs autonomy, AI, and aerospace founders building dual-use capability, with check sizes scaling from seed into growth as defense programs mature.
recent_activity: Lightspeed participated in Helsing's EUR 450 million round and Nominal's $75 million Series B, and closed over $9 billion in new funds in December 2025.

name: LocalGlobe
notes: LocalGlobe is a London seed firm within the Phoenix Court family of funds, which also includes Latitude and Solar. The team backs European founders from pre-seed through seed across enterprise, fintech, frontier tech, climate, and health.
thesis: Operates as a long-term partner from pre-seed onward, with thematic exposure to climate and deep tech through the wider Phoenix Court platform.
recent_activity: Recent coverage included portfolio company Empathy's Series B and Spore.Bio's pre-seed round, with Phoenix Court continuing to expand its multi-fund platform.

name: Lockheed Martin Ventures
award: $1B fund as of April 2026 (expanded from prior $400M)
notes: Lockheed Martin Ventures is the defense contractor's strategic investment arm, founded in 2007 and based in Bethesda, MD. It operates as an evergreen fund with a $1B capacity as of April 2026, up from $400M.
thesis: Backs deep-tech companies that advance national security capabilities and align with Lockheed's mission areas across autonomy, AI, sensors, quantum, materials, and power.
recent_activity: Announced a $1B fund expansion in April 2026, the largest increase since 2007. Completed Terran Orbital acquisition in October 2024.
correction_note: Lockheed expanded its venture arm to $1B in April 2026, signalling continued strategic investment in defense, aerospace, and dual-use technologies.

name: Lowercarbon Capital
notes: Lowercarbon Capital is a San Francisco climate fund founded by Chris and Crystal Sacca. It backs companies that cut new CO2, remove existing carbon, or buy more time through adaptation and planetary cooling.
thesis: Aggressive on fusion, carbon removal, geoengineering, agriculture, and industrial materials. Three buckets: slashing emissions, sucking up carbon, buying time.
recent_activity: Maintains an open hiring board across portfolio companies and continues to lead rounds across fusion, thermal storage, and carbon removal.

name: Lowercarbon Nuclear Fund
notes: Dedicated fusion and advanced nuclear vehicle inside Chris Sacca's Lowercarbon Capital. Funds development-stage fusion and fission companies with capital structured to support long technical timelines. Domain migrated from lowercarboncapital.com to lowercarbon.com.
thesis: Backs fusion and advanced fission companies that can deliver firm carbon-free power at industrial scale.
recent_activity: Continues to anchor megaround financings in fusion and thermal storage. Commonwealth Fusion advanced SPARC commissioning while Pacific Fusion progressed pulser milestones through 2025.

name: Lux Capital
notes: Lux Capital is a New York deep tech firm investing across biotech, defense, robotics, semiconductors, and AI. Their stated approach is to turn sci-fi into sci-fact by backing contrarian bets decades out.
thesis: Backs scientists and engineers building at the edge of what is possible, with a multi-decade view.
recent_activity: Recruiting for a 2026 student fellowship and continues to publish the Riskgaming newsletter and Securities podcast.

name: MCJ
award: Operates both MCJ funds and the MCJ Collective community; collective fees apply for member access
notes: Climate-focused investor that grew out of My Climate Journey. Public Apply to Join intake for the Collective and a separate process for the fund. Heavy activity in energy, nuclear, energy storage, and industrial tech. Produces Inevitable Podcast and a newsletter.

name: MCJ Collective
notes: Distributed climate-focused early-stage fund tied to the My Climate Journey podcast and community. Backs energy, nuclear, storage, industrial AI, and adjacent climate tech. Network includes a deep angel and operator pool.
thesis: Backs founders rewriting energy, industrial, and AI infrastructure with a climate angle.
recent_activity: Made 21 investments in 2025 and facilitated 100+ hires through the talent network. Inevitable Podcast continues weekly.

name: MIT The Engine
notes: The Engine is a nonprofit tough-tech incubator and venture firm spun out of MIT, based in Cambridge, MA. It runs 227,000 square feet of lab and engineering space alongside Blueprint, Whiteboard, and partner programs for science-heavy founders.
thesis: Backs companies addressing global challenges through hard science and engineering with long time horizons and shared infrastructure.
recent_activity: Operates 150+ resident companies across 19 industries. Blueprint has graduated 735+ companies raising over $475M. Integrates with the NSF Builder Platform across nine regional innovation engines.

name: MUFG Innovation Partners
notes: MUFG Innovation Partners is the Tokyo-based corporate venture arm of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group, established in 2019. The team manages five funds and has invested in 20 companies, with three unicorns in its portfolio: Fundbox, Carsome, and Chainalysis.
thesis: Backs early and growth-stage startups in lending, payments, wealth and asset management, capital markets, banking software, sustainability, and discovery sectors.
recent_activity: MUFG Innovation Partners signed a letter of intent to participate as co-founding member of ESG data initiative Gprnt and continued deploying its $20 billion yen startup fund.

name: MUUS Climate Partners
notes: MUUS Climate Partners is a New York climate venture firm founded in 2022 by the family office of Michael Sonnenfeldt. The team has backed 22 early and growth stage decarbonization companies across energy, buildings, and industry, with a debut $50 million fund.
thesis: Backs founders building toward a zero-emissions economy, with checks deployed across early and growth rounds in renewable power, building electrification, and circular industry.
recent_activity: MUUS made three new investments in 2025 with a follow-on into Harvest Thermal, and continued to expand its team with the addition of Chelsea Adler as head of investor relations.

name: MaC Venture Capital
notes: MaC Venture Capital is a Los Angeles seed firm backing founders across aerospace, food robotics, fintech, mobility, and consumer software. The firm partners deeply with founders beyond writing the initial check.
thesis: Backs bold founders at seed across consumer, enterprise, and deep tech, including space and defense applications.

name: MaRS Discovery District
notes: Toronto innovation hub and one of the largest urban innovation centers in North America. Supports startups in cleantech, health, fintech, and enterprise software with advisory, capital connections, and lab space across the MaRS Centre.
thesis: Connects Canadian and global startups to capital, customers, and advisors across cleantech, health, and emerging technology.
recent_activity: Hosting the MaRS Climate Impact conference in November 2026 and Toronto Takes on the World event in May 2026. Continues to run the Momentum and MaRS Capital programs.

name: Maersk Growth
notes: Maersk Growth is the corporate venture arm of A.P. Moller-Maersk, embedded inside the parent group's strategy function. The fund focuses on energy transition, supply chain, and decarbonization of shipping aligned with Maersk's 2040 net-zero target.
thesis: Backs founders tackling fundamental challenges in global trade, logistics, and supply chains, including marine electrification.
recent_activity: Participated in Fleetzero's $43M Series A for battery-electric cargo ships in January 2026.

name: Maine Angels
award: Pooled angel investments at the seed stage
notes: Maine Angels is a membership-based group of accredited investors founded in 2003. The group has invested $44M across more than 100 companies primarily in Maine and New England, including TimberHP, a Maine high-performance wood fiber insulation manufacturer.
