thesis: Pools accredited angel investment into seed-stage companies in Maine and New England across climate, biotech, manufacturing, and adjacent sectors.

name: MakeHaven
notes: MakeHaven is a 24/7 community makerspace in New Haven, Connecticut. The 10,000 square foot facility is an official Fab Lab with electronics, wood, metal, casting, plastics, ceramics, 3D printing, laser cutting, and a community bio lab.
thesis: Provides on-demand fabrication access, instruction, and prototyping infrastructure to members, including ClimateHaven resident startups.

name: Maker Nexus
award: $160 per month full-shop access, 7 days a week
notes: 28,000 sq ft Sunnyvale 501(c)(3) makerspace that filled the Bay Area gap left by TechShop's 2017 closure. Operates seven days a week with full woodshop, metal machining, sheet metal, welding, 3D printing, laser cutting, and an electronics lab.
thesis: Provides accessible Bay Area shop infrastructure for hardware makers, students, and pre-seed prototyping at low monthly rates.
recent_activity: Continued to expand classes and youth programs through 2025-2026 with a published 2025 Annual Report and steady membership pricing starting at $75 to $160 per month.

name: Maniv Mobility
notes: Dedicated mobility venture firm. Backs new vehicles, fleet software, autonomy stack, charging infrastructure, and energy storage for transport. Active across the US, Europe, and Israel.

name: Manufacturing USA Institutes (17 institutes)
deadline: America Makes: $14.5M materials calls (6/2/2026); MIAMI & INSITE additive metals (7/9/2026). Other institutes have separate calls.
notes: Manufacturing USA is a NIST-led network of 17 public-private institutes advancing domestic manufacturing innovation and workforce development. Member institutes share facilities, run R&D consortia, and train workers across advanced manufacturing domains.
thesis: Federally-funded industry-led consortia that fund manufacturing R&D and workforce projects across robotics, photonics, biomanufacturing, additive, and clean energy.
recent_activity: Institute roster also includes CyManII, EPIXC, IACMI, LIFT, NIIMBL, NextFlex, PowerAmerica, RAPID, and REMADE.

past_awardees.name: Boeing
past_awardees.description: Leads project advancing 3D-printed high-temperature nickel superalloy parts for engines and aerospace components.

past_awardees.name: RTX (Raytheon Technologies)
past_awardees.description: Leads project on 3D-printed refractory metals for hypersonic and extreme-environment hardware.

past_awardees.name: ATI Specialty Metals
past_awardees.description: Manufactures specialty metal powders and alloys used in additive manufacturing of aerospace and defense parts.

past_awardees.name: Quintus Technologies
past_awardees.description: Builds hot isostatic press equipment that post-processes 3D-printed metal parts to remove defects.

past_awardees.name: RPM Innovations
past_awardees.description: Builds large-scale directed-energy-deposition 3D printers for metal aerospace and defense parts.

past_awardees.name: IC3D
past_awardees.description: Leads project developing recycled-PET filament for sustainable industrial 3D printing.

name: Marble
award: EUR 2,500 to 3,000 monthly stipend; EUR 250K on company formation
notes: Marble is a Paris-based climate deep tech venture studio founded in 2021 by Benjamin Tincq and Jonny Everett. The 12-month program pairs scientists with operators to build companies, providing a monthly stipend of EUR 2,500 to 3,000 plus EUR 250,000 on company formation.
thesis: Builds founder pairs to tackle hard climate problems across energy, industry, agriculture, and resilience, with the goal of slashing emissions, removing carbon, and cooling the planet.
recent_activity: Marble's first ten companies have all raised follow-on funding, and the studio continues to recruit Founders-in-Residence for new cohorts through 2026.

name: Marlinspike Capital
notes: Arlington, VA-based venture firm investing in aerospace, defense, AI, autonomy, and cybersecurity. Portfolio spans 20+ companies with two recent exits in Voyager Technologies and Kodiak. Pentagon and IC pedigree on the team.
thesis: Backs pioneering technologies addressing national security needs with large commercial markets.
recent_activity: Realized exits in Voyager Technologies and Kodiak. Continues active investing across aerospace, AI, autonomy, and cyber.

name: Mass Medical Angels (MA2)
notes: New England-focused angel group founded in 2007 and based in Brookline. Targets seed and early-stage therapeutics, diagnostics, medical devices, health IT, and lab equipment with $250K to $3M per round, often syndicating with funds for full coverage.
thesis: Backs seed-stage New England life science companies aiming for 5x returns within 5 years through syndicated angel rounds.
recent_activity: Continues to host monthly meetings and syndicate with venture funds across the Boston life sciences cluster.

name: MassChallenge
notes: A global, equity-free startup accelerator founded in Boston in 2009. Runs cohort programs across healthcare, security, sustainable food, climate, and financial systems. Partner network includes BAE Systems, BCBS Massachusetts, Deloitte, Eli Lilly, MassMutual, and Verizon.
thesis: Equity-free acceleration with cash prizes (up to $100K+), corporate matchmaking, and industry-specific Challenge Programs for mid-to-late stage hardtech.
recent_activity: Currently between cohorts with notification sign-ups open for upcoming programming.

name: MassRobotics Accelerator
notes: The MassRobotics Accelerator is a 13-week in-person program at MassRobotics in Boston's Seaport, powered by the Mass Tech Collaborative. Two cohorts have awarded $2 million total in equity-free grants to 20 robotics startups, whose collective fundraising tops $2 billion.
thesis: Backs pre-seed and seed robotics founders across medical, mobility, autonomy, defense, agriculture, construction, and manufacturing with $100K equity-free MassTech grants plus deep mentor support.
recent_activity: MassRobotics continued cohort programming in 2024 and 2025, presented at the Robotics Summit and Expo Demo Day in Boston, and is recruiting sponsors for the next accelerator.

name: Material Impact
notes: Boston-based deep tech VC focused on material science. Nearly $800M under management across roughly 30 portfolio companies. Often acts as first money in and helps spin technology out of universities.
thesis: Backs first-of-their-kind material science companies across cleaner production, biomanufacturing, and resilient infrastructure.
recent_activity: Most recent investment in Folio Photonics, February 25, 2026.

name: Maven Capital Partners
notes: Glasgow-based UK private capital firm with 50+ investment professionals across 11 regional offices. Manages regional venture funds, EIS and VCT vehicles, and growth capital for tech-enabled and life science businesses.
thesis: Backs growth-stage UK businesses across deep tech, climate, healthcare, and software with regional reach.
recent_activity: Backed a GBP 2.6M Esk investment and Not In the Guide Books through Maven Cognition. Has invested over GBP 1B in UK businesses since 2009.

name: Mer Angels
award: Typical checks €150K to €500K+ into blue-economy startups
notes: Angel network focused on the blue economy: ocean conservation, marine biodiversity, water pollution, underwater communication. Runs an annual Ocean Pitch Challenge. European base with global deal flow.

name: Mercedes-Benz Group Ventures
notes: Mercedes-Benz Group Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Mercedes-Benz, with strategic investments aimed at the connected, autonomous, shared, and electric future of mobility. The group also runs STARTUP AUTOBAHN, one of Europe's largest open innovation platforms for mobility startups.
thesis: Backs Series B and later startups whose technology can fold into Mercedes-Benz vehicles, production, or services, including electric drive, autonomy, and luxury digital experiences.
recent_activity: Mercedes-Benz launched its first electric-first MMA architecture in 2025 with more than 40 new models planned through 2027, continuing to absorb portfolio technology such as YASA's axial flux motors.

name: Microsoft Climate Innovation Fund
notes: The Microsoft Climate Innovation Fund is a $1B program deploying equity and debt into climate technology. It targets underfunded markets with measurable carbon, water, waste, and ecosystem outcomes.
thesis: Backs underfunded climate solutions aligned with Microsoft's operations and customer demand. Will not lead, take majority stakes, or fund public companies.
recent_activity: Has committed to over 40 companies and multiple fund managers as of 2025, including Buoyant Ventures, Closed Loop Partners, and Energy Impact Partners.

name: Mitacs (Canada)
notes: Mitacs is a Canadian national innovation organization based in Vancouver that bridges industry and academia. It funds research internships and postdoctoral placements across advanced manufacturing, AI, cleantech, cybersecurity, health, IT, and quantum.
thesis: Matched funding (CAD $15K-$120K per project) for industry-academic R&D collaborations and skilled talent placements.
recent_activity: Announced a partnership with the Helmholtz Association in May 2026 for Canada-Germany research collaboration, plus aerospace partnerships with Mexico's Tecnologico Nacional de Mexico and ANUIES.

name: Mitsubishi Chemical Group MCG
notes: Mitsubishi Chemical Group's venture activities run primarily through Diamond Edge Ventures. The unit invests in startups aligned with the group's core materials, chemistry, electronics, and health businesses, plus emerging adjacent areas.
thesis: Invests in advanced materials, electric vehicle and battery technology, electronics, health and medical devices, food ingredients, green materials, and advanced manufacturing.
recent_activity: Most recent disclosed investment was a Series A in Boston Materials in September 2025.

name: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Future Stream
notes: Future Stream is the innovation and venturing arm of Mitsubishi Heavy Industries in Tokyo. The group invests across energy transition, aerospace, and industrial automation alongside MHI's broader corporate development activities.
thesis: Backs growth-stage startups whose technologies plug into MHI's industrial, energy, and aerospace businesses, from electrified industrial heat to advanced manufacturing.
recent_activity: In March 2026 MHI invested in AtmosZero as an original equipment manufacturer partner backing electrified industrial steam technology.

name: Mitsubishi UFJ Capital
notes: Venture capital arm of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group, founded in 1974. Backs IT, SaaS, life sciences, AI, electronics, biotech, fintech, and healthcare from pre-seed through pre-Series A. Has invested in 526 companies with 81 going public to date.
thesis: Backs Japanese and international deep tech, biotech, and materials companies through IPO or M&A.
recent_activity: 34 new investments in the past 12 months as of December 2025. Hutzper and PRONI listed on Tokyo Stock Exchange in December 2025.

name: Mitsui Global Investment
notes: Mitsui and Co. Global Investment is the Menlo Park corporate venture arm of Mitsui and Co., investing in early and growth-stage technology companies. The firm has made 91 investments and seen 87 exits, including IPOs of Proterra and Spire.
thesis: Backs founders in healthcare, IT, mobility, agritech, and foodtech where strategic value can be captured for the broader Mitsui group.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Autotalks was acquired in June 2025, and Mitsui launched joint venture seed fund E12 Ventures with Morado Ventures focused on climate tech, agtech, foodtech, digital health, and fintech.

name: Molten Ventures
notes: Molten Ventures is a publicly listed London growth investor backing European founders from Series A onward. The firm has operated for two decades across enterprise software, deep tech, and health.
thesis: Backs European technology companies from Series A through growth, providing capital and network support across enterprise software, deep tech, and health.

name: MoreVC
notes: European mobility seed firm investing in fleets, electric vehicles, and the supply chain for transport electrification.

name: Mubadala Capital Ventures
notes: Asset management subsidiary of Mubadala Investment Company in Abu Dhabi with $430B across managed, advised, and administered funds. Allocates to venture, growth equity, credit, and private equity across climate, life sciences, and technology.
thesis: Backs growth-stage technology, life sciences, and climate companies with sovereign-scale capital and global reach.
recent_activity: Announced a KAIO partnership for digital private market access and launched Aldar Capital connecting global investors with GCC real assets in December 2025. Corient acquired European wealth managers representing $214B in September 2025.

name: N47 (formerly Siemens next47)
notes: Formerly next47, now operating as N47. Independent VC originally spun out of Siemens. Backs infrastructure, systems, and applications companies with strong product-market fit early on. Sectors span industrial AI, security, autonomy, and frontier compute.
thesis: Backs builders with strong product traction before traditional growth metrics emerge.
recent_activity: Recent publications on home health AI infrastructure, physical AI, and the frontline economy.
correction_note: Siemens spun out next47 in 2025, now operating independently as N47.

name: NASA Tipping Point
notes: NASA Tipping Point is a Space Technology Mission Directorate program that co-funds industry development of space technologies near commercial readiness. Awards have funded lunar power, in-space manufacturing, and reentry hardware.
thesis: Pushes promising space technologies past the tipping point to flight, sharing costs with private companies for moon, Mars, and orbital capabilities.
recent_activity: Most recent disclosed cohort awarded 11 contracts totaling $150M for lunar regolith manufacturing, lunar power grids, hypersonic decelerators, and radioisotope power.

name: NEDO (Japan)
notes: Japan's New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization, the country's largest public R&D promoter for energy, environmental, and industrial tech. Runs the Green Innovation Fund and multi-year project funding across renewables, hydrogen, batteries, and manufacturing. Open to international partners on Japan-relevant tech.
thesis: Funds collaborative research and demonstration projects that move Japan toward carbon neutrality and industrial competitiveness.
recent_activity: Reopened a supplementary call in May 2026 for single-junction perovskite solar cell mass production under the Green Innovation Fund. Five-year program runs fiscal 2026 through 2030.

name: NIH RADx
notes: RADx (Rapid Acceleration of Diagnostics) is an NIH initiative launched during COVID-19 to speed development, commercialization, and deployment of diagnostic technologies. NIBIB continues to operate the RADx Tech innovation funnel and the Independent Test Assessment Program (ITAP).
thesis: Non-dilutive funding and validation infrastructure for at-home and point-of-care diagnostics, now expanded beyond COVID to respiratory disease, endometriosis, mpox, maternal health, and lesion testing.
recent_activity: Recent program initiatives target diagnostics for endometriosis, mpox, maternal health, and other gaps using the established RADx Tech infrastructure.

name: NIH SBIR
notes: The NIH SBIR and STTR programs collectively distribute roughly $1.4 billion per year across 24 NIH institutes, making them the largest non-defense small business research programs in the US. The SEED office at NIH provides funding plus pitch coaching, IP consulting, and regulatory guidance.
recent_activity: Congress reauthorized the SBIR and STTR programs on April 13, 2026, with the next standard receipt date set for September 5, 2026.

name: NIST Manufacturing Extension Partnership
notes: The Manufacturing Extension Partnership is a US Department of Commerce program at NIST that supports small and medium-sized manufacturers. The program operates 51 state-level centers offering technical assistance, supplier scouting, cybersecurity resources, and matching grants.
recent_activity: NIST announced a Notice of Intent for a Technology Accelerator Pilot Program in May 2026 and expanded its partnership with CESMII to advance smart manufacturing.

name: NOAA SBIR
notes: Small Business Innovation Research program at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Funds Phase I awards up to $175K and Phase II awards up to $600K for ocean, weather, climate observation, and coastal resilience technologies.
thesis: Funds small businesses building ocean, atmospheric, weather, and coastal resilience technologies aligned with NOAA's mission.
recent_activity: Issued the FY2026 NOAA SBIR Phase I solicitation with focus areas spanning ocean exploration, climate adaptation, and weather prediction.

name: NRC IRAP
deadline: Rolling : engage via Industrial Technology Advisor (ITA)
notes: Canada's Industrial Research Assistance Program, run by the National Research Council. Provides advisory and funding support to Canadian SMEs commercializing technology, with Industrial Technology Advisors embedded with companies. Covers cleantech, AI, defense, and IP services.
thesis: Funds Canadian SMEs increasing innovation capacity and bringing R&D to market.
recent_activity: Scheduled to integrate into the new Canada Innovation Corporation by 2026-27. NRC IRAP remains operational until that transition.

past_awardees.name: 4AG Robotics
past_awardees.description: Builds automated mushroom-harvesting robots used in commercial growing operations.

past_awardees.name: Carbon Upcycling Technologies
past_awardees.description: Captures CO2 by reacting it with industrial waste like fly ash to make low-carbon cement additives.

past_awardees.name: Svante
past_awardees.description: Builds rotary contactor systems that capture CO2 from cement and other industrial flue gas streams.

past_awardees.name: GHGSat
past_awardees.description: Operates satellites that measure methane emissions from individual oil, gas, and waste facilities.

past_awardees.name: Moment Energy
past_awardees.description: Repurposes used electric vehicle batteries into stationary energy storage systems.

past_awardees.name: Ionomr Innovations
past_awardees.description: Builds hydrocarbon-based ion exchange membranes for fuel cells and green hydrogen electrolyzers.

past_awardees.name: Saltworks Technologies
past_awardees.description: Builds modular industrial wastewater and lithium brine treatment systems.

name: NSF Convergence Accelerator
deadline: Between cycles : program future uncertain as of May 2026
notes: Cross-disciplinary NSF program that funds team-based research aimed at societal challenges. Phase I awards roughly $650K for nine months of concept development, with selected teams advancing to Phase II at up to $5M over two years. Tracks rotate annually with recent emphasis on AI, biotech, and climate.
thesis: Funds convergent research teams from academia, industry, and nonprofits with a deliverable in three years.
recent_activity: Active solicitations rotate annually; current tracks listed on the program page.

name: NSF I-Corps
notes: NSF I-Corps is the National Science Foundation's customer discovery program for researchers commercializing federally funded science. The 7-week immersive curriculum runs through regional Hubs and has helped launch more than 1,000 startups.
thesis: Trains scientists and engineers in evidence-based customer discovery before they build products no one wants, accelerating transfer of NSF-funded research into industry.
recent_activity: Spring 2026 application window opened for the Fall 2026 National I-Corps Teams cohorts, with 20 to 30 teams per training cohort hosted by an I-Corps Hub.

name: NSF Regional Innovation Engines (NSF Engines)
award: Type-1 up to $15M / 2 yrs; Type-2 up to $160M / 10 yrs
deadline: Between cycles : 2nd cohort in final selection. Next solicitation not yet announced.
notes: An NSF program funding regional R&D ecosystems through the Directorate for Technology, Innovation and Partnerships. Each Engine starts with a $15M two-year award and can scale to $160M over a decade. Total NSF commitment approaches $1.6B across regions building capacity in semiconductors, AI, biotech, energy storage, and other critical technologies.
thesis: Funds the consortium, not the startup. Startups participate through the regional Engine as a commercialization partner.
recent_activity: Selected 15 finalists in September 2025 for the second Engines competition. The first cohort of 10 Engines was named in January 2024.

past_awardees.name: Upstate New York Energy Storage Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Binghamton University building a regional supply chain for next-generation batteries in upstate New York.

past_awardees.name: Central Florida Semiconductor Innovation Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by University of Central Florida growing a semiconductor manufacturing and packaging cluster in Florida.

past_awardees.name: Louisiana Energy Transition Engine (NSF FUEL)
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Louisiana State University commercializing clean fuels, carbon management, and offshore wind technology along the Gulf Coast.

past_awardees.name: Great Lakes Water Innovation Engine (NSF ReNEW)
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Current building a water innovation hub focused on PFAS removal, lead pipe replacement, and water reuse.

past_awardees.name: Colorado-Wyoming Climate Resilience Engine (NSF CO-WY)
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Innosphere Ventures commercializing climate adaptation tech across Colorado and Wyoming.

past_awardees.name: Piedmont Triad Regenerative Medicine Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Wake Forest Institute for Regenerative Medicine building a biomanufacturing hub for tissues and cell therapies in North Carolina.

past_awardees.name: Paso del Norte Defense and Aerospace Innovation Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by the University of Texas at El Paso developing defense and aerospace manufacturing along the Texas-New Mexico border.

past_awardees.name: North Dakota Advanced Agriculture Technology Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Grand Farm commercializing autonomous agriculture and precision farming tech across the northern Great Plains.

past_awardees.name: North Carolina Textile Innovation Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by North Carolina State University building advanced, sustainable textile manufacturing across the Carolinas.

past_awardees.name: Southwest Sustainability Innovation Engine
past_awardees.description: Coalition led by Arizona State University building water, energy, and materials innovation across the desert southwest.

name: NavalX
notes: NavalX is the Department of the Navy innovation network operating 18 regional Tech Bridges aligned to Naval Commands and Warfare Centers. The organization removes barriers for traditional and nontraditional industry partners to engage with the Navy and Marine Corps.
thesis: Provides non-dilutive engagement pathways, accelerators, and Crucible Ignitor studio support to startups and researchers building dual-use technology for the Navy and Marine Corps.
recent_activity: Ran a 10-week accelerator from November 2025 through February 2026. The 2026 Crucible Ignitor cohort marks its third year of dual-use tech commercialization studio activity.

name: New Climate Ventures
notes: New Climate Ventures is an Austin-based seed firm investing in early-stage startups that produce, power, or consume goods with materially lower carbon intensity. The portfolio spans industrial decarbonization, sustainable food and materials, and the built environment.
thesis: Backs seed-stage companies whose core product directly reduces or avoids carbon emissions across power, industry, food, and materials.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Atmos was acquired in 2024. The fund continues active seed investing across industrial decarbonization and food.

name: New York Angels
award: Member-driven; group is one of the larger sustained syndicators in NYC
notes: Long-running New York City angel group operating from 1216 Broadway. Public Apply for Funding intake via Gust. Sector-agnostic but actively writes into hardware, medtech, and frontier-tech rounds when conviction is high.

name: Newlab
hq: Brooklyn, NY (plus Detroit, New Orleans, Montevideo, Riyadh)
award: Membership-based access to fabrication labs plus cohort-driven studio investments
notes: Newlab is a venture platform for critical technology with five locations across Brooklyn, Detroit, New Orleans, Montevideo, and Riyadh. The 84,000 sq ft flagship at Brooklyn Navy Yard provides fabrication space, while Newlab supports more than 400 member startups that have raised over $5.8 billion in venture capital and produced $2.3 billion in exits.
thesis: Operates as a venture platform, not a traditional accelerator, combining shop access, government and corporate partnerships, and selective studio capital to commercialize critical technology.
recent_activity: Newlab launched The 23rd, a 380,000 sq ft manufacturing campus in Detroit, hosted its Continuums event in Brooklyn for 500-plus members, and continued the 2026 Founder Fellowship for climate tech founders.

name: NextCorps Manufacturing Accelerator
award: Free 9-week virtual program plus connections to vendors and funding
notes: The NextCorps Manufacturing Accelerator is a free virtual program funded by Empire State Development and administered by NextCorps. The 9-week curriculum helps startups move from works-like prototypes to mass production using DoD Manufacturing Readiness Level metrics and DOE Build4Scale content.
thesis: Concentrates on advanced materials, medical devices, and lab-based hardware companies, helping them produce Design for Manufacturing documents, Bills of Materials, and Manufacturing Roadmaps.
recent_activity: Spring 2026 cohort applications were open through April 10, 2026 with an info session on April 8 and program kickoff on April 15.

name: NightDragon
notes: NightDragon is a San Francisco growth firm led by Dave DeWalt focused on cybersecurity, defense, AI, quantum, and national security. The NightScale platform connects portfolio companies to 100+ advisors and 100+ industry partners.
thesis: Growth-stage capital and operational support for SecureTech companies addressing real-world security challenges.
recent_activity: Announced GDIT partnership for federal tech adoption and celebrated HawkEye 360's IPO. Invested in Armada AI for edge computing.

name: Norrsken Foundation Impact Accelerator
notes: Norrsken is a Stockholm-based impact foundation with hubs in Barcelona, Kigali, Brussels, and Amsterdam (opening 2026). Founded by Klarna co-founder Niklas Adalberth, it runs accelerators, hubs, and five investment vehicles spanning Europe and Africa.
thesis: Backs impact founders across climate, health, food, and AI for good, with European and African mandates.
recent_activity: Operates five funds (Norrsken VC, Launcher, Evolve, 22, and Africa Seed). Programs include Prompt What Matters for AI applications and Norrsken/100 annual rankings.

name: Norrsken VC
notes: Stockholm-based pan-European impact venture firm tied to the Norrsken Foundation. Backs early-stage companies across materials, energy, healthcare, and AI. Open-sources its impact framework and ties impact measurement to fund returns.
thesis: Backs European founders building scalable companies with measurable positive impact across the UN SDGs.
recent_activity: Most recent dated update is the 2024 Impact Report. 2025-2026 activity not surfaced on the homepage.

name: Northern Gritstone
notes: Northern Gritstone is an independent investment company headquartered in Manchester, with offices in Leeds, Sheffield, and London. The firm backs spinouts from the universities of Manchester, Leeds, Liverpool, and Sheffield with permanent capital of GBP 382M.
thesis: Invests in deep tech, advanced materials, semiconductors, AI, healthtech, and gene therapy spinouts from Northern England's research universities under a profit-with-purpose mandate.
recent_activity: Secured an additional GBP 20M from British Business Bank and Andrew Law and led a GBP 2.5M seed round in techbio company Imperagen.

name: Northzone
notes: Northzone is a multi-stage European venture firm founded in 1996 with offices in London, New York, Stockholm, Berlin, and Amsterdam. The team backs category creators across climate, AI, and consumer, with a dedicated Climate Transformation Fund alongside the core strategy.
thesis: Backs European and US founders building category-defining companies, with climate and industrial software bets running through both the main fund and a separate Climate Transformation vehicle.
recent_activity: Northzone backed Cuspai and Xbow at seed and continued deploying its Climate Transformation Fund into industrial software and freight electrification through 2025 and 2026.

name: Octopus Energy Generation
notes: Renewable energy investment arm of Octopus Energy with GBP 8.5B in assets under management and 5.7 GW of operating capacity. Funds utility-scale wind, solar, battery storage, and hydrogen projects across Europe and Australia.
thesis: Backs renewable generation and storage assets that lower the cost of clean electricity for retail customers.
recent_activity: Announced GBP 3B hydrogen plan with RES and continued to expand AU and EU pipeline through 2026, powering 2.8 million homes annually from managed assets.

name: Octopus Ventures
notes: Octopus Ventures is a London-based multi-sector firm investing from pre-seed through Series A across seven verticals. The firm backs founders building businesses with positive social or environmental impact.
thesis: Concentrates on B2B Software, Bio, Climate, Consumer, Deep Tech, Fintech, and Health, with a bias toward founders solving meaningful problems.
recent_activity: Highlighted Definely and Ben Medland's Drift (mobile renewable energy) in recent portfolio coverage.

name: OrbiMed
notes: A New York-based global healthcare investment firm managing roughly $20B across public and private strategies. Covers biopharmaceuticals, medical devices, digital health, diagnostics, and healthcare services. Over 150 professionals in 12 offices have backed 500+ companies and 130+ IPOs.
thesis: Venture, growth, royalty, and credit checks ($5M-$100M+) across the entire healthcare stack.
recent_activity: Ranked #1 on Endpoints News' top 100 biotech VCs. Raised $1.86B for Healthcare Royalty & Credit Fund V. Portfolio company Perfuse acquired by Bayer.

name: Outsiders Fund
notes: Outsiders Fund is a seed and Series A firm founded in 2020 by Datto founder Austin McChord and Teddy Seem. The fund avoids hot sectors and instead backs founders modernizing unglamorous industries such as cattle supply chains, industrial painting, and satellite buses.
thesis: Targets mismatches between sector-specific technology adoption and broader frontier capability, writing $500K to $8M checks into US and Canadian seed and Series A founders.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Apex Space became a unicorn in 2025, the fund's first.

name: Oxford Science Enterprises
notes: Oxford Science Enterprises is the independent venture firm partnered with the University of Oxford to commercialize its research. The firm has a GBP 1.3 billion net asset value, more than 60 staff, and over 50 spinouts spanning life sciences, deep tech, and energy.
thesis: Builds and funds Oxford spinouts with patient capital, taking concentrated positions from founding through scale-up across therapeutics, quantum, fusion, and biology.
recent_activity: Oxford Ionics was acquired by IonQ for $1.075 billion in June 2025, Dark Blue Therapeutics was acquired for up to $840 million in January 2026, and Wild Bioscience closed a $60 million Series A in October 2025.

name: Pale Blue Dot
notes: A Malmö-based pre-seed and seed venture firm investing in climate and sustainability technology across Europe and the US. Founded by former startup founders. Organizes thesis around three pillars: Optimize, Adapt, and Pioneer.
thesis: €0.5M-€2M pre-seed and seed checks into climate startups reducing emissions, building resilience, or pioneering new sustainable systems.
recent_activity: Backed Pollen (e-mobility, May 2026), Cinqo (waste management, March 2026), Tangible (climate finance, February 2026), and VARM (decarbonization, February 2026).

name: Panasonic Ventures
notes: Panasonic Ventures is the Cupertino-based corporate venture group of Panasonic Holdings. Founded in 1998, the group focuses on later-stage investments and incubation in energy storage, industrial technology, and B2B software.
thesis: Backs later-stage companies in energy storage, semiconductors, industrial automation, and enterprise software with strategic relevance to Panasonic's product lines.
recent_activity: Most recent disclosed deal was a January 2026 later-stage VC investment in an AI model company.

name: Parkwalk Advisors
notes: London-based university-spinout fund founded in 2009 and now part of IP Group. Invests through EIS-eligible vehicles in deep tech, healthtech, and cleantech companies emerging from Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial, Bristol, and other UK universities.
thesis: Bridges UK academic IP and commercial markets by anchoring spinouts at the seed and Series A stage.
recent_activity: Quantum Motion closed a $160M Series C in May 2026 with Parkwalk participation. Hosted the Parkwalk Portfolio Showcase 2026 highlighting healthtech and deep tech spinouts.

name: Patrick J. McGovern Foundation
notes: The Patrick J. McGovern Foundation is a Boston-based philanthropy stewarding a $1.5B endowment focused on AI and data for public purpose. It has directed more than $500M to climate action, public health, education, and democratic governance.
thesis: Funds nonprofits applying AI and data tools to climate resilience, health equity, human rights, journalism, crisis response, and digital literacy.
recent_activity: Announced $75.8M in new commitments across 149 grants in 13 countries in December 2025. Plans to expand the public-purpose AI agenda at India's Global AI Summit in 2026.

name: Phoenix Venture Partners
notes: Phoenix Venture Partners is a Palo Alto VC specializing in advanced materials. The firm invests in early to revenue-stage companies applying novel materials, devices, manufacturing processes, and tools across computing, electronics, transportation, energy, and life sciences.
thesis: Backs advanced materials and novel devices with the potential to catalyze new products and redefine industries, paired with hands-on support in IP strategy, business development, and recruiting.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Myant acquired Boston-based FIGUR8 in April 2026 to add musculoskeletal intelligence to its Internet of Humans platform. ODC closed a $45M Series A in March 2026.

name: Pillar VC
notes: A Boston pre-seed and seed firm backing technically brilliant founders at inception. Active across AI, biology, healthcare, and deep tech, with strong ties to MIT and Harvard spinouts. Operates Pillar VC plus Pillar Biosciences.
thesis: $500K-$5M first checks at company formation in technical breakthroughs across drug discovery, pathology, synthetic biology, and software.
recent_activity: Announced Fund IV at $175M. Portfolio company Abridge raised $300M at a $5.3B valuation. Latent Labs launched with $50M.

name: Pioneer (by Daniel Gross)
notes: Pioneer was a remote-first accelerator founded in 2018 by Daniel Gross and Rishi Narang, backed by Stripe and Marc Andreessen. It funded 150+ companies with 300+ founders across 50+ countries before halting new investments in 2024.
thesis: Tournament-based admission for solo founders worldwide, with $100K checks and mentorship from a network of operators.
recent_activity: Stopped making new investments in 2024. Portfolio aggregate value reported above $2B.
correction_note: Halted new investments in 2024. Listed for completeness only; not currently funding new founders.

name: Planet A Ventures
notes: Planet A Ventures is a Hamburg climate fund with an in-house science team that uses life cycle assessments to evaluate every investment. Scientists hold veto power over deals that lack quantifiable environmental impact.
thesis: Backs European seed-stage founders in materials, food, energy, and industry whose products demonstrably reduce emissions, waste, water use, or biodiversity loss.
recent_activity: Makersite raised €60M Series B in July 2025 for Microsoft supply chain optimization. Arsenale Bioyards raised €9.5M for biomanufacturing in February 2025.

name: Playground Global
notes: Playground Global is a Palo Alto deep-tech firm founded by Andy Rubin. It invests across next-gen compute, automation, energy transition, and engineered biology.
thesis: Backs breakthrough science 'somewhere between improbable and impossible' across quantum, AI acceleration, novel computing, robotics, energy, and AI-bio.
recent_activity: Hosted AGM 2025, the firm's annual portfolio gathering, in Palo Alto.

name: Plug and Play Tech Center
notes: Plug and Play is a Sunnyvale, CA accelerator and corporate-innovation network founded by Saeed Amidi. It runs industry-specific programs in mobility, energy, sustainability, maritime, and insurtech, matching startups with corporate partners rather than acting as a pure capital provider.
thesis: Connects early and growth-stage startups with corporate partners across regulated industries via twice-yearly batches plus pilot introductions.
recent_activity: Has invested in over 1,000 companies to date with $10.9B in known invested capital and an average check size of $15.9M.

name: Plural Platform
notes: Operator-led European fund based in London and founded by former entrepreneurs from Wise, Skype, Songkick, and Onfido. Invests at seed and Series A in deep tech, climate, and biotech companies that take on systemic European risks.
thesis: Funds founders tackling Europe's GDP-level opportunities in deep tech, climate, and biotech with operator-led capital.
recent_activity: Led Proxima Fusion's Series A and continues to anchor European defense and frontier energy rounds throughout 2025-2026.

name: Polaris Partners
notes: Polaris Partners is a Boston firm with nearly three decades of healthcare and technology investing. The team builds therapeutics and medical platforms in close partnership with scientific founders, including longtime collaborator Bob Langer.
thesis: Backs biotech and digital health founders from inception through growth, often serving as company-builder alongside academic scientists.
recent_activity: Triveni Bio dosed first healthy volunteers in a Phase 1 trial in May 2026, and Dyno Therapeutics launched two new AAV capsids and an AI platform the same month.

name: Porsche Ventures
notes: Porsche Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Porsche AG, operating from six locations worldwide. The team has invested in 52 companies including four unicorns and focuses on mobility, electrification, autonomy, and customer experience.
thesis: Invests globally in frontier technology and digital business models that advance the industrial sector, with strategic emphasis on customer experience, mobility, and digital lifestyle.
recent_activity: Invested in &Charge in 2026. Portfolio company Urgently was acquired by Agero in March 2026.

name: Powerhouse Ventures
notes: Powerhouse Ventures is an Oakland climate tech seed firm founded in 2018 by Emily Kirsch. The firm pairs investing with Powerhouse Innovation, a sister advisory practice that runs climate programs for utilities and corporates, giving the fund proprietary deal flow and pilot customers.
thesis: Backs seed stage software founders advancing clean energy, mobility, and industry, with checks of $200K to $2M from a $70M+ Fund II.
recent_activity: Powerhouse ran its 2025 Annual General Meeting and launched the New Dawn climate conference, which drew more than 1,000 attendees. Fund II continued active deployment through 2025 and 2026.

name: Prelude Ventures
notes: Prelude Ventures is a San Francisco climate-only fund operating across the built environment, carbon management, compute, food and agriculture, energy, manufacturing, and mobility. Their tagline reads 'for them, climate is everything.'
thesis: Backs companies with the greatest potential to mitigate climate change, with a sector-broad climate mandate.
recent_activity: Based at One Ferry Building in San Francisco. Continues active deployment across seven climate verticals.

name: Primary Venture Partners
award: Specialized seed fund with a 60+ person operating team supporting portfolio companies
notes: NYC-anchored seed investor publishing an annual Request for Startups (most recent: 2026). Portfolio includes Etched (AI compute hardware), Biological Computing Co, and infrastructure-heavy companies. Best fit for hardware-software hybrids at seed.

name: Promus Ventures
notes: Chicago and Luxembourg-based deep tech firm investing in space, robotics, and frontier engineering companies. Manages a dedicated space vehicle, Orbital Ventures, with the Government of Luxembourg as an anchor LP.
thesis: Funds ambitious deep tech founders solving complex physical-world problems with capital from the US and Europe.
recent_activity: Press kit refreshed November 2025; team continues to invest from Orbital Ventures II across satellite, propulsion, and dual-use companies.

name: Quantonation
notes: Quantonation is a global early-stage VC specializing in quantum technology and deep physics. The firm invests across quantum computing, networks, sensing, and breakthrough physics-based startups in Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific.
thesis: Backs companies pioneering quantum advantage, including neutral-atom and photonic quantum computing, quantum networks, and ultra-precise sensors.
recent_activity: Recent checks include Yaqumo (Japan, neutral-atom quantum), Vertical Compute (Belgium, memory-centric AI), Chiral (Switzerland, nanomaterial semiconductors), and PrediQT.

name: R/GA Ventures
notes: Corporate-backed seed fund with several themed cohorts including mobility (with partners like Toyota), retail, and venture studio programs.

name: RA Capital Management
notes: Boston-based multi-stage investor in public and private healthcare and life science companies. Runs an internal research arm called TechAtlas that maps scientific and clinical data. Often a crossover investor bridging private rounds into IPO.
thesis: Backs drugs, devices, diagnostics, and research tools using evidence-based scientific analysis.

team_sample.name: Shawn Valliere
team_sample.title: Head of Family Office and Partner Services

recent_activity: No specific 2025-2026 milestones surfaced on the homepage.

name: Razor's Edge Ventures
notes: National security focused multi-stage VC founded in 2011 and based in Reston, VA. Backs autonomous systems, space, cyber, AI, and dual-use companies. Total AUM exceeds $1.25B across four funds.
thesis: Backs high barrier-to-entry technology companies solving key national security challenges with dual-use commercial potential.
recent_activity: Closed Fund IV in September 2025 at $560M, the firm's largest fund to date.

name: Red Bear Angels (Cornell)
notes: Red Bear Angels is Cornell University's angel investment network, founded in 2012. The group has deployed over $25M across 40-plus companies, with explicit focus on automation, hardware, and physical AI.
thesis: Writes $100K to $1M checks at seed to Series A in Big Data, Crypto, Fintech, Healthcare, and Robotics, requiring a Cornell founder, C-suite, faculty, or staff connection.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Verasight saw polling data picked up by 10-plus major outlets in May 2026. SafeRide Health won the Healthcare Analytics Innovation Award. Harbinger Motors launched an off-grid power division with Airstream as its first customer.

name: Refactor Capital
notes: Refactor Capital is a solo-GP seed fund based in San Francisco backing hard tech founders working in atoms rather than software. It has $300M in assets under management across five funds and writes $1M to $2M seed checks.
thesis: Targets hard but not impossible companies in aerospace, energy, bio, critical materials, defense, nuclear, and advanced manufacturing where technical execution is the primary moat.
recent_activity: Announced $50M Fund 5 in May 2026 with full LP recommitment. Recent checks include co-leading TBC's $25M seed and Hexium's $12M seed for laser isotope separation.

name: Refresh Materials
notes: Specialty seed fund focused on advanced materials, biomaterials, and the circular economy. Targets capital-efficient material innovation companies across packaging, textiles, construction, and chemicals.
thesis: Backs capital-efficient advanced materials and biomaterials founders building circular-economy supply chains.
recent_activity: Site was unreachable at the time of scraping; firm continues to source seed-stage materials science companies based on public filings.

name: Repsol Corporate Venturing
notes: Repsol Corporate Venturing is the venture arm of Spanish energy firm Repsol, with more than EUR 100 million under management. The team has made 45 investments at an average initial check of EUR 1.5 million, with activity across Spain, the US, Israel, and the UK.
thesis: Backs companies in low-carbon and circular economy, advanced mobility and renewables, and digital optimization of energy assets, taking minority stakes from Series A through growth.

team_sample.name: Gema Garcia Gonzalez
team_sample.title: Director of Open Innovation and Corporate Venturing

recent_activity: Repsol invested in network management software company Sybol in March 2026, and EV battery-swap portfolio company Ample reached unicorn status.

name: Robin Hood Ventures
award: $250K to $1M per round, often syndicating with VCs
notes: Robin Hood Ventures is a Philadelphia angel group founded in 1999. The group invests $250K to $1 million per round across software, healthcare IT, hardtech, and life sciences, often syndicating with regional venture capitalists and other angel groups.
thesis: Backs mid-Atlantic early-stage founders with explicit hardtech and life sciences focus, taking board seats and contributing to governance.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Virion Therapeutics shared promising preclinical cancer data, and Proscia was selected by PathGroup Labs for diagnostic services.

name: Robotics Factory (Pittsburgh Robotics Network)
award: Up to $100K plus 12 months of prototyping space
notes: Robotics Factory is a joint program of Innovation Works and Pittsburgh Robotics Network with EDA funding. The program runs Create, Accelerate, and Scale tracks for robotics founders in Southwestern Pennsylvania.
thesis: Helps robotics startups create, accelerate, and scale through up to $100K in pre-seed funding plus prototyping space and a robotics testing shop in Lawrenceville.
recent_activity: 16 Robotics Factory startups exhibited at Pittsburgh's Robotics and AI Discovery Day in November 2025. Applications are open for the next Accelerate cohort.

name: Root Ventures
notes: Root Ventures is a San Francisco seed firm founded in 2015 by engineers to back hard technology founders. The team underwrites engineering risk in robotics, aerospace, advanced manufacturing, and developer tools, and operates a hardware lab with machine shops and lab equipment.
thesis: Backs founders solving complex technical challenges where engineering risk is the central question, with $1M to $3M seed checks from a $190M Fund IV.
recent_activity: Root Ventures Fund IV closed at $190 million in late 2025 and remained active across robotics and frontier tech seed rounds.

name: Roux Institute (Northeastern)
award: Founder Residency plus Maine Summer Accelerator
notes: The Roux Institute is Northeastern University's Portland, Maine graduate campus focused on AI, life sciences, and engineering. The institute has supported about 140 startups and $40 million in funded research, with 646 graduate students enrolled and a 92 percent post-graduation job placement rate.
thesis: Pairs graduate education and industry research with a Founder Residency and Maine Summer Accelerator, supporting Maine-anchored AI, biotech, and climate founders.

team_sample.name: Rai Winslow
team_sample.title: Director of Life Science and Medical Research

team_sample.name: Jennifer Dy
team_sample.title: Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering

recent_activity: The institute hosted a climate conversation with Dr. Ayana Elizabeth Johnson and Barton Seaver in April 2026 and is running the AI in Action 2026 Business Summit on June 5, 2026.

name: S2G Ventures
notes: S2G Investments is a multi-asset firm focused on food and agriculture, energy, and oceans. The firm has 120+ portfolio companies and $2.8B in assets under management, anchored by the Walton family.
thesis: Invests at the seams of sector transitions across commodity-driven, policy-sensitive markets through Venture and Growth and Special Opportunities strategies.
recent_activity: Closed the $1B Solutions Fund I in May 2026 to scale growth-stage companies. Hired Grant Leslie in November 2025 and Kevin Lo in July 2025.

name: SBI Investment
notes: Investment arm of SBI Group, the Japanese financial services conglomerate. Operates dedicated funds in biotechnology, AI, fintech, and Web3, often co-investing alongside Asian and US venture firms.
thesis: Backs growth-stage technology companies with SBI Group's distribution and balance sheet across Asia.
recent_activity: Continued to anchor cross-border biotech and AI rounds with US and European partners through 2025-2026.

name: SGInnovate
notes: SGInnovate is Singapore's state-owned deep tech investor and ecosystem builder. The organization co-invests alongside private VCs in AI, biotech, climate, and quantum companies and runs Deep Tech Central, a community platform with 190,000+ members.
thesis: Co-invests with private VCs in Singapore-anchored deep tech companies across AI, biotech, climate, and quantum while building talent and community infrastructure.

name: SOFTBANK Vision Fund
notes: SoftBank Vision Fund is the mega growth fund inside SoftBank Group, backing transformational technology companies at scale. Major positions span AI hardware, AI infrastructure, and AI applications, with checks ranging from $100 million to $1 billion or more.
thesis: Backs founders with outsized ambition across the AI economy stack, from semiconductors and compute through applications, often building positions across multiple rounds.
recent_activity: Vision Fund published its 2025 CEO Survey on portfolio confidence and AI adoption, with continued coverage of Wayve autonomy and other portfolio milestones.

name: SOFWERX
notes: SOFWERX is a 501(c)(3) innovation hub serving US Special Operations Command. The organization connects government, industry, academia, and national labs for rapid prototyping, Tech Tuesday forums, and contracting events.
thesis: Compresses the proposal-to-prototype timeline for special operations forces by running prize challenges, assessment events, and rapid contracting.
recent_activity: Hosted Hardware Enabled AI Acceleration assessment event in January 2026 and Rapid Capability Assessment 17 in April 2026. SOF Week 2026 ran May 18 to 21 with PEO-M and PEO-SOFSA collaboration events.

name: SOSV (IndieBio + HAX)
notes: SOSV's pre-seed deep tech accelerator, launched in 2015 as Silicon Valley's first biotech program with on-site bio-labs. In 2026 the IndieBio brand was folded into regional identities, SOSV NY and SOSV SF, with scope widened to chemistry, physics, AI, and biology. Graduates have raised over $3.6 billion across 310 companies.
thesis: Backs scientist-founders pre-seed with lab space, capital, and a 4-month residency in NYC or SF.
recent_activity: Rebranded IndieBio under SOSV NY and SOSV SF in early 2026 to reflect expansion beyond biotech into broader deep tech.
correction_note: SOSV retired the IndieBio brand in early 2026. SF and NYC programs now operate as SOSV SF / SOSV NY. HAX remains the hardware track.

name: Saint-Gobain NOVA External Venturing
notes: The external venturing arm of Saint-Gobain, the global building materials group. Invests in startups whose technology aligns with the group's sustainable construction strategy. Team of 17, including four principals across France and the US, with 36 active portfolio companies.
thesis: €1M-€10M strategic checks into materials, building products, and climate-aligned manufacturing startups.
recent_activity: Joined Solar Materials' €20M Series B alongside the EIC Fund in April 2026.

name: Samsung Catalyst Fund
notes: Strategic investment arm of Samsung based in Menlo Park with offices in San Jose, Tel Aviv, and Seoul. Funds deep tech AI infrastructure across cloud, networking, autonomous systems, sensors, and quantum.
thesis: Backs deep tech companies in AI infrastructure, semiconductors, and autonomous systems where Samsung can serve as a strategic partner.
recent_activity: Led a $50M round for Normal Computing in March 2026. Portfolio company Teramount was acquired by Molex in April 2026 and Tenstorrent unveiled its Galaxy AI Platform in May 2026.

name: Saudi Aramco Wa'ed Ventures
notes: Aramco's venture capital fund, deploying $270M+ across 94 startups. Originally built to grow Saudi Arabia's tech ecosystem, now invests globally across enterprise applications, high tech, consumer, retail, and vertical SaaS. Most active in Saudi Arabia (49 deals) and the UAE (7 deals).
thesis: $1M-$10M seed and Series B+ checks into local Saudi tech and international companies with relevance to energy transition, industrial, and consumer markets.
recent_activity: Made 13 investments in 2025 and 2 more through March 2026. Portfolio company Pasqal is deploying Saudi Arabia's first quantum computer with Aramco.

name: Scale For ClimateTech
award: Up to $50K non-dilutive per startup plus manufacturing support
notes: Scale For ClimateTech is a joint program from NextCorps and SecondMuse supported by NYSERDA. It combines the former M-Corps and Hardware Scaleup programs and provides up to $50K of non-dilutive support plus manufacturing assistance to climate hardware startups.
thesis: Provides non-dilutive funding and manufacturing scale-up support to climate hardware founders, with sister Venture For ClimateTech program backing earlier-stage teams.
recent_activity: Scale For ClimateTech Demo Day for Cohort 7 is scheduled for June 16, 2026 in New York City. Venture For ClimateTech selected 10 startups for Cohort 6 in May 2026.

name: Schaeffler Venturing
notes: The corporate venturing function at Schaeffler, the German industrial and mobility group. Set up in 2021 by Niels Fehre. Uses a venture client model with a 6-9 month proof-of-concept phase to evaluate strategic fit with Schaeffler business units. Looking beyond automotive into adjacent industries including humanoid robotics.
thesis: €1M-€10M strategic investments in mobility, industrial automation, robotics, and energy transition technology that can integrate into Schaeffler operations.
recent_activity: Invested in Agility Robotics in November 2025. Targeting €600M in synergies by 2029, with 40% by end of 2026.

name: Schematic Ventures
notes: Seed-stage venture firm in San Francisco focused on the technology that makes and moves the world. Invests across supply chain, manufacturing, logistics, mobility, and industrial AI from pre-seed through Series A.
thesis: Backs founders building software and hardware that modernize the industrial economy: supply chain, manufacturing, and logistics.
recent_activity: Closed 2025 investments in CVector, Guepard, Neurocad, and Vibe Robotics, leaning into AI-enabled industrial tooling and robotics programming interfaces.

name: Schmidt Sciences (successor to Schmidt Futures)
notes: Schmidt Futures was Eric and Wendy Schmidt's science and public-interest tech philanthropy. After a 2023-2024 restructuring, the work continues primarily through Schmidt Sciences, a 2024 spin-off that funds research across AI, astrophysics, biosciences, climate, and science systems.
thesis: Proactive philanthropic funding for unconventional science. Does not accept unsolicited proposals.
recent_activity: Schmidt Sciences awarded $45M to carbon-cycle research, $18M to AI2050 Fellows, $11M for AI in humanities, and $3M+ to study AI's labor-market impact. AI2050 has $125M committed over five years.
correction_note: Schmidt Futures effectively succeeded by Schmidt Sciences (founded 2024) for scientific philanthropy.

name: Schneider Electric Ventures
notes: SE Ventures is the corporate venture fund of Schneider Electric, with EUR 1 billion in committed capital across two funds. The team has backed 60-plus active companies, with about 70 percent in North America and 20 percent in Europe, and more than 60 percent of portfolio companies hold commercial partnerships with Schneider Electric.
thesis: Backs climate tech and industrial tech founders accelerating electrification and digitization, with focus on grid modernization, industrial automation, smart buildings, and data center optimization.
recent_activity: SE Ventures launched a 12-week climate and industrial tech accelerator in April 2025, offering $100K SAFEs to pre-seed startups with access to Schneider's network of 650,000 service providers.

name: Scout Ventures
notes: Early-stage frontier-tech fund based in New York, led by partners drawn from the military, intelligence community, and national labs. Writes $2M to $5M seed checks across AI, quantum, robotics, advanced materials, space, and security.
thesis: Backs founders from the military, intelligence, and research-lab communities building dual-use frontier technology.
recent_activity: Named to Time's Top Venture Capital Firms of 2025 list. Portfolio company Voyager Space landed a $900M Air Force contract and Tomahawk Robotics was acquired by AeroVironment.

name: Section 32
notes: Frontier technology and health venture firm founded by Bill Maris, the original founder of Google Ventures. Invests across computational biology, drug discovery, AI for science, and deep tech. Now operates under the s32.com domain.
thesis: Funds founders applying frontier technology to biology, health, and the physical sciences.
recent_activity: Migrated branding from section32.com to s32.com and continues to anchor Series A and B rounds in AI-driven drug discovery and computational health companies.

name: Sequoia Capital
notes: Sequoia Capital is a Menlo Park multi-stage generalist with a long hardtech and bio track record. The firm has backed SpaceX and is currently building a significant book in American Dynamism, defense, and frontier AI infrastructure.
thesis: Backs founders building category-defining companies from seed through growth, with a current emphasis on AI infrastructure and frontier compute alongside selective hardtech and bio bets.
recent_activity: In 2026 Sequoia partners published the high-profile 'This is AGI' thesis and continued backing frontier AI and dual-use infrastructure rounds.

name: Seraphim Space
notes: A London-based pure-play space technology investor operating as Generation Space. Manages roughly $550M across the Space Venture Fund, co-investment vehicles, the Seraphim Space Accelerator, and the publicly listed Seraphim Space Investment Trust. Has backed 145+ SpaceTech startups raising over $10B in aggregate.
thesis: £500K-£10M checks across seed, Series A, and Series B in earth observation, communications, climate intelligence, defense, and direct-to-device.
recent_activity: HawkEye 360 IPO'd in May 2026. SSIT completed a £137M C-share equity raise. Skylo-Space42 announced GEO direct-to-device service.

name: Shell Ventures
notes: Shell's corporate venture arm, established in 1998 as the first CVC in the oil and gas industry. Based in The Hague with offices globally. Backs early, scale, and growth stage companies whose technology can be deployed inside Shell's business, with a dedicated $1.4B fund for energy transition bets.
thesis: Strategic checks ($5M-$50M) into hydrogen, EV charging, storage, biofuels, and other technologies that lower emissions or accelerate energy transition.
recent_activity: Has made 204 investments to date and committed to a $1.4B energy transition CVC vehicle.

name: Shield Capital
notes: An early-stage venture fund investing at the intersection of commercial technology and national security. Focus is artificial intelligence, autonomy, cybersecurity, and space. Team blends veteran investors, founders, and former national security leaders.
thesis: Seed and Series A checks ($2M-$15M+) into dual-use companies relevant to defense, intelligence, and critical infrastructure.
recent_activity: Co-led Star Catcher's $65M Series A in May 2026 with B Capital and Cerberus, and led a Seed in Autonomous Cyber in May 2026.

name: Silent Ventures
notes: Silent Ventures is an early-stage defense and national security fund founded by Jackson Moses in 2022. The firm writes pre-seed and seed checks into aerospace, defense, and dual-use startups, with 40+ portfolio companies and offices in Dallas, Austin, and Denver.
thesis: Concept-stage pre-seed and seed checks into aerospace, defense, and national security founders, with an emphasis on hardware-heavy dual-use companies.
recent_activity: Named to Business Insider's 2026 Seed 100 list. Portfolio includes Saronic's $1.75B 2025 round and Castelion's 2025 hypersonic missile contracts.

name: Silent Ventures and Silent Capital Syndicate
award: Early-stage fund checks plus private syndicate co-investment access
notes: Silent Ventures is a Dallas-based aerospace, defense, and national security venture firm. Founder Jackson Moses runs the early-stage fund plus the affiliated Silent Capital private syndicate, which provides invite-only co-investment access under SEC Rule 506(b).
thesis: Concentrates pre-seed and seed checks on autonomous systems, hypersonics, edge computing, and advanced manufacturing for the defense industrial base.
recent_activity: Portfolio company Saronic raised $1.75B at a $9.25B valuation. Armada raised a $230M Series B. Silent Ventures was named to Business Insider's Seed 100 for 2026.

name: Simons Foundation
notes: The Simons Foundation is a private New York foundation funding basic science across mathematics, physical sciences, life sciences, neuroscience, and autism research. It operates the Flatiron Institute and SFARI alongside external grant programs.
thesis: Funds basic research and methods development across mathematics, physics, life sciences, and neuroscience, including the in-house Flatiron Institute and SFARI autism research initiative.
recent_activity: In May 2026 Flatiron Institute physicists used tensor networks on classical computers to challenge prior quantum supremacy claims. Simons Graduate Fellowships in Ecology and Evolution opened applications for 2027.

name: Sky VC (formerly JetBlue Ventures)
notes: Sky VC (formerly JetBlue Ventures) is an independent early-stage VC for travel and transportation founded in 2016. The firm was acquired by SKY Leasing in May 2025 and now operates with access to relationships across 100-plus airlines, MROs, and OEMs.
thesis: Invests in Enterprise Technologies modernizing legacy travel and transportation systems and Frontier Technologies including robotics, next-gen connectivity, and quantum.
recent_activity: Acquired by SKY Leasing in May 2025, welcomed Arielle Ring as President in June 2025, and rebranded to SKY VC in September 2025.
correction_note: Spun out from JetBlue in 2025-26 as an independent VC, Sky VC, focused on travel, aviation, and mobility.

name: Sloan Foundation
notes: The Alfred P. Sloan Foundation is a New York grantmaking institution founded in 1934. The foundation funds high-quality, impartial science research, technology, higher education, and public understanding of science.
thesis: Funds basic research, fellowships, and exploratory work in economics, energy and environment, history of science, matter-to-life biology, open source for science, and STEM education.
recent_activity: Sloan announced the 2026 class of Sloan Research Fellowships and launched the Exemplary Pathways to STEM Graduate Education program with grants up to $500,000 per institution.

name: Sofinnova Partners
notes: Sofinnova Partners is a Paris-based life sciences specialist active in biopharma, medtech, industrial biotech, and digital medicine. The firm operates with a 'Partners for Life' approach, building long-term relationships with founders across multiple funds.
thesis: Backs European and transatlantic life sciences founders from Series A through later rounds, with dedicated strategies for biopharma, medtech, industrial biotech, and digital medicine.
recent_activity: In May 2026 Hemab Therapeutics closed an upsized IPO and CellCentric raised a $220M Series D. HAYA Therapeutics dosed its first Phase 1 cardiac fibrosis cohort.

name: Sony Innovation Fund
notes: Sony Innovation Fund is the global venture arm of Sony, with 198 portfolio investments across consumer, entertainment, deep tech, climate, and enterprise SaaS. The team operates from Tokyo, the US, Europe, India, and Israel.
thesis: Backs early and growth stage companies in robotics, AI, sensors, gaming, climate, and entertainment, often where the technology connects to Sony's businesses.
recent_activity: Invested in CREW Carbon (water treatment) and YOAKE Entertainment in early 2026, with 12 new deals in the trailing twelve months.

name: Space Capital
notes: Space Capital is a New York seed-stage firm investing in the space economy. The team backs companies in GPS, geospatial intelligence, and satellite communications, and publishes the widely cited quarterly Space Investment Quarterly report.
thesis: Backs seed-stage founders building the space-based infrastructure layer that underpins large terrestrial industries, including positioning, earth observation, and connectivity.

name: SpaceWERX
award: STRATFI up to $15M govt match + $30M private = $60M total
deadline: Space PY26.1 STRATFI NOO closes May 29, 2026 (8 days)
notes: Innovation arm of the US Space Force. Runs SBIR and STTR programs plus STRATFI and TACFI matching pools that bridge Phase II to Phase III with government and private matching dollars up to $30M+. Also operates Spark, Vector, and The Refinery accelerator.
thesis: Funds small businesses on dual-use space technology aligned to US Space Force priorities.
recent_activity: PY26.2 TACFI coming soon. Space PY26.1 STRATFI accepting submissions through May 29. The Refinery currently accepting project submissions.

past_awardees.name: Albedo
past_awardees.description: Builds high-resolution imaging satellites flying in very low Earth orbit.

past_awardees.name: Gravitics
past_awardees.description: Builds modular space station components and in-orbit logistics platforms for commercial and military use.

past_awardees.name: LeoLabs
past_awardees.description: Operates a global ground radar network that tracks satellites and debris in low Earth orbit.

past_awardees.name: Umbra
past_awardees.description: Builds synthetic aperture radar satellites that image Earth through clouds and at night.

past_awardees.name: Xona Space Systems
past_awardees.description: Builds a low-Earth-orbit constellation that provides GPS-style positioning more accurately than today's signals.

past_awardees.name: CesiumAstro
past_awardees.description: Builds active phased-array communications hardware for satellites and aircraft.

past_awardees.name: Starfish Space
past_awardees.description: Builds small satellites that rendezvous with and service other spacecraft in orbit.

past_awardees.name: Rise8
past_awardees.description: Builds software delivery and security tools for the US Space Force and Air Force.

name: Spearhead
award: Selected founders receive a $2M fund to deploy; top performers scale into a second fund up to $10M
notes: Naval Ravikant's program that turns founders into angel investors. Founders apply to lead a $2M fund and write checks into other companies. Indirect path for hardtech founders, who often raise from Spearhead fund leads. Sector-agnostic.

name: Speedinvest
notes: Speedinvest is a pan-European seed firm with over EUR 1.2B in assets under management and 40-plus investment professionals across London, Berlin, Paris, Munich, and Vienna. The firm has dedicated sector teams for deep tech, climate, and industrial tech.
thesis: Backs European founders from pre-seed to growth through sector-specialist teams, with deep tech focused on AI, quantum, defense, and robotics academic spinouts.
recent_activity: Active investor with 351 portfolio companies and 28 new investments in the trailing twelve months as of March 2026.

name: Spring Lane Capital
notes: Boston- and Montreal-based firm providing project equity and corporate equity for distributed sustainable infrastructure. Covers four sectors: energy, waste-to-value, transportation, and food and agriculture. Portfolio includes 14+ platform investments with about 164,000 metric tons of CO2-equivalent impact in 2024.
thesis: Funds the missing middle of distributed climate infrastructure with project finance hybrid structures.
recent_activity: Running a DeveloperU workshop helping innovators transition into project developers. Active podcast on climate finance.

name: Squadra Ventures
notes: Squadra Ventures is a Baltimore and DC-based seed firm founded in 2019 by Guy Filippelli, a former Army intelligence officer who led RedOwl Analytics to a Forcepoint acquisition. It backs cyber and national security companies at pre-seed to Series A.
thesis: Leads pre-seed to Series A rounds with $2M-$4M checks in dual-use technologies for government, military, and mission-critical industries.
recent_activity: Raised $105M for Squadra II in 2024, surpassing its $80M target with a final goal of $110M-$120M.

name: Standard Industries Holdings
notes: Privately held global industrial holding company with ~$11B in annual revenue and 20,000 employees across 50 countries. Operates Standard Building Solutions, Standard Performance Materials, Standard Investments, and Winter Properties; venture activity flows through 40 North Ventures.
thesis: Backs building materials, industrial deep tech, and climate technology companies that intersect Standard's operating businesses.
recent_activity: Named to Forbes' America's Top Private Companies 2025 list. Continues to deploy capital through 40 North Ventures across climate and building materials.

name: Stanford Angels & Entrepreneurs
notes: Stanford Angels & Entrepreneurs is a remote-first network of 1,100+ Stanford alumni members established in 2010. The group has helped 100+ startups raise capital since spring 2020 with introductions and angel checks.
thesis: Connects Stanford alumni angels with Stanford-affiliated founders at pre-seed and seed stages through introductions and pooled angel checks.

name: Stanford StartX
notes: Founder-led nonprofit community for Stanford-affiliated entrepreneurs. Provides accelerator programming, mentorship, and access to Stanford faculty and alumni. Portfolio totals roughly $200B in valuation with 21 unicorns and 146 companies above $100M.
thesis: Backs Stanford-affiliated technical and life science spinouts with mentorship and a peer network, no equity taken.
recent_activity: Announced a partnership with Stanford Research Park to anchor The Link, a new innovation hub opening in early 2027.

name: Stanley Ventures
notes: Stanley Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Stanley Black and Decker, established in 2016 and based in Boston. The team holds about 44 portfolio companies in manufacturing, software, and physical security, investing $1 million to $5 million in seed and Series A rounds.
thesis: Backs early-stage founders in batteries, wireless charging, asset tracking, joining technologies, materials, automation, 3D printing, robotics, recycling, electrification, and industrial IoT.
recent_activity: Stanley Black and Decker continued its Techstars Founder Catalyst Program for diverse founders in the Mid-Atlantic while reviewing legacy venture positions during 2025 portfolio cleanup.

name: Starbridge Venture Capital
notes: DC-based early-stage VC founded in 2017 focused on space, defense, and frontier deep tech. Investment areas include energy storage, deep space exploration, advanced materials, connectivity, space manufacturing, and robotic swarms. Portfolio of 18 companies including one unicorn.
thesis: Backs early-stage commercial space and defense technology with hands-on operational support.
recent_activity: Three seed-stage investments at an average round size of $5.59M.

name: Starburst Aerospace Accelerator
notes: An aerospace and defense innovation catalyst founded in 2012. Combines startup accelerators, strategy consulting, and venture investments. 60-person team across LA, Paris, Tel Aviv, Munich, Madrid, Singapore, Seoul, and Washington DC, with 60 corporate and government partners including NATO and major aerospace primes.
thesis: Mentorship plus corporate and government matchmaking for pre-seed and seed aerospace, defense, and space startups.
recent_activity: Maintains 150 portfolio startups across the global network. Continues to run cohort programs from eight global locations.

name: Startup Chile
notes: Start-Up Chile is Latin America's first public accelerator, run by Corfo. The program has supported more than 2,000 startups generating over $2B in global sales and $5.8B in combined portfolio valuation.
thesis: Provides equity-free financing across three tracks (Build, Ignite, Growth) so global founders can develop startups out of Chile.
recent_activity: Open Partners Call inviting mentors, investors, and corporates to join the network; active recruitment of acceleration teams under the Corfo 2026 program.

name: Steel Atlas
notes: Steel Atlas is a New York industrials-focused venture fund backed by a global network of industrial conglomerates. The firm concentrates each fund on roughly ten companies tied to US reindustrialization.
thesis: Invests across Move (supply chain and logistics), Power (energy and generation), Build (manufacturing automation), plus critical minerals and battery technology.
recent_activity: Published November 2025 memo on Fund II portfolio strategy and radioisotope investments, with continued thought leadership on critical minerals, hydrogen, and nuclear.

name: Steel Perlot
notes: Steel Perlot is a deep tech venture builder and investment platform founded in 2020. The firm is chaired by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt and concentrates on AI, defense, and dual-use technology.
thesis: Backs AI and dual-use defense companies, often with a venture builder approach rather than pure passive investing.
recent_activity: Most recent disclosed investment was in Ethernal Labs in October 2025.

name: Stellantis Ventures
notes: Corporate venture arm of Stellantis, the global automaker formed from PSA and FCA. Invests in mobility, electrification, autonomous driving, connected vehicles, and advanced manufacturing technologies that can be deployed across Stellantis brands.
thesis: Backs technology that accelerates Stellantis's Dare Forward 2030 plan across electrification, software-defined vehicles, and autonomy.
recent_activity: Continues to invest from the EUR 300M fund launched in 2022, with 2025 activity concentrated in advanced battery chemistries and mobility AI.

name: Stellar Ventures
notes: Stellar Ventures is a woman-led space VC founded in 2022 and tied to aerospace engineering firm Stellar Solutions. The firm closed its $23M SV Andromeda Fund I and writes early checks into terrestrial and orbital space tech companies.
thesis: Backs the next generation of space entrepreneurs with technical due diligence and government customer access channeled through Stellar Solutions.
recent_activity: Participated in Vast's Series A in March 2026. Has built a 28-company portfolio focused on satellite, launch, in-space services, and national security.

name: Strategic Response Fund (SRF, ISED Canada)
notes: Canada's flagship industrial innovation funding vehicle, recently restructured as the Strategic Response Fund (SRF) under Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada. Targets tariff response, R&D in critical minerals, aerospace, cleantech, biomanufacturing, and life sciences, plus an AI Compute Challenge.
thesis: CAD $10M-$500M+ in non-dilutive funding for large-scale industrial innovation projects in Canada.
recent_activity: Prime Minister Carney launched a new auto industry strategy in February 2026. Steel-sector protection measures announced in July 2025. AI Compute Access Fund opened in June 2025.
correction_note: Restructured from the Strategic Innovation Fund in 2026. Continued as Canada's flagship industrial innovation funding vehicle.

name: Stray Dog Capital
notes: Kansas-based venture firm focused on alternative proteins and animal-free technology. Backs cultivated meat, fermentation, plant-based food, and cellular agriculture companies at seed and Series A.
thesis: Backs companies replacing animal products through plant-based, fermentation, and cellular agriculture technologies.
recent_activity: Continues to lead seed and Series A rounds for cultivated meat and fermentation companies, with focus on capital-efficient platforms in 2025-2026.

name: Susa Hardware (Susa Ventures)
notes: A San Francisco seed-stage fund focused on data-driven companies with defensible moats. Operates with a founder-first model: no board seats and a policy against voting founders out. Portfolio includes Robinhood, Flexport, and Skydio.
thesis: Seed checks (~$1-3M) into data-rich, moat-driven companies including hardware and frontier tech.
recent_activity: Made 18 investments in 2025 and 6 more through April 2026, with recent deals in automated sourcing (Cavela) and Adapt.com.

name: Sustainable Development Technology Canada (SDTC)
award: Seed up to CAD $100K; Startup CAD $50K-$1M; Scale-up CAD $1M-$10M+
deadline: Manual verification required : site returned 403
notes: Sustainable Development Technology Canada is a federally-funded cleantech non-dilutive funder based in Ottawa. As of 2025-2026, SDTC programming is transitioning to the National Research Council (NRC IRAP), with full integration into the new Canada Innovation Corporation slated by 2026-27.
thesis: Non-dilutive funding for Canadian climate-tech companies at pre-commercial demonstration stages.
recent_activity: Resumed funding eligible startups after 2024 governance review and is now winding down. New applications will run through NRC IRAP, and eventually the Canada Innovation Corporation.

name: Sustainable Future Tech Fund
notes: Sustainable Future Tech Fund is described as a Nordic climate fund targeting industrial decarbonization, batteries, and renewable energy startups. The supplied URL does not resolve and a public web presence under this exact name could not be verified.

name: Sustainable Future Ventures
notes: Sustainable Future Ventures is a EUR 100 million venture fund backed by PATRIZIA SE, with offices in London and Hamburg. The team backs technology companies that improve sustainability across the real estate and built-environment value chain.
