thesis: Invests at late seed to Series B in software and hardware for urban, energy, and industrial infrastructure, leveraging PATRIZIA's EUR 50 billion+ real asset platform to open commercial doors.
recent_activity: Sustainable Future Ventures continued deploying its first fund into built-environment technology through 2025 and 2026, with a team of six professionals across London and Hamburg.

name: Sustainable Ocean Alliance (SOA) Ocean Solutions
notes: Sustainable Ocean Alliance is a global ocean network operating across 115 countries. The organization runs three programs: the Ocean Leadership Network, the Ecopreneur Network for ocean startups, and the Ocean Leaders Fellowship.
thesis: Backs ocean-focused ventures across aquaculture, ocean carbon, sustainable shipping, and coastal resilience.
recent_activity: Reports 65+ ocean startups, 80+ youth hubs, and 300+ grantees in the network.

name: Suzano Ventures
notes: Suzano Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Brazilian pulp and paper firm Suzano, the world's largest eucalyptus pulp producer. The fund backs startups in bioeconomy, sustainable materials, and forest-positive technology.
thesis: Invests in startups using forest-derived inputs and bio-based processes to build sustainable materials, packaging, and carbon-positive products.

name: Synthesis Capital
notes: Synthesis Capital is a London food technology venture firm with more than $300 million in assets under management, billed as the largest dedicated alternative protein fund. The team backs cultivated meat, precision fermentation, and plant-based food founders alongside enabling biotech platforms.
thesis: Backs seed through growth-stage food and biology founders that can deliver scaled, lower-impact protein and ingredients across the global food system.
recent_activity: Synthesis continued deploying Fund I across cultivated and fermentation founders while avoiding what the team called the plant-based consumer packaged goods blood bath of 2025.

name: TCA Venture Group (Tech Coast Angels)
award: Pooled angel investments across hardware, medtech, cleantech, and software
notes: TCA Venture Group, renamed from Tech Coast Angels in 2024, is a Southern California angel network founded in 1997. The group has roughly 400 members across six regional networks and has deployed more than $300M into 540-plus companies, with portfolio follow-on of $1.7B.
thesis: Backs California-based founders with seed and Series A checks across cleantech, medtech, hardware, IoT, fintech, and AI, syndicating with more than 50 affiliated angel groups.
recent_activity: Hosted the SIGNAL Portfolio Showcase in May 2026 and ran Crossroads Podcast episodes on AI funding consolidation, M&A trends, and due diligence innovation.

name: TDK Ventures
notes: TDK Ventures is the corporate venture arm of TDK Corporation, headquartered in San Jose with offices in Boston, Bengaluru, and Tokyo. The firm manages $500M across three funds and 53 portfolio companies with one IPO and six M&A exits.
thesis: Backs early-stage Impact Scaling entrepreneurs in computing and connectivity, AgTech and HealthTech, mobility and space, industrial and robotics, energy and cleantech, and next-generation materials.
recent_activity: Fund 3 launched at $150M in 2025. Recent investments include The Guild (medium-lift launch) in January 2026, Rodatherm geothermal in September 2025, and Spiritus carbon removal in March 2025.

name: TPG Rise Climate
notes: Dedicated climate strategy within TPG's Rise impact platform, launched in 2021. Manages over $20B across Rise Climate I and II, targeting growth-stage decarbonization investments in clean energy, transport, industrial, and agriculture.
thesis: Backs growth-stage climate companies in clean energy, decarbonized transport, and industrial decarbonization at the inflection point of scale.

team_sample.name: Jim Coulter
team_sample.title: Founding Partner and Managing Partner, TPG Rise Climate

recent_activity: Closed Rise Climate II in 2025 above its $10B target and continued to back grid, storage, and industrial decarbonization deals through 2026.

name: Tau Ventures
notes: Tau Ventures is an early-stage AI fund founded in 2019 and based in Palo Alto. The firm invests pre-seed to growth across healthcare, enterprise, and frontier tech, bringing an operator mindset to its work with founders.
thesis: Backs applied AI companies across healthcare, enterprise, and frontier industries where automation can meaningfully change operations.
recent_activity: Highlighted 2026 portfolio additions including Understood Care for AI-driven patient advocacy and Statisfy building AI agents for customer success.

name: Techstars Industries (Space, Energy, Defense, Sustainability)
notes: Mentorship-driven three-month accelerator founded in 2006 that runs themed programs alongside its flagship. Themed verticals include Space (Los Angeles, with US Space Force and NASA JPL), Energy, Defense, and Sustainability. Standard terms are $120K in cash plus convertible note for 6%.
thesis: Backs pre-seed founders, with industry-specific cohorts that plug them into corporate, government, and lab partners.
recent_activity: Techstars Space Accelerator 2026 applications opened in March 2026, based in Los Angeles, with US Space Force and JPL as partners.

name: Temasek
notes: Temasek is the Singapore state-owned investor with a global portfolio across life sciences, sustainability, and frontier technology. Its Sustainable Living trend portfolio reached $34B as of March 2025 and the firm aims to halve net portfolio emissions by 2030.
thesis: Invests in growth and late-stage companies advancing decarbonization, food, water, waste, energy, materials, clean transport, and the built environment, often through dedicated vehicles such as Decarbonization Partners with BlackRock.
recent_activity: Hosts Ecosperity Week 2026 on sustainable AI, adaptation, and climate finance from 18 to 21 May 2026. Co-invests with Breakthrough Energy Ventures on 10+ shared portfolio companies.

name: Templeton World Charity Foundation
notes: Templeton World Charity Foundation is a Bahamas-headquartered philanthropic funder of interdisciplinary research on human flourishing. The Big Questions portfolio is its largest funding area, supporting science, theology, philosophy, and human society projects.
thesis: Funds discovery, development, and launch-stage research that explores foundational questions about human nature, flourishing, and well-being.
recent_activity: Issued a 2026 global request for planning grants on listening and learning in a polarized world, awarding up to $100K per grant.

name: Third Derivative (RMI)
award: Curated investor access + RMI technical mentorship
notes: A climate tech accelerator founded in 2020 by RMI and New Energy Nexus. Connects climate startups with investors, corporate partners, and industry experts to commercialize hard-to-abate technologies. Coverage spans batteries, biofuels, cooling, and industrial decarbonization.
thesis: Curated investor and corporate matchmaking for climate startups in industrial, built environment, and harder-to-abate sectors.
recent_activity: Accepting applications for the Future Industries Partnership targeting industrial and built environment startups across Asia and the Middle East.

name: Third Rock Ventures
notes: Boston- and San Francisco-based life sciences firm operating a company-creation model. Nineteen years of operation, 73 portfolio companies, and 26 marketed products to date. Founded-by-VC pattern with hands-on early team building.
thesis: Builds and backs biotech and healthcare companies addressing meaningful clinical needs from inception.
recent_activity: Otarmeni (gene therapy for hearing loss, from Decibel) was approved by FDA. Aqvesme treatment for thalassemia-related anemia advanced via Agios.

name: Third Sphere
award: Early-stage fund supporting urban climate and infrastructure companies; 500+ portfolio jobs open
notes: Climate and urban-systems investor across Affordable and Clean Energy, Sustainable Cities, Climate Action, and Connected Transportation. Founder outreach via website contact channels; no formal public form.

name: Tiger Global Hard Tech
notes: Tiger Global is a New York global growth investor with a research-driven, long-term approach. The firm has expanded into defense, space, and frontier compute, participating in late-stage rounds for category leaders like Waymo and Zipline.
thesis: Backs high-quality, research-validated companies at growth stages globally, with growing exposure to hardtech, defense, space, and AI infrastructure leaders.
recent_activity: Raising Private Investment Partners 17 (PIP 17), a $2.2B fund disclosed in December 2025. Recent participations include Waymo Series D (Feb 2026) and Zipline (Mar 2026).

name: TotalEnergies Ventures
notes: TotalEnergies Ventures is the corporate venture arm of French energy major TotalEnergies. The $400M Carbon Neutrality Ventures fund has pivoted to an accelerator model focused on smaller, earlier-stage investments.
thesis: Backs alternative and renewable energy, energy efficiency, waste management, and greenhouse-gas reduction startups. Continues to manage existing portfolio relationships.
recent_activity: TotalEnergies is winding down the CVC unit in favor of an accelerator. Existing portfolio relationships remain managed by the venture team.
correction_note: Pivoted from corporate venture capital toward an accelerator model in 2025-26. Energy transition focus continues.

name: Tough Tech Today
award: Editorial discovery channel; no direct funding
notes: Monthly podcast and content network hosted by Forrest Meyen and Jonathan Miller out of Cambridge. Profiles hardtech and tough tech founders across robotics, defense, energy, and biotech. Acts as a discovery channel for investor and operator introductions.
thesis: Surfaces hardtech founders and investors through editorial discovery rather than direct funding.
recent_activity: Most recent episode "Solving the Unknown" aired December 9, 2025 featuring Forrest and JMill discussing tough tech founder pipelines.

name: Toyota Ventures
notes: Toyota Ventures is the early-stage corporate venture arm of Toyota Motor Corporation, based in Los Altos, CA. It runs two funds: Frontier for deeptech and Climate for carbon-reducing technology.
thesis: Backs disruptive deeptech and climate startups, prioritizing financial returns first and strategic value second. Frontier covers robotics, autonomy, materials, quantum, and bio.

team_sample.name: Gill Pratt
team_sample.title: Board Member and TMC Chief Scientist

recent_activity: Announced investment in Decart for real-time world models in May 2026.

name: Trimble Ventures
notes: Trimble Ventures is the corporate venture arm of Trimble, focused on construction, agriculture, geospatial, and transportation technologies. The fund invests across software, AI, robotics, augmented reality, and connected devices.
thesis: Backs software and connected hardware that extends Trimble's reach in construction tech, logistics tech, and crop tech.
recent_activity: Most recent disclosed check was a Series A III in Xona Space Systems in March 2025. Trimble acquired portfolio company Document Crunch in 2026 to add AI-powered risk tools.

name: Trousdale Ventures
award: Multi-stage fund focused on forever companies; check sizes range across seed through growth
notes: Backs founders in Space and Mobility, Climate Tech, Deep Tech, and Consumer. Direct outreach via website contact; no open application form. Frequently co-invests with major hardtech rounds in space and autonomy.

name: Trucks Venture Capital
notes: Seed-stage fund focused on the future of transportation. Backs autonomous vehicles, fleet logistics, urban mobility, freight, and electric trucks.

name: Trust Ventures
notes: Trust Ventures is an Austin venture firm founded in 2018 that backs startups working in heavily regulated industries. The team provides capital plus pro bono regulatory and policy advisory, helping founders clear political and licensing barriers in energy, healthcare, transportation, and finance.
thesis: Invests in seed and Series A companies where existing technology is held back by policy stasis, then pairs capital with regulatory strategy to clear paths to commercial scale.
recent_activity: Trust made four new investments in the year through July 2025, including a November 2025 seed round into Voya Energy. Portfolio company Base Power crossed unicorn status in 2025.

name: Type One Ventures
notes: Type One Ventures is a Los Angeles frontier tech firm founded in 2019. The firm invests in companies advancing humanity toward a Type I civilization, with a single thesis spanning space, robotics, AI, energy, and human longevity.
thesis: Backs technologies enabling a scalable space economy and the human performance and longevity tools needed to sustain long-duration habitation off Earth.
recent_activity: Raised target for second fund focused on space and deep tech. Tarek Waked sits on the boards of Gravitics and Space Forge.

name: UK Catapult Network
notes: Innovate UK's network of nine sector technology and innovation centres across 65+ national locations. Provides R&D facilities and technical expertise to help UK companies de-risk new products. Coverage spans cell and gene therapy, compound semiconductors, connected places, digital, energy systems, high-value manufacturing, medicines discovery, offshore renewables, and satellite applications.
thesis: Facility access and project funding to UK companies developing physical and life-science innovation.
recent_activity: Published the Spring 2025 Prospectus across all nine centres. High Value Manufacturing Catapult shaped national advanced manufacturing strategy.

name: USDA SBIR
notes: USDA SBIR is the Small Business Innovation Research program run by the National Institute of Food and Agriculture. The program funds startups in agriculture, food, forestry, rural energy, and biotech with Phase I awards up to $175K and Phase II up to $650K.
thesis: Funds non-dilutive R&D in agriculture, food, forestry, rural energy, and biotech, with sequential Phase I feasibility and Phase II prototype awards.

name: Unilever Ventures
notes: Unilever Ventures is the venture and growth capital arm of Unilever, with offices in London and Mumbai. The team backs modern consumer brands and the technology platforms behind them.
thesis: Backs founders building beauty, wellness, and consumer commerce companies that can scale through Unilever's distribution and brand expertise.

name: Union Square Ventures Climate Fund
notes: Dedicated climate practice within Union Square Ventures, the New York firm founded by Fred Wilson and Brad Burnham. Funds software-first decarbonization, grid, and consumer climate companies aligned with USV's broader access-to-capital and access-to-knowledge thesis.
thesis: Backs companies that broaden access to climate action through software, networks, and APIs across grid, carbon, and consumer climate categories.
recent_activity: Grace Carney published "The DC Revolution Will Not Be Centralized" in March 2025, framing USV's continued interest in distributed energy and decentralized climate infrastructure plays.

name: United Airlines Ventures
notes: United Airlines Ventures is the strategic venture arm of United Airlines, based in Chicago. The fund invests in sustainable aviation fuel, electric aircraft, and aviation decarbonization technologies aligned with the airline's net-zero goals.
thesis: Backs growth-stage companies developing sustainable aviation fuel, electric and hydrogen aircraft, and supporting decarbonization technologies for commercial aviation.

name: Up.Partners
notes: Multimodal moving-world fund (formerly UP.Labs). Backs ground, aerial, and space mobility, with platforms in autonomy, robotics, and AI.

name: Urban Future Lab (NYU Tandon ACRE)
award: Multi-year incubation, prize competition awards, non-dilutive support
notes: Urban Future Lab is the longest-running climatetech incubator in New York City, housed at NYU Tandon. ACRE has supported 200+ startups with a 92% survival rate that have collectively raised over $4.2B. The hub also runs the Offshore Wind Innovation Hub.
thesis: Provides multi-year incubation, non-dilutive prize competition awards, and market access support to early-stage urban climate technology companies.

name: Velocity
notes: Velocity is the University of Waterloo startup incubator, operating since 2008. The program has supported over 500 startups with a combined enterprise value of $40B across hardware, software, and life sciences.
thesis: Backs Waterloo-connected founders with coaching, community, non-dilutive grants, and access to coworking and prototyping.
recent_activity: Announced the newest cohort in May 2026 and is staging the Velocity Sports-Tech Showcase in Toronto. Published the Momentum 2025 founder insights report.

name: Velocity (University of Waterloo)
award: Free incubation, no equity, no fees
notes: University of Waterloo incubator considered Canada's largest tech startup program. Provides free incubation with no equity or fees, hardware shop access including laser cutters and electronic test gear, and is open to non-Waterloo founders.
thesis: Removes friction from Canadian early-stage hardware and biotech founders with free incubation and shop access.
recent_activity: Welcomed a new cohort in May 2026, hosted the Velocity Sports-Tech Showcase during Toronto Tech Week, and released the Momentum 2025 founder insights report.

name: Veriten
notes: Houston-based energy research, advisory, and investment platform founded by Maynard Holt. Operates weekly programs C.O.B. Tuesday and Super-Spiked, advises corporates and investors, and deploys capital across oil, gas, power, and decarbonization.
thesis: Backs durable energy and power companies with data-driven, economics-grounded long-term theses.
recent_activity: May 2026 C.O.B. Tuesday featured Alaska Governor Mike Dunleavy and Secretary Doug Burgum on resource development. Super-Spiked examined refiner strategies under geopolitical volatility.

name: Versant Ventures
notes: Versant Ventures is a San Francisco biotech-only specialist founded in 1999, managing $5.5B across six offices. Distinctive Discovery Engines run in-house drug discovery teams that launch companies with academic founders.
thesis: Builds biotech companies with academic founders by combining Discovery Engines, 30+ academic partnerships, and a multi-stage capital base.
recent_activity: Reports 50+ IPOs and 50+ M&A exits since founding, with a team carrying 100+ years of VC experience.

name: Vertex Ventures
notes: Vertex Holdings is the Singapore venture capital platform of Temasek, managing $6.8 billion across seven regional fund partnerships and more than 300 portfolio companies. Vertex Ventures operates regional funds in China, Israel, Southeast Asia and India, the US, and Japan, plus Vertex Ventures HC for biotech and medtech.
thesis: Backs early and growth-stage technology and healthcare founders globally through an integrated platform of seven regional fund teams sharing diligence and follow-on capital.

name: Vinnova
notes: Sweden's government innovation agency, founded in 2001. Funds collaborative research and innovation projects with grants ranging from small feasibility studies to multi-million-euro multi-year programs across climate, life sciences, mobility, and digitalization.
thesis: Funds Swedish R&D and innovation projects that strengthen national competitiveness across strategic sectors.
recent_activity: Issued 2025-2026 calls under the Climate Neutral Industry mission and Sustainable Mobility programs aligned with Swedish industrial decarbonization.

name: Volkswagen Group Innovation Fund
notes: Volkswagen Group's venture activity now flows mostly through Leitmotif, an independent firm with a $300M fund anchored by Volkswagen. Leitmotif has offices in Palo Alto and Munich and bridges US and European decarbonization technology.
thesis: Invests in later-stage decarbonization with a soft spot for hard tech, splitting roughly 70/30 between proven billion-dollar markets and revolutionary 2030s bets.
recent_activity: Most recent investment was in RobCo in January 2026. Co-led Harbinger Motors' $100M Series B earlier in the year.

name: Volvo Cars Tech Fund
notes: Volvo Cars Tech Fund is the corporate venture arm of Volvo Cars, founded in 2018 in Gothenburg, Sweden. The fund has made 32 investments to date in transportation and mobility, supporting Volvo's plan to become a fully electric, circular business by 2040.
thesis: Backs strategic technology trends reshaping the auto industry, including AI, electrification, autonomous driving, and digital mobility services.
recent_activity: The fund's most recent disclosed investment was a June 2025 seed round into RIIICO, alongside continued backing of NEURA Robotics and production-line inspection company Viospatia.

name: Voyager Capital
notes: Voyager Capital is a Seattle venture firm investing in Pacific Northwest and Western Canadian founders. The firm backs early-stage companies in AI-driven enterprise software, software-driven hardware, supply chain, and sustainable agriculture.
thesis: Focuses on capital-efficient AI and industrial software companies in the Pacific Northwest, plus software-driven hardware in agriculture and supply chain.
recent_activity: May 2026 saw portfolio company Carbon Robotics featured in CNBC Disruptor 50; Hiya went live with Branded Calling on Vodafone covering nearly 100% of UK mobile subscribers.

name: Voyager Ventures
notes: Voyager Ventures is an early-stage climate fund founded in 2021 by Sierra Peterson and Sarah Sclarsic. It backs companies in energy, industrial, climate tech, advanced manufacturing, mobility, materials, and carbon management.
thesis: Bets that electrification, automation, AI, and advanced manufacturing converge to form new economic foundations.
recent_activity: Closed a $275M Fund II in January 2026. Portfolio company Tem raised $75M and Anthro Energy committed $42M to a Louisville manufacturing facility.

name: Wellcome Leap
notes: Wellcome's DARPA-style bold-bets unit, structured as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Runs 8-year multi-team programs in health science across 30 countries and 170+ organizations. Executed 15 programs in its first six years.
thesis: Funds breakthrough health science programs over 5-10 year horizons with a global research network.
recent_activity: Launched Focused Antibiotics ($50M) program. Active programs include VISIBLE, FORM, CARE, Untangling Addiction, Dynamic Resilience, and Q4Bio (now with a new $2M prize).

name: Wellcome Trust
notes: UK-based global health charity with an endowment of over GBP 36B, founded in 1936 from the estate of Henry Wellcome. Funds discovery research and three priority areas: infectious disease, mental health, and climate and health.
thesis: Funds discovery research and translation across infectious disease, mental health, and climate and health.
recent_activity: Announced expanded climate and health funding programs and continued multi-year commitments to mental health discovery science through 2025-2026.

name: West Gate (NREL / National Lab Reserve)
hq: Golden, CO (operated at NREL and partner labs)
notes: West Gate is a lab-embedded entrepreneurship program run by the National Laboratory of the Rockies (formerly NREL), based in Golden, Colorado. It places founders inside the lab for a two-year fellowship with paired scientists and access to research facilities focused on affordable, secure energy.
thesis: Backs founders at TRL 3 or higher with energy technologies that need bench validation alongside national-lab scientists.
recent_activity: Four cohorts and 17 innovators to date have raised $55M in follow-on funding. The 2026 cohort is closed; 2027 application details are due in spring 2026.
correction_note: Program operates at NREL and partner labs, not solely PNNL as previously listed. DOE-funded entrepreneurship program for energy, grid, materials, and environmental startups.

name: World Fund
notes: Berlin-based European climate VC investing in technologies with potential to abate at least 100 megatons of CO2 per year. Evaluates each deal with a climate performance potential life-cycle analysis and IRIS+ impact metrics.
thesis: Backs European climate tech founders building companies that can each abate 100+ megatons of CO2 annually.
recent_activity: Backed Lithosquare's $25M round and Barocal's $10M raise in 2025-2026. Published the Europe's Power Play report on European grid and infrastructure investment.

name: Y Combinator
notes: Y Combinator is a Mountain View accelerator founded in 2005 that invests $500K in selected startups across four batches per year. Founders relocate to San Francisco for a three-month program ending in Demo Day, with ongoing alumni support after.
thesis: Backs early founders across software, hardware, biotech, and defense with a standard deal of $125K post plus $375K MFN.
recent_activity: Recent alumni announcements include Emergent at $70M Series B, Govdash at $30M Series B, Fleetzero at $43M Series A for battery-powered cargo ships, and Deepgram at $130M Series C. Meta's $14B Scale AI deal closed in 2025.

name: Yale Planetary Solutions Impact Accelerator
notes: Yale Ventures accelerator launched in Fall 2025 specifically for hardtech ventures with physical components. Focus areas include carbon management, low-carbon industry, biosystems, food and agriculture, and water.
thesis: Translates Yale research into hardtech companies with measurable planetary impact, requiring solutions to be physical-systems based.
recent_activity: Inaugural cohort entered the program in Fall 2025 with founders transitioning to ClimateHaven in New Haven for follow-on lab space.

name: Yamaha Motor Ventures
notes: Strategic venture arm of Yamaha Motor Co with offices in Silicon Valley and Tokyo. Invests in mobility, robotics, agriculture technology, marine technology, and industrial automation aligned with Yamaha's hardware-heavy product portfolio.
thesis: Backs hardware-led startups in mobility, robotics, and agriculture where Yamaha can act as a strategic partner.
recent_activity: Continues to back agricultural robotics and unmanned marine platforms through 2025-2026, with portfolio companies expanding into commercial deployments.

name: a16z American Dynamism
notes: Andreessen Horowitz's investment practice for companies serving the US national interest. Covers aerospace, defense, public safety, education, housing, supply chain, industrials, manufacturing, and energy. Treats government as a customer, competitor, or stakeholder.
thesis: Backs founders building dual-use and national-interest hardtech across the full stage range, from seed to growth.
recent_activity: Published the 'American Dynamism 50: Fight of the Future' AI list in 2025 and added General (ret.) Bryan Fenton as Special Advisor.

name: a16z Bio + Health
notes: Andreessen Horowitz's life sciences and healthcare practice. Backs founders building drugs, diagnostics, devices, care platforms, healthcare infrastructure, and AI applications across biology and medicine. Team blends former founders, operators, MDs, PhDs, scientists, and technologists.
thesis: Seed through growth checks ($2M-$50M+) into software-aware biotech, digital health, and healthcare infrastructure.
recent_activity: Launched a biotech ecosystem venture fund in partnership with Eli Lilly. Runs Bio Builders and Health Builders programs and the Raising Health podcast.

name: mHUB
award: 6-month accelerator plus 18 months of follow-on lab access and mentorship
notes: MHUB is a Chicago hardtech innovation center with 11 prototyping labs and $6 million in shared equipment. Portfolio companies have raised $2.33 billion in capital, launched 1,852 products, received 627 patents, and generated $2.25 billion in revenue.
thesis: Runs vertical accelerators in MedTech, energy tech, and sustainable manufacturing, pairing six months of programming with 18 months of follow-on lab access plus mentorship.
recent_activity: mHUB announced the Compute-Energy Nexus Accelerator and received a $1 million grant from The Rockefeller Foundation in March 2026 for data center sustainability work.

name: AFWERX SBIR Phase I (Air Force)
deadline: Within DoD BAA: Air Force topics posted at dodsbirsttr.mil; rolling BAA
notes: AFWERX manages Air Force + Space Force SBIR. Open topics across all tech areas. Submit via DSIP. Source: afwerx.com

name: AFWERX SBIR STRATFI (Strategic Funding Increase)
deadline: Available to current Phase II AFWERX awardees: apply through contracting officer
notes: Government matches private co-investment. Bridge from prototype to production. Max $100M. Source: afwerx.com

name: AFWERX SBIR TACFI (Tactical Funding Increase)
deadline: Available to current AFWERX Phase II awardees
notes: Smaller bridge than STRATFI. Rapid acquisition pathway. Source: afwerx.com

name: ARPA-E OPEN Programme
deadline: Periodic: not annual; check arpa-e.energy.gov/funding-opportunities for active solicitations
notes: Not strictly SBIR: ARPA-E has own FOA process. No pre-specified technology area. Highest risk/reward energy grants. Source: arpa-e.energy.gov

name: CDC SBIR Phase I
deadline: Multiple windows: Jun 23, Jun 26, Aug 19, Sep 6, Dec 10, 2026: check cdc.gov/sbir
notes: Multiple deadlines/year. Topics: chronic disease, infectious disease, workplace safety, environmental health. Source: cdc.gov

name: CDC STTR Phase I
deadline: Same windows as SBIR: Sep 6, Dec 10, 2026; Jan 6, Apr 6, 2027
notes: Requires formal research institution partner. Source: cdc.gov + team-80.com/blog/sbir-deadlines

name: DARPA SBIR Phase I
deadline: Within DoD BAA cycle: DARPA topics posted at dodsbirsttr.mil; email SBIR_BAA@darpa.mil for tech questions
notes: DARPA has strictest criteria. No topic Q&A via DSIP: email DARPA directly. Direct-to-Phase-II available for some topics. Source: dodsbirsttr.mil + darpa.mil

name: DHS SBIR Phase I
deadline: Annual: DHS runs Sep-Oct pre-solicitation per sbir.gov planner; check dhs.gov/science-and-technology/sbir
notes: 5 priority areas: border security, chemical/biological defence, cybersecurity, first responders, maritime. Source: dhs.gov

name: DHS SBIR Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees notified
notes: Up to $1M over 24 months. Transition to DHS programmes expected. Source: dhs.gov

name: DOE SBIR Phase I Release 1 (FY2026)
deadline: FY2026 release dates TBD: DOE undergoing internal restructuring; check science.osti.gov/sbir
notes: Historically May-Aug window. DOE has 11 programme offices participating. Source: science.osti.gov/sbir

name: DOE SBIR Phase I Release 2 (FY2026)
deadline: FY2026 release 2 dates TBD: typically Sep-Jan window; check science.osti.gov
notes: Second annual release. Different programme offices than Release 1. Source: science.osti.gov/sbir

name: DOE SBIR Phase II (FY2026)
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees notified by programme office
notes: Up to $1.7M over 24 months. Full commercialisation plan required. Source: science.osti.gov/sbir

name: DOT SBIR Phase I
deadline: Annual: Feb-Apr pre-solicitation window; check transportation.gov for FY2026 opening
notes: Topics: highway safety, rail, aviation, transit, pipelines, autonomous systems. Source: transportation.gov

name: DOT SBIR Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees only
notes: Strong transition-to-adoption path for transport safety tech. Source: transportation.gov

name: Dept of Education SBIR Phase I (IES)
deadline: Annual: Nov-Jan application window; check ies.ed.gov/sbir for FY2026 solicitation
notes: Institute of Education Sciences manages. Focuses on education technology with rigorous evidence base. Source: ies.ed.gov

name: Dept of Education SBIR Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees
notes: Strong commercialisation evidence required. Randomised trial data often needed. Source: ies.ed.gov

name: DoD SBIR 2026 Phase I BAA (Air Force / Space Force)
deadline: BAA 2026 P1 active: check dodsbirsttr.mil for DAF open topics
notes: Air Force + Space Force topics. PIEE account required for NIST 800-171 compliance. Source: dodsbirsttr.mil

name: DoD SBIR 2026 Phase I BAA (Army)
deadline: BAA 2026 P1 active: check dodsbirsttr.mil/topics-app for Army open topics
notes: Submit via DSIP portal. Must complete foreign disclosure form (Vol 7). Source: dodsbirsttr.mil

name: DoD SBIR 2026 Phase I BAA (Navy)
deadline: BAA 2026 P1 active: check dodsbirsttr.mil for Navy open topics
notes: Submit via DSIP portal. Navy topics: undersea warfare, surface warfare, aviation. Source: dodsbirsttr.mil

name: DoD SBIR Phase II
award: Up to $1,250,000+ (some up to $2,500,000 for STRATFI/TACFI)
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees notified by their DoD component
notes: Phase I awardees auto-eligible. STRATFI/TACFI programmes allow larger Phase II awards for transition-ready tech. Source: dodsbirsttr.mil

name: DoD SBIR Strategic Breakthrough Award (new S.3971)
deadline: Available FY2027+ for agencies with >$100M SBIR budget: check dodsbirsttr.mil
notes: NEW from S.3971 Apr 2026. Must have prior Phase II award + 100% private capital match. Bridges 'valley of death'. Source: S.3971 + Crowell & Moring analysis

name: DoD STTR 2026 Phase I BAA
award: Up to $1,500,000 (Phase I + Phase II combined for STTR, 21 months Phase I)
deadline: BAA 2026 P1 active: check dodsbirsttr.mil for STTR open topics
notes: STTR requires formal research institution partner. Separate from SBIR. Source: dodsbirsttr.mil

name: EPA SBIR Phase I
deadline: Annual: typically Apr-Jun window; check epa.gov/sbir for FY2026 solicitation
notes: EPA awards ~25 Phase I contracts/year. Topics: drinking water, air quality, green chemistry, homeland security. Source: epa.gov/sbir

name: EPA SBIR Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees eligible
notes: Limited Phase II slots (~10/yr). Strong commercial application required. Source: epa.gov/sbir

name: NASA SBIR 2026 Phase I (Appendix A)
deadline: OPEN NOW: May 21, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET
notes: OPEN. BAA released Apr 17, 2026. Submit via ProSAMS portal. Tech questions due May 5, 2026 at 5PM ET. Source: nasa.gov

name: NASA SBIR 2026 Phase I (Future Appendices)
deadline: Additional appendices planned throughout 2026: subscribe at nasa.gov/sbir_sttr for updates
notes: NEW: NASA switched to rolling BAA with multiple appendix releases per year. Source: nasa.gov

name: NASA SBIR Ignite Phase I
deadline: Summer 2026 appendix planned: check nasa.gov/sbir_sttr
notes: Ignite has broader technology scope than mainline. Part of new BAA framework. Source: nasa.gov

name: NASA SBIR Phase II
award: Up to $750,000 (24 months); up to $1,500,000 for extended Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees notified by NASA programme office
notes: ~30% Phase I awardees advance to Phase II. Phase IIE available for extended commercialisation. Source: nasa.gov

name: NASA STTR 2026 Phase I (Appendix B)
deadline: OPEN NOW: May 21, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET
notes: OPEN. Same deadline as SBIR Appendix A. Requires research institution partner. ProSAMS portal. Source: nasa.gov

name: NIH Direct-to-Phase II (R44 DP2)
deadline: Same standard due dates: Sep 5, Jan 5, Apr 5
notes: Skip Phase I. Must demonstrate preliminary data equivalent to Phase I. Not all ICs participate. Source: seed.nih.gov

name: NIH SBIR Phase I (R43)
award: Up to $300,000 (most ICs); up to $400,000 for some ICs (24 months)
deadline: New omnibus solicitation pending post-reauth: standard due dates: Sep 5, Jan 5, Apr 5
notes: 27 NIH Institutes + CDC + FDA participate. Must align with institute mission. Source: grants.nih.gov

name: NIH SBIR Phase II (R44)
deadline: Same standard due dates as Phase I: Sep 5, Jan 5, Apr 5
notes: Requires prior Phase I award from any federal agency. Up to $2M over 24 months. Source: grants.nih.gov

name: NIH STTR Phase I (R41)
deadline: Standard due dates: Sep 5, Jan 5, Apr 5
notes: Requires formal research institution partnership (30% of work at partner institution). Source: grants.nih.gov

name: NIH STTR Phase II (R42)
deadline: Standard due dates: Sep 5, Jan 5, Apr 5
notes: Requires prior R41 Phase I. Research institution partner required. Source: grants.nih.gov

name: NIH Technical + Business Assistance (TABA)
award: Phase I: up to $6,500 | Phase II: up to $50,000
deadline: Requested within Phase I or Phase II application: or as admin supplement post-award
notes: NEW as of Apr 13, 2026 (S.3971). Covers consultants, staff training, commercialization support, IP protection. Source: NOT-OD-26-075

name: NIST SBIR Phase I
deadline: Annual: check nist.gov/tpo for FY2026 solicitation dates
notes: NIST focuses on measurement science, advanced manufacturing, quantum info science, cybersecurity standards. Source: nist.gov/tpo

name: NIST SBIR Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees

name: NOAA SBIR Phase I
deadline: Annual: Nov-Feb window; check techpartnerships.noaa.gov/sbir for FY2026 solicitation
notes: Topics: weather forecasting, ocean exploration, climate monitoring, fisheries tech, satellite data. Source: techpartnerships.noaa.gov

name: NOAA SBIR Phase II
deadline: Invitation-only: Phase I awardees

name: NSF SBIR Phase I
deadline: Targeting Jul 2026 close for next cycle: check seedfund.nsf.gov/solicitations/
notes: Resuming post-reauth. Start with Project Pitch (required before full proposal). Non-dilutive. US for-profit small biz only. 6-month Phase I. Source: seedfund.nsf.gov

name: NSF SBIR Phase II
deadline: Available to Phase I awardees: rolling post Phase I award
notes: Phase I must be complete. Up to $1M over 24 months. Same merit-based review. Source: seedfund.nsf.gov

name: NSF SBIR/STTR Fast-Track
deadline: Same cycle as Phase I: targeting Jul 2026
notes: Submit Phase I + Phase II simultaneously. Reduces time to Phase II. NSF pilot programme. Source: seedfund.nsf.gov

name: SAM.gov Registration (Required for ALL SBIR)
deadline: Complete BEFORE applying: registration takes up to 10 business days
notes: Unique Entity ID (UEI) from SAM.gov required for ALL SBIR/STTR awards. Register early. Source: sbir.gov/apply

name: Strategic Breakthrough Award (NEW: S.3971)
deadline: Available FY2027 for agencies with >$100M SBIR budgets: check individual agency sites
notes: NEW from S.3971. Requires prior Phase II award + 100% private capital match. Up to 0.5% of agency extramural budget. 90-day award timeline required. Source: S.3971 + Crowell analysis

name: TABA Technical + Business Assistance (UPDATED: S.3971)
award: Phase I: up to $6,500 | Phase II: up to $50,000
deadline: Requested within application or as admin supplement post-award: effective Apr 13, 2026
notes: Updated amounts in S.3971. Use for consultants, IP protection, commercialisation training, foreign risk screening. Source: NOT-OD-26-075 + S.3971

name: USDA SBIR Phase I (NIFA)
deadline: Annual: typically Jun-Aug application window; check nifa.usda.gov for FY2026 solicitation
notes: USDA NIFA manages. 8 priority research areas incl. food security, rural prosperity, bioenergy. Source: nifa.usda.gov
